- ✓ TODO-1065: Delete remaining lowering-side AST semantic fallbacks for migrated families. Completed: lowering now treats the pilot routing semantic product as authoritative for the remaining direct-call and method-call seams instead of reconstructing equivalent meaning from AST/import state once a semantic product is present. `makeResolveCallPathFromScope(...)` no longer remaps unresolved rooted semantic direct-call targets back through imports, method-call lowering now fails immediately when a published semantic-product method target does not resolve to a lowered definition, and focused helper/source-lock coverage now pins both the authoritative unresolved direct-call path and the fail-closed method-call contract instead of the old compatibility fallbacks.
- ✓ TODO-1064: Precompute lowering lookup tables during semantic-product publication. Completed: semantic-product publication now fills owned `publishedRoutingLookups` tables for the pilot routing slice, covering direct-call, method-call, bridge-path, and callable-summary lookup state as the semantic product is built instead of rebuilding those tables inside `IrLowererSemanticProductTargetAdapters.cpp`. Lowering now reads the published routing lookups directly, focused adapter/source-lock coverage pins the new contract and the deleted rebuild helpers, and the remaining fallback scans are limited to hand-built semantic-program fixtures that do not populate the published tables yet.
- ✓ TODO-1063: Replace pilot routing snapshot export with append-only fact collectors. Completed: the pilot routing semantic-product families now flow through validator-owned append-only collectors built once by `collectPilotRoutingSemanticProductFacts()` and consumed through `takeCollected*ForSemanticProduct()` exports instead of `*SnapshotForSemanticProduct()` reconstruction. `SemanticsValidate.cpp` now consumes collected direct-call, method-call, bridge-path, and callable-summary entries directly, the old bridge derivation helper was deleted, and focused source-lock plus worker-count semantic-product coverage now pin the new collector boundary and callable-summary parity.
- ✓ TODO-1066: Pilot worker-local validation context for one deterministic slice. **Group 14 - SoA bring-up and end-state cleanup**
- ✓ [S4-01a1] Enforced canonical helper authority for read helper `count` and split the remaining read-helper leaf. Completed: old-surface explicit `/soa_vector/count` call and slash-method forms now reject with deterministic unknown-method diagnostics unless a same-path `/soa_vector/count` helper is actually visible, canonical paths remain accepted, and semantics coverage in `test_semantics_calls_and_flow_collections_02` now locks old-surface reject behavior.
- ✓ [S4-01a2] Enforced canonical helper authority for read helpers `get` and `get_ref`. Completed: explicit old-surface `/soa_vector/get|get_ref` call and slash-method forms now reject with deterministic unknown-method diagnostics unless same-path helper definitions are visible, canonical helper forms remain accepted, and semantics/source-lock coverage now asserts both helper gates.
- ✓ [S4-01b] Enforced canonical helper authority for borrowed helpers `ref` and `ref_ref`. Completed: explicit old-surface `/soa_vector/ref|ref_ref` call and slash-method forms now reject with deterministic unknown-method diagnostics unless same-path helper definitions are visible, canonical helper forms remain accepted, and semantics/source-lock coverage now locks both borrowed helper gates.
- ✓ [S4-01c] Enforced canonical helper authority for conversion helpers `to_aos` and `to_aos_ref`. Completed: explicit old-surface `/to_aos|/to_aos_ref` call and slash-method forms now reject with deterministic unknown-method diagnostics unless same-path helper definitions are visible, canonical conversion helper forms remain accepted, and semantics/source-lock coverage now locks borrowed and non-borrowed conversion helper gates.
- ✓ [S4-02] Removed canonical conversion fallback dependence on experimental conversion-helper wildcard alias wiring. Completed: `SemanticsValidatorBuildImports.cpp` no longer has dedicated `/std/collections/experimental_soa_vector_conversions/*` alias registration (`soaVectorToAos*`); canonical conversion routing remains on `/std/collections/soa_vector/to_aos(_ref)` semantics, and source-lock coverage now asserts that hardcoded conversion-wildcard alias shim is absent.
- ✓ [S4-03] Deleted one full compiler-owned SoA compatibility island end-to-end (experimental conversion wildcard alias shim). Completed: removed the compiler-owned `registerExperimentalSoaVectorConversionWildcardAliases(...)` code path from import alias construction, added source-lock coverage asserting removal, and added a focused parse+lower+IR validation regression proving bare `soaVectorToAosRef(...)` still resolves through generic wildcard import scanning.

**Group 15 - Semantic memory footprint and multithread compile substrate**
- ✓ [P1-02] Delivered a measured peak-RSS reduction on the representative primary semantic fixture. Completed: definition validation now routes full and chunked passes through a shared stable-index resolver (removing transient full-range stable-index vector materialization in serial/single-partition fallback flows), and benchmark measurement on `math_star_repro` `ast-semantic` shows median peak RSS reduced from `3,492,544,512` bytes (baseline report `benchmarks/semantic_memory_baseline_report.json`) to `2,765,406,208` bytes (current report `build-release/benchmarks/p1_02_current_report.json`), a `20.8%` reduction.
- ✓ [P1-01] Enforced benchmark regression gates against checked-in budget policy. Completed: `scripts/semantic_memory_ci_artifacts.py --mode benchmark` now runs budget/trend checks by default (with explicit opt-out), benchmark CMake target/test wiring now passes policy and trend report paths into that gate, policy docs now define benchmark-mode pass/fail flow, and benchmark-harness wrapper coverage now locks benchmark-mode pass/fail propagation.
- ✓ [P1-03] Delivered deterministic parallel-ready semantic partition validation behind worker-mode flagging. Completed: semantic-memory benchmark tooling now supports `--definition-validation-workers 1|2|both`, emits worker-mode delta rows plus per-mode dump hashes, fails `both` mode on dump hash mismatches, and CMake now exposes dedicated expensive parity target/test wiring (`primestruct_semantic_memory_definition_worker_parity`, `PrimeStruct_semantic_memory_definition_worker_parity`) that runs the same parity contract via CI artifact capture.

**Group 15 - Semantic memory footprint and multithread compile substrate**
- ✓ [P0-15] Added a canonical benchmark regeneration command snippet in docs that maps each fixture/phase pair to expected report rows. Completed: `docs/semantic_memory_benchmark_policy.md` now includes an explicit canonical `semantic_memory_benchmark.py` command (fixed fixture list + phases + report path) and a full `(fixture, phase)` expected-row mapping table for the 10-fixture two-phase run.
- ✓ [P0-16] Added focused benchmark-harness coverage that fails when required fixture/phase tuples are missing from regenerated reports. Completed: `test_compile_run_benchmark_harness.cpp` now runs a partial regenerated report (`no_import,math_vector`) and asserts required canonical tuple-matrix validation exits nonzero with explicit missing tuple output.
- ✓ [P0-17] Disambiguated method-target memoization benchmark delta rows across simultaneous graph-local mode sweeps. Completed: `scripts/semantic_memory_benchmark.py` now groups `method_target_memoization_deltas` by fixture/phase plus semantic-product-force, no-fact-emission, fact-family, and graph-local mode dimensions (key/side-channel/dependency-scratch) and includes those dimensions in each delta row, while `test_compile_run_benchmark_harness.cpp` runtime coverage now executes `--method-target-memoization both --graph-local-auto-key-mode both` and asserts two distinct delta rows (`compact` vs `legacy-shadow`) instead of one conflated row.
- ✓ [P0-18] Disambiguated graph-local key-mode benchmark delta rows across simultaneous semantic-product force sweeps. Completed: `scripts/semantic_memory_benchmark.py` now groups `graph_local_auto_key_mode_deltas` by fixture/phase plus memoization mode, semantic-product-force, no-fact-emission, fact-family, and graph-local side-channel/dependency-scratch dimensions (instead of only fixture/phase/memoization), and `test_compile_run_benchmark_harness.cpp` runtime coverage now executes `--semantic-product-force both --graph-local-auto-key-mode both` and asserts two distinct key-mode delta rows (`on` and `off` force modes) instead of one conflated row.
- ✓ [P0-19] Disambiguated graph-local side-channel benchmark delta rows across simultaneous semantic-product force sweeps. Completed: `scripts/semantic_memory_benchmark.py` now groups `graph_local_auto_side_channel_mode_deltas` by fixture/phase plus memoization mode, key mode, semantic-product-force, no-fact-emission, fact-family, and dependency-scratch mode (instead of only fixture/phase/memoization/key), and `test_compile_run_benchmark_harness.cpp` runtime coverage now executes `--semantic-product-force both --graph-local-auto-side-channel-mode both` and asserts two distinct side-channel delta rows (`on` and `off` force modes) instead of one conflated row.
- ✓ [P0-20] Disambiguated graph-local dependency-scratch benchmark delta rows across simultaneous semantic-product force sweeps. Completed: `scripts/semantic_memory_benchmark.py` now groups `graph_local_auto_dependency_scratch_mode_deltas` by fixture/phase plus memoization mode, key mode, side-channel mode, semantic-product-force, no-fact-emission, and fact-family (instead of only fixture/phase/memoization/key/side-channel), and `test_compile_run_benchmark_harness.cpp` runtime coverage now executes `--semantic-product-force both --graph-local-auto-dependency-scratch-mode both` and asserts two distinct dependency-scratch delta rows (`on` and `off` force modes) instead of one conflated row.
- ✓ [P0-21] Canonicalized semantic-memory benchmark fact-family fallback mode across delta grouping paths. Completed: `scripts/semantic_memory_benchmark.py` now routes delta grouping through shared helper `benchmark_row_fact_families_mode(...)` so missing row metadata consistently falls back to `fact_families="auto"` (instead of mixed `auto`/`all` defaults) across semantic-validation, semantic-product-force, method-target memoization, and graph-local key/side-channel/dependency-scratch delta reports, and source-lock coverage in `test_compile_run_benchmark_harness.cpp` now asserts the shared helper is present and the old `"fact_families", "all"` fallback string does not reappear.
- ✓ [P0-22] Canonicalized legacy `fact_families=all` row metadata during delta grouping so mixed historical/new rows still pair. Completed: `scripts/semantic_memory_benchmark.py` now normalizes `benchmark_row_fact_families_mode(...)` values `""|all|auto` to canonical `auto` before grouping, and runtime benchmark-harness coverage in `test_compile_run_benchmark_harness.cpp` now imports the helper script with synthetic rows and asserts `compute_semantic_product_force_deltas(...)` still emits one paired delta row with canonical `fact_families="auto"` when legacy `all` and canonical `auto` rows are mixed.
- ✓ [P0-23] Canonicalized blank legacy benchmark mode metadata before delta grouping so mixed rows keep attribution pairing. Completed: `scripts/semantic_memory_benchmark.py` now normalizes blank/default row mode metadata through shared helpers for `semantic_product_force`, `method_target_memoization`, and graph-local key/side-channel/dependency-scratch modes before all delta-grouping keys are built, and benchmark-harness coverage in `test_compile_run_benchmark_harness.cpp` now includes a synthetic runtime check that `compute_method_target_memoization_deltas(...)` still emits one delta row with canonical `semantic_product_force="auto"` when on/off memoization rows mix blank and explicit `auto` force metadata.
- ✓ [P0-24] Canonicalized legacy `no_fact_emission` row metadata before delta grouping so string-typed false rows keep attribution pairing. Completed: `scripts/semantic_memory_benchmark.py` now routes all delta-grouping keys through shared `benchmark_row_no_fact_emission_mode(...)` normalization (instead of direct `bool(row.get(...))` coercion) so legacy `"false"`/`"0"` row metadata canonicalizes to `False`, and benchmark-harness coverage in `test_compile_run_benchmark_harness.cpp` now includes a synthetic runtime check that `compute_semantic_product_force_deltas(...)` still emits one canonical delta row when mixed string/bool false metadata is present.
- ✓ [P0-25] Canonicalized mixed-case and variant legacy benchmark mode metadata before delta grouping so case/order variants keep attribution pairing. Completed: `scripts/semantic_memory_benchmark.py` now lower-canonicalizes row mode metadata for semantic-product force, method-target memoization, and graph-local key/side-channel/dependency-scratch modes (including `_`→`-` variants for graph-local modes), and fact-family row metadata now lower-canonicalizes plus normalizes collector-family order via `SEMANTIC_COLLECTOR_FAMILIES` before grouping; benchmark-harness coverage in `test_compile_run_benchmark_harness.cpp` now includes a synthetic runtime check that `compute_graph_local_auto_dependency_scratch_mode_deltas(...)` still emits one canonical delta row when mixed-case and reordered legacy metadata is present.
- ✓ [P0-26] Canonicalized null legacy `no_fact_emission` row metadata before delta grouping so null/false rows keep attribution pairing. Completed: `scripts/semantic_memory_benchmark.py` now treats `no_fact_emission=None` as false (and also treats string `none`/`null` as false) in `benchmark_row_no_fact_emission_mode(...)`, and benchmark-harness coverage in `test_compile_run_benchmark_harness.cpp` now includes a synthetic runtime check that `compute_semantic_product_force_deltas(...)` still emits one canonical delta row when null and false row metadata are mixed.
- ✓ [P0-27] Canonicalized null legacy benchmark mode metadata across non-boolean row mode fields before delta grouping so null/default rows keep attribution pairing. Completed: `scripts/semantic_memory_benchmark.py` now treats `None` (and `null`/`none` string variants where applicable) as canonical defaults for `semantic_product_force`, `method_target_memoization`, graph-local key/side-channel/dependency-scratch modes, and fact-family auto-mode fallback, and benchmark-harness coverage in `test_compile_run_benchmark_harness.cpp` now includes a synthetic runtime check that `compute_graph_local_auto_key_mode_deltas(...)` still emits one canonical delta row when null/default metadata rows are mixed with explicit canonical rows.
- ✓ [P0-28] Canonicalized boolean-like legacy on/off row metadata before delta grouping so textual and numeric boolean aliases keep attribution pairing. Completed: `scripts/semantic_memory_benchmark.py` now canonicalizes `semantic_product_force` and `method_target_memoization` aliases (`1|true|yes` -> `on`, `0|false|no` -> `off`) before delta grouping, source-lock coverage in `test_compile_run_benchmark_harness.cpp` now asserts those canonicalization branches remain in the helper script, and runtime benchmark-harness coverage now includes a synthetic check that both `compute_semantic_product_force_deltas(...)` and `compute_method_target_memoization_deltas(...)` still emit one canonical delta row when mixed boolean-like legacy metadata rows are present.

**Group 15 - Semantic memory footprint and multithread compile substrate**
- ✓ [P0-01] Added a minimized checked-in reproducer fixture derived from the high-RSS `/std/math/*` case as the primary memory regression target. Completed: `benchmarks/semantic_memory/fixtures/math_star_repro.prime` is now explicitly documented as the primary high-RSS reproducer, and benchmark-harness guard coverage locks it as the first `primary` fixture with a minimal `/std/math/*` call-chain body.
- ✓ [P0-02] Added benchmark fixtures for no-import and `/std/math/vector` semantic runs (`ast-semantic` + `semantic-product`). Completed: benchmark docs now call out `no_import.prime` and `math_vector.prime` as semantic-memory attribution fixtures, and benchmark-harness execution coverage now runs `semantic_memory_benchmark.py` on exactly those fixtures and asserts the four required fixture-phase result tuples (`no_import`/`math_vector` × `ast-semantic`/`semantic-product`).
- ✓ [P0-03] Added benchmark fixtures for `/std/math/vector+matrix` and `/std/math/*` semantic runs (`ast-semantic` + `semantic-product`). Completed: benchmark docs now call out `math_vector_matrix.prime` and `math_star_repro.prime` as the paired vector+matrix-vs-star attribution fixtures, and benchmark-harness execution coverage now runs `semantic_memory_benchmark.py` on exactly those fixtures and asserts the four required fixture-phase result tuples (`math_vector_matrix`/`math_star_repro` × `ast-semantic`/`semantic-product`).
- ✓ [P0-04] Added a large non-math include fixture with comparable definition count to separate math-specific behavior from include-scaling behavior. Completed: `non_math_large_include.prime` now imports `/std/bench_non_math/*` (a benchmark-only non-math include surface built on `/std/collections/*` + `/std/file/*`), and benchmark-harness coverage compiles both `non_math_large_include` and `math_star_repro` AST dumps and asserts large, ratio-bounded top-level definition-count comparability.
- ✓ [P0-05] Added paired fixtures that compile identical math bodies via inline source vs stdlib imports. Completed: benchmark docs now call out `inline_math_body.prime` and `imported_math_body.prime` as the inline-vs-import attribution pair, and benchmark-harness coverage asserts import-stripped body text stays identical while `semantic_memory_benchmark.py` returns the exact four fixture-phase tuples (`inline_math_body`/`imported_math_body` × `ast-semantic`/`semantic-product`).
- ✓ [P0-06] Added `1x/2x/4x` scale-step fixtures and reported RSS/time slope. Completed: benchmark docs now call out `scale_1x.prime`, `scale_2x.prime`, and `scale_4x.prime` as the linear-chain scale-step fixtures, and benchmark-harness coverage asserts the fixture size steps (`f1`/`f3`/`f7`) while validating `semantic_memory_benchmark.py` emits both phase `scale_slopes` rows with canonical `x_axis`/fixture ordering and finite RSS/time slope values.
- ✓ [P0-07] Added benchmark helper wall-time + peak-RSS machine-readable report rows per fixture/phase run. Completed: benchmark-harness execution coverage now runs `semantic_memory_benchmark.py --runs 1 --fixtures no_import --phases ast-semantic --report-json ...` and asserts the report schema plus single-row wall/RSS sample arrays (`wall_seconds`, `peak_rss_bytes`) and matching median/worst aggregates for that fixture/phase tuple.
- ✓ [P0-08] Extended benchmark report coverage with key-cardinality metrics (distinct direct/method target keys and max key length). Completed: benchmark-harness execution coverage now runs `semantic_memory_benchmark.py --runs 1 --fixtures no_import,math_vector --phases ast-semantic,semantic-product --report-json ...` and asserts semantic-product rows include `key_cardinality` with `distinct_direct_call_target_keys`, `distinct_method_call_target_keys`, and `max_target_key_length` while ast-semantic rows omit that payload.
- ✓ [P0-09] Locked default three-run benchmark sampling and median/worst rollups. Completed: benchmark-harness execution coverage now runs `semantic_memory_benchmark.py` without `--runs` (single `no_import`/`ast-semantic` tuple), asserts report-level and row-level `runs == 3`, validates three-sample wall/RSS arrays, and checks row median/worst fields match computed values from those samples.
- ✓ [P0-10] Locked benchmark-only per-fact-family collector toggles for independent enable/disable behavior. Completed: benchmark-harness execution coverage now runs `semantic_memory_benchmark.py --fact-families direct_call_targets`, `--fact-families method_call_targets`, and `--fact-families direct_call_targets,method_call_targets` on the same semantic-product fixture and asserts the report toggles direct/method key-cardinality counts independently while preserving canonical `fact_families` row metadata.
- ✓ [P0-11] Added benchmark-only semantic-validation-without-fact-emission mode for validator-vs-fact A/B attribution. Completed: `semantic_memory_benchmark.py` now supports `--semantic-validation-without-fact-emission off|on|both` (with `--no-fact-emission` as a compatibility alias), emits per-fixture/phase `semantic_validation_without_fact_emission_deltas` RSS/time rows, and benchmark-harness execution coverage now runs `--semantic-validation-without-fact-emission both` on a semantic-product fixture and asserts facts-on/facts-off key-cardinality behavior plus A/B delta-report presence.
- ✓ [P0-12] Added benchmark-only semantic-product force on/off override for gate-effectiveness A/B runs. Completed: `semantic_memory_benchmark.py --semantic-product-force` now supports `both` mode for paired `on`/`off` runs (guarded to non-`semantic-product` phases), emits per-fixture/phase `semantic_product_force_deltas` RSS/time rows, and benchmark-harness execution coverage now runs `--phases ast-semantic --semantic-product-force both --semantic-phase-counters` and asserts forced-`on` semantic-product build facts are present while forced-`off` facts stay zero.
- ✓ [P0-13] Locked checked-in initial semantic-memory baseline report coverage (fixture + phase + RSS/time). Completed: benchmark-harness baseline-artifact coverage now parses `benchmarks/semantic_memory_baseline_report.json` as JSON and enforces schema/runs, full fixture×phase pair coverage, required RSS/time fields on every row, and expensive-offender annotations so baseline drift or incomplete tuple coverage fails deterministically.
- ✓ [P0-14] Marked semantic-memory benchmark/trend CTest entries as serial expensive targets when baseline rows exceed expensive-test thresholds. Completed: benchmark-harness CMake guard coverage now verifies baseline `expensive_thresholds`/`expensive_offenders` contain threshold exceedance evidence and locks both `PrimeStruct_semantic_memory_benchmark` and `PrimeStruct_semantic_memory_trend` to `RUN_SERIAL` + `expensive` labels/timeouts.
- ✓ [P1-01] Added explicit compile-pipeline semantic-product decision states for pure `ast-semantic` paths. Completed: compile-pipeline output now publishes `CompilePipelineSemanticProductDecision` (`SkipForAstSemanticDump`, non-consuming skip, and benchmark force on/off states), run-time semantic-product requests derive from that explicit decision helper, and compile-pipeline registry coverage now asserts decision states across ast-semantic, consuming, skip, and benchmark-forced flows.
- ✓ [P1-02] Added phase-level assertions/counters proving semantic-product builder skips pure `ast-semantic` paths. Completed: semantic validation now records `semanticProductBuild.callsVisited = 1` only when semantic-product build executes, and compile-pipeline registry coverage now asserts ast-semantic benchmark phase-counters keep semantic-product build counters at zero while validation counters remain populated.
- ✓ [P1-03] Added focused coverage proving semantic-product generation only occurs for semantic-product dumps and consuming emit paths. Completed: compile-pipeline registry coverage now runs a dump/emit scenario matrix (`pre_ast`, `ast`, `ir`, `type-graph`, `ast-semantic`, `semantic-product`, `native` emit, `vm` emit) and asserts semantic-product request/build/publication remains off for non-consuming dumps while staying on for semantic-product dumps and consuming emit paths.
- ✓ [P1-04] Migrated one non-consuming backend dispatch entry point to skip semantic-product requests. Completed: `primec` now pre-resolves backend kind before compile-pipeline execution and sets `skipSemanticProductForNonConsumingPath` when no backend exists and no dump stage is requested, so unknown emit-kind paths avoid semantic-product allocation; backend-registry source-lock coverage now asserts that preflight skip wiring.
- ✓ [P1-05] Migrated a second non-consuming backend IR-preparation entry point to skip semantic-product requests. Completed: `primevm` now keeps `--debug-replay` on a trace-only fast path whenever no dump stage is requested, bypassing compile-pipeline/IR-preparation semantic-product demand on that non-consuming entrypoint; backend-registry source-lock coverage now asserts this fast path remains before compile-pipeline invocation.
- ✓ [P1-06] Added/refreshed backend-entrypoint inventory and mapped each remaining non-consuming family to one explicit follow-up leaf. Completed: `docs/semantic_product_backend_entrypoint_inventory.md` now tags outstanding helper families with their owned leaves (`P1-06a` and `P1-06b`) and includes an explicit gap-to-leaf mapping table so the remaining non-consuming work is unambiguous.
- ✓ [P1-06a] Migrated `tests/unit/test_ir_pipeline_helpers.h::parseAndValidateThroughCompilePipeline` to skip semantic-product requests when semantic output is not requested. Completed: non-semantic-output helper calls now set `skipSemanticProductForNonConsumingPath` through explicit semantic-product intent selection, and backend-registry source-lock coverage now asserts that skip intent wiring remains in the helper.
- ✓ [P1-06b] Added explicit semantic-product intent plumbing to `include/primec/testing/CompilePipelineDumpHelpers.h` so non-consuming dump families cannot regress into implicit semantic-product allocation. Completed: dump-stage capture helpers now require an explicit `CompilePipelineSemanticProductIntent` argument (no implicit stage-to-intent helper), semantic-boundary callers pass `SkipForNonConsumingPath` for `ast-semantic`/`ir` and `Require` for `semantic-product`, and backend-registry source-lock coverage now asserts this explicit intent wiring.

- ✓ [P1-08] Added formatter/update path that resolves module references without re-copying entries for that migrated fact family. Completed: semantic-product formatter and downstream semantic-target adapter wiring now resolve first-slice module-indexed families through `semanticProgramDirectCallTargetView(...)`, `semanticProgramMethodCallTargetView(...)`, `semanticProgramCallableSummaryView(...)`, and `semanticProgramBindingFactView(...)` rather than per-module copied entry storage, with source-lock coverage pinning those view-based update paths.

- ✓ [P1-10] Migrate a second high-volume copied fact family to canonical reference/index storage. - ✓ [P1-11] Add semantic-product text-parity regression test for the second dedup slice. Completed: `SemanticsValidatorStatementVectorHelpers.cpp` now reuses statement-scope `hasNamedStatementArgs` inside `validateBuiltinNamedReceiverShape(...)`, `findCanonicalBuiltinCompatibilityOperandIndex(...)`, and downstream named-argument gating, and it now hoists `helperAllowsSoaVectorTarget` out of the inner candidate-receiver loop so `helperName == "push" || helperName == "reserve"` is computed once per helper-shape validation pass instead of once per candidate probe; source-lock coverage in `test_ir_pipeline_validation_18` now asserts the hoisted helper-name SoA-target bool.
- ✓ [P2-32] Removed remaining early-path named-argument rescans in statement vector helper validation by lifting the statement cache to shared scope. Completed: `SemanticsValidatorStatementVectorHelpers.cpp` now computes `hasNamedStatementArgs` before early bare-mutator and legacy SoA checks and reuses it for bare positional reorder gating plus the old explicit SoA named-argument reject path, instead of rescanning `stmt.argNames` via local `hasNamedArgs` and direct `hasNamedArguments(stmt.argNames)` calls in those early branches; source-lock coverage in `test_ir_pipeline_validation_18` now asserts the shared cache and rejects the removed local early-path scan declaration.
- ✓ [P2-33] Removed repeated resolved-helper visibility lookups in statement vector helper fallback gating by caching visibility booleans after receiver probing. - ✓ [P2-34] Removed repeated resolved-helper definition map lookups in statement vector helper receiver-probe gating by caching the map-membership bool once per statement pass. - ✓ [P2-35] Removed repeated named `values` receiver-index scans in statement vector helper fallback probing by caching the index once per statement pass. - ✓ [P2-36] Removed repeated declared/imported visibility call pairs in statement vector mutator explicit-path checks by routing those checks through one shared helper. Completed: `SemanticsValidatorStatementVectorHelpers.cpp` now defines `hasDeclaredOrImportedPath(...)` and reuses it for explicit canonical/alias mutator call + method path checks and bare builtin preferred-path visibility checks instead of repeating `!hasDeclaredDefinitionPath(path) && !hasImportedDefinitionPath(path)` in each branch; source-lock coverage in `test_ir_pipeline_validation_18` now asserts the shared helper and rejects the removed inline explicit-call pair shape.
- ✓ [P2-37] Removed repeated experimental-vector prefix scans in statement vector helper fallback branches by caching the resolved-helper prefix bool once per statement pass. Completed: `SemanticsValidatorStatementVectorHelpers.cpp` now computes `isResolvedExperimentalVectorHelper` once from `vectorHelperResolved` and reuses it for indexed-removal experimental-bridge detection plus experimental helper template-arg inference gating, instead of repeating `/std/collections/experimental_vector/` `rfind(...)` checks in each branch; source-lock coverage in `test_ir_pipeline_validation_18` now asserts the shared prefix bool and rejects the removed inline `helperCall.templateArgs.empty()` + repeated `rfind(...)` check shape.
- ✓ [P2-38] Removed repeated standalone SoA growth borrow conflict block logic in statement vector helper branches by routing it through one shared helper. Completed: `SemanticsValidatorStatementVectorHelpers.cpp` now defines `failIfStandaloneSoaGrowthBorrowed(...)` and reuses it in `push`, `reserve`, `remove_at/remove_swap`, and `clear` paths instead of repeating inline `isSoaGrowthBinding(...)` + `resolveStandaloneSoaGrowthRoot(...)` + `hasActiveBorrowForRoot(...)` borrow-conflict blocks per branch, while preserving clear-only gating; source-lock coverage in `test_ir_pipeline_validation_18` now asserts the shared helper and rejects the removed repeated inline borrow-conflict block shape.
- ✓ [P2-39] Removed repeated builtin receiver-index fallback expressions in statement vector helper builtin branches by caching the fallback-adjusted receiver index once per statement pass. - ✓ [P2-40] Removed repeated builtin operand-index fallback expressions in statement vector helper builtin branches by routing them through one shared helper. - ✓ [P3-11] Migrated `callable_summaries` semantic-product strings to ID-backed internal storage with unchanged dump output. - ✓ [P3-12] Migrated `binding_facts` semantic-product strings to ID-backed internal storage with unchanged dump output. Completed: binding facts now carry interned symbol IDs for scope/site/name/resolved path/type/reference-root text while preserving the existing semantic-product text surface.
- ✓ [P3-13] Migrated `return_facts` semantic-product strings to ID-backed internal storage with unchanged dump output. Completed: return facts now carry interned symbol IDs for definition/return kind/struct/type/reference-root text while preserving unchanged semantic-product dump output.
- ✓ [P3-14] Migrated `local_auto_facts` semantic-product strings to ID-backed internal storage with unchanged dump output. Completed: local-`auto` facts now carry interned symbol IDs for scope/binding/type/initializer/try/on_error-related text while preserving the existing dump text.
- ✓ [P3-15] Migrated `try_facts` semantic-product strings to ID-backed internal storage with unchanged dump output. Completed: `try(...)` facts now carry interned symbol IDs for scope/operand/value/error/context/on_error text while preserving the semantic-product dump format.
- ✓ [P3-16] Migrated `on_error_facts` semantic-product strings to ID-backed internal storage with unchanged dump output. Completed: `on_error` facts now carry interned symbol IDs for definition/handler/error/bound-arg/result text while preserving dump parity.
- ✓ [P3-01] Added deterministic compilation-local string interning (`SymbolId`) with explicit lifetime/ownership rules. Completed: semantic fact hot paths now use a compilation-scoped symbol interner contract with stable ownership boundaries for ID-backed string storage.
- ✓ [P3-02] Added repeat-run determinism tests that identical single-thread inputs produce identical `SymbolId` assignments. Completed: interner tests now lock repeat-run and input-order determinism for single-thread compilation.
- ✓ [P3-03] Added worker-local interner API boundaries (no shared mutex in hot paths) plus deterministic merge hooks. Completed: worker-local interner snapshots/merge seams are explicit and deterministic merge hooks are covered by merge determinism tests.
- ✓ [P3-04] Migrated call-target path keys from `std::string` to `SymbolId` while keeping diagnostics text unchanged. Completed: call-target keying now uses interned IDs and resolves to canonical text only at diagnostic/dump formatting boundaries.
- ✓ [P3-05] Migrated method-target keys from `std::string` to `SymbolId` while keeping diagnostics text unchanged. Completed: method-target keying now uses interned IDs with unchanged user-facing diagnostic text output.

- ✓ [P3-07] Migrated one additional semantic-product fact family to ID-backed internal storage with unchanged dump output. Completed: bridge-routing fact-family migration continued past call-target facts with snapshot parity coverage.

- ✓ [P3-08] Removed one transitional string shadow field whose owning family is fully migrated and parity-tested. - ✓ [P3-09] Updated the migration inventory with explicit one-leaf follow-ups for each remaining fact family not yet fully ID-backed. - ✓ [P3-18] Removed `method_call_targets.resolvedPath` string shadow and required `resolvedPathId` on semantic-product paths. - ✓ [P3-21] Removed `binding_facts.resolvedPath` string shadow and required `resolvedPathId` in semantic-product consumers. Completed: binding semantic-product facts now route through `resolvedPathId` only, and lowerer binding coverage now rejects facts with missing/invalid resolved-path ids (including `InvalidSymbolId`) via focused regression and source-guard checks.
- ✓ [P3-19] Removed `bridge_path_choices.helperName` string shadow and required `helperNameId` on semantic-product paths. - ✓ [P3-20] Removed `callable_summaries.fullPath` string shadow and required `fullPathId` in semantic-product consumers. - ✓ [P4-02] Wrote deterministic ordering rules for per-worker interner merge (tie-breaks, stable keys, collision handling). Completed: the multithread design note now codifies deterministic interner merge ordering/tie-break rules and collision handling constraints used by implementation/tests.

- ✓ [P4-05] Extended interner merge helper from 2 workers to N workers while preserving deterministic IDs. Completed: N-worker deterministic merge helper support is implemented in `SymbolInterner`.
- ✓ [P4-06] Added N-worker interner merge determinism regression tests. Completed: N-worker merge tests now lock permutation/repeat-run determinism and deterministic tie-break behavior.
- ✓ [P4-07] Extracted read-only definition index/prepass (symbols + resolved declarations) with no validator-state mutation. Completed: the definition prepass API and implementation now provide deterministic read-only declaration indexing used by validation scheduling.

- ✓ [P4-09] Implemented deterministic N-way definition partitioner and standalone stability unit tests. Completed: definition partitioner implementation plus unit tests now lock deterministic balanced contiguous chunking and repeat-run stability.
- ✓ [P4-10] Ran two deterministic definition-validation work chunks in parallel behind a feature flag. Completed: two-chunk definition-validation flag wiring is implemented and covered by success/diagnostic parity tests.
- ✓ [P4-11] Extended worker scheduling from two chunks to N chunks via deterministic partitioner. Completed: configurable worker-count scheduling is implemented through benchmark worker-count wiring over the deterministic partitioner.
- ✓ [P4-12] Added focused ordering tests that lock diagnostic and semantic-product output order under N-chunk scheduling. Completed: n-chunk ordering/equivalence tests now lock diagnostic order and semantic-product output stability under multichunk scheduling.

- ✓ [P5-02] Added optional phase-level allocation counters (allocation count + allocated bytes) for semantic validation/fact collection, off by default. Completed: benchmark allocation counters now flow through the same opt-in semantic phase counter payload and remain disabled unless explicitly requested.
- ✓ [P5-03] Added optional per-phase RSS checkpoints around semantic validation and semantic-product build, emitted in benchmark reports. Completed: benchmark RSS checkpoints now emit per-phase `rss_before_bytes`/`rss_after_bytes` snapshots in semantic phase counters and in semantic-memory benchmark report artifacts when enabled.
- ✓ [P5-04] Added repeated-compile leak check (same fixture N times in one process) and report RSS drift. Completed: benchmark repeat-compile mode now runs multiple compile iterations in one process, emits repeat leak-check payload (`rss_before_bytes`, `rss_after_bytes`, per-run RSS, `rss_drift_bytes`), and is wired into semantic-memory benchmark report output.
- ✓ [P4-13] Added single-thread vs multithread equivalence tests for diagnostics ordering and semantic-product output. Completed: compile-pipeline `/std/math/*` worker-count equivalence coverage now asserts worker `1` and worker `4` produce identical semantic-product dump output and identical diagnostic message ordering on the same stress fixtures.
- ✓ [P4-14] Added explicit worker-count equivalence tests for `1`, `2`, and `4` workers. Completed: compile-pipeline `/std/math/*` worker-count equivalence coverage now asserts workers `1`, `2`, and `4` produce identical semantic-product dump output and identical diagnostic message ordering on the same stress fixtures.
- ✓ [P4-15] Added concurrency stress tests on high-definition-count imports (including `/std/math/*`) with deterministic-output assertions. Completed: compile-pipeline stress coverage now exercises high-definition-count `/std/math/*` sources under benchmark worker-count mode and locks deterministic semantic-product dump output plus deterministic diagnostic ordering across repeated stress runs.

- ✓ Replace semantic-product source-location lookup adapters with id-based joins. - ✓ Make semantic-product identity and provenance explicit across the lowering boundary. - ✓ Make graph-backed query and `try(...)` facts required for lowerer call/result setup. - ✓ Move result/return metadata consumption to semantic-product-only ownership. Completed: semantic-product-backed lowering now builds `getReturnInfo(...)` caches from published callable summaries plus return facts instead of running AST/graph return inference whenever a `SemanticProgram` is present, and the focused lowerer inference tests now lock that semantic-product-owned return/result metadata path directly.

**Group 11 - Near-term graph queue**
- ✓ Expand positive end-to-end graph conformance coverage for query-local helper dispatch on C++/VM/native after lifting the compile-pipeline `if branches must return compatible types` boundary. Completed: IR lowering now preserves collection binding metadata across `if` branch joins and branch-shaped local `auto` initializers fed by resolved collection-returning calls, the compile-pipeline parity corpus now treats query-local `/vector/count` direct-call and slash-method forms as successful graph cases, and the C++/VM/native backend suites now execute those same direct-call and slash-method helper routes end-to-end instead of pinning the old lowering failure.
- ✓ Implement graph performance guardrails and sustained perf coverage now that the regression-budget contract is documented. Completed: baseline metrics, regression thresholds/reporting, and sustained perf coverage are now in place.
- ✓ Add baseline graph timing and invalidation metrics for the current stabilized graph surface. Completed: the type-resolution graph dump now includes prepare/build timing, node/edge counts, per-kind node/edge totals, SCC counts, and invalidation fan-out counters, while the graph snapshot helper now surfaces prepare/build timing, node/edge totals, and the same invalidation counters.
  - ✓ Add regression thresholds and reporting for those graph metrics. Completed: type-graph dumps now report optional prepare/build budget caps and over-budget flags via `PRIMESTRUCT_GRAPH_PREPARE_MS_MAX` and `PRIMESTRUCT_GRAPH_BUILD_MS_MAX`, and the graph snapshot helper now surfaces the same budget/over-budget data.
- ✓ Add sustained graph performance coverage over the stabilized graph surface. Completed: baseline timing/budget metrics now exist; a lightweight graph perf snapshot test records prepare/build timings; budgeted prep/build coverage is wired; and a long-running graph perf soak is available behind `PRIMESTRUCT_GRAPH_SOAK`.
  - ✓ Add a lightweight graph perf snapshot test that records prepare/build timings for a fixed corpus.
  - ✓ Add budgeted perf coverage for graph prep/build on a representative compile-pipeline fixture.
  - ✓ Add a long-running graph perf soak (disabled by default) for larger program suites.
- ✓ Prototype optional parallel solve now that its execution and merge contract is documented. Completed: a first partition/execution layer now groups condensation DAG components into stable layers with deterministic merge ordering, and parity coverage asserts the layer-order toggle matches the default solve.

**Group 12 - Semantics/lowering boundary**
Boundary note: this group is now split into semantic-product creation, pipeline plumbing, lowering cutover, and cleanup so it can be worked incrementally instead of as one flat migration queue.

Lowering cutover:
- ✓ Implement the `prepareIrModule` / `IrLowerer::lower` entrypoint cutover now that the handoff contract is documented. Completed: production entrypoints now pass the semantic product into `prepareIrModule(...)` and the main `IrLowerer::lower(...)` path; lowerer-wide effect validation, import/layout setup, and helper/local setup now prefer semantic-product-backed lowering facts, and the legacy raw-`Program` compatibility overload is now retired under helper-surface cleanup.

Coverage and migration cleanup:
- ✓ Migrate tests and public testing helpers from `primec/testing/SemanticsValidationHelpers.h` now that their boundary/migration contract is documented, moving lowering-facing assertions onto the semantic-product inspection surface. Completed: lowering-facing dump, compile-pipeline, and backend-facing assertions now route through semantic-product-aware helper surfaces, graph/type-resolution testing snapshots now live in `primec/testing/SemanticsGraphHelpers.h`, `primec/testing/SemanticsValidationHelpers.h` is reduced to syntax-owned canonicalization/assertion helpers, the legacy raw-`Program` `IrLowerer::lower(...)` compatibility overload plus its final fallback parity case are gone, the public prepared-IR pipeline helper is internalized, no redundant public backend helper entrypoints or backend-facing compatibility shims remain, and the testing-only semantic snapshot transport layer is fully deleted.

  now preserve borrowed-root provenance through local `slot` aliases,
  `soaColumnBorrowSlot<T>(...)` / `vectorBorrowSlot<T>(...)` now validate through
  `[return<Reference<T>>]`, and the public value-returning wrappers intentionally
  keep dereferencing back to whole-element `T` until the later `SoaColumn<T>`
  borrowed element-view carrier lands.

- ✓ Allowed helper `return<Reference<T>>` contracts to return borrowed-value carrier
  expressions rooted in parameter-owned storage. Completed: direct
  `return(borrow(...))` now validates for helper `return<Reference<T>>` contracts
  when the borrowed `uninitialized<T>` storage is rooted in parameter-owned
  reference/pointer storage, including `borrow(dereference(slot))`, while local
  borrow escapes remain rejected and the remaining stdlib slot-helper blocker is
  slot-pointer provenance through local `slot` aliases.

- ✓ Split the oversized Group 8 stdlib slot-borrow helper exposure item into the real helper-return,
  slot-provenance, and final helper-contract slices. Completed: the live queue and spec/docs now
  pin the actual blockers explicitly: helper `return<Reference<T>>` still only accepts direct
  parameter references, and `soaColumnSlotUnsafe<T>(...)` / `vectorSlotUnsafe<T>(...)` still do
  not preserve borrowed-root provenance through the local `slot` pointer, so the final
  `soaColumnBorrowSlot<T>(...)` / `vectorBorrowSlot<T>(...)` retargeting no longer hides those
  prerequisites inside one oversized TODO.

- ✓ Extended standalone borrowed-value typing across pointer/reference-backed
  `borrow(dereference(slot))` storage. Completed: standalone `[Reference<T>]`
  bindings now validate through dereference-backed `uninitialized<T>` storage in
  regular bindings plus block-expression and `if`-branch binding sites, while
  the next remaining borrowed-view work starts at exposing that same carrier
  through `soaColumnBorrowSlot<T>(...)` and `vectorBorrowSlot<T>(...)`.

- ✓ Introduced direct-storage standalone borrowed-value typing so `[Reference<T>]`
  bindings can use `borrow(storage)` without `location(...)`. Completed: direct local and
  field storage now validate through that borrowed-value carrier, including regular binding,
  block-expression binding, and `if`-branch binding sites, while pointer/reference-backed
  `borrow(dereference(slot))` remains the next slot-backed carrier slice.

- ✓ Split the oversized Group 8 language-level slot-borrow carrier item into direct-storage
  borrow typing and later pointer/reference-backed `dereference(slot)` routing. Completed:
  the live queue and spec/docs now pin the current mismatch explicitly: direct
  `borrow(storage)` still validates mainly through `[return<T>]`-style positions while
  standalone `[Reference<T>] ref{borrow(storage)}` remains rejected with
  `Reference bindings require location(...)`, so that direct-storage carrier must land before
  the later slot-backed stdlib helper exposure work.

- ✓ Split the oversized Group 8 slot-borrow carrier item into the real language-level
  `borrow(dereference(slot))` carrier primitive plus the later stdlib helper exposure slices.
  Completed: the live queue and spec/docs now pin the current boundary explicitly:
  `soaColumnBorrowSlot<T>(...)` and `vectorBorrowSlot<T>(...)` still expose
  `borrow(dereference(slot))` through `[return<T>]` helper surfaces, so the next honest
  prerequisite is a language-level standalone borrowed-value carrier on that exact expression
  path.

- ✓ Split the oversized Group 8 single-column borrowed carrier item into the real slot-borrow
  carrier primitive plus the later `SoaColumn<T>` and wrapper/helper exposure slices.
  Completed: the live queue and spec/docs now pin the current boundary explicitly:
  `soaColumnBorrowSlot<T>(...)` and `vectorBorrowSlot<T>(...)` still return whole-element `T`
  through `borrow(dereference(slot))`, so a reusable slot-borrow carrier must exist before a
  `SoaColumn<T>` borrowed element-view carrier or wrapper `ref(...)` surface can materialize.

- ✓ Split the oversized Group 8 standalone borrowed element-view carrier item into the real
  missing single-column carrier substrate plus the wrapper/helper exposure slice. Completed:
  the live queue and spec/docs now pin the current boundary explicitly: a reusable borrowed
  element-view carrier over `SoaColumn<T>` must exist before experimental-wrapper
  `SoaVector<T>.ref(i)` and `soaVectorRef<T>(...)` can stop returning whole-element `T`.

- ✓ Split the oversized Group 8 standalone borrowed `ref(...)` item into the real missing
  experimental-wrapper borrowed-value carrier plus separate direct-local and explicit
  dereference receiver-routing slices. Completed: the live queue and spec/docs now pin the
  current boundary explicitly: `SoaVector<T>.ref(i)` and canonical
  `/std/collections/soa_vector/ref` still return whole-element `T` values through
  `soaColumnRef<T>(...)` rather than standalone borrowed-view objects.

- ✓ Retired the stale backend-helper cleanup tail. Completed: the remaining public backend-oriented testing surfaces are now explicitly pinned as intentional stable APIs (`primec/testing/CompilePipelineDumpHelpers.h`, `primec/testing/EmitterHelpers.h`, and `primec/testing/IrLowererHelpers.h`), so there are no redundant backend helper entrypoints or backend-facing compatibility shims left to delete.
- ✓ Deleted redundant lowering-facing pipeline helper entrypoints and the matching pipeline-facing compatibility helper. Completed: the old public `prepareCompilePipelineIrForTesting(...)` entrypoint and `CompilePipelinePreparedIr` surface are gone from `primec/testing/CompilePipelineDumpHelpers.h`, backend conformance keeps the shared semantic-product-aware public surface, and the remaining direct prepared-IR plumbing now lives only in local test helpers that still need one-off compile-pipeline setup.
- ✓ Retired the legacy raw-`Program` `IrLowerer::lower(...)` compatibility overload. Completed: the final graph-snapshot fallback parity case is deleted, public/test helper entrypoints now lower only through the semantic-product-aware path, and the raw lowering wrapper is gone from `IrLowerer.h`.
- ✓ Implemented the `prepareIrModule` / `IrLowerer::lower` entrypoint cutover. Completed: production entrypoints now pass the semantic product into `prepareIrModule(...)` and the main `IrLowerer::lower(...)` path; lowerer-wide effect validation, import/layout setup, and helper/local setup now prefer semantic-product-backed lowering facts, while the only remaining raw-`Program` lowerer entry is the compatibility overload still used by tests/helpers and tracked under helper-surface cleanup.
- ✓ Replaced lowerer-wide entry/effect/runtime-reflection validation scans with semantic-product-backed entry and callable facts. Completed: top-level definition/execution effect validation now prefers semantic-product callable summaries, while native-backend software-numeric and runtime-reflection rejection are explicitly pinned as syntax-owned backend policy scans over spelled expression surfaces.
- ✓ Replaced lowerer helper/local setup reads of raw `Program` state with semantic-product-backed callable, binding, return, and `on_error` facts. Completed: helper/local setup now prefers semantic-product `on_error`, callable, binding, and return facts where lowering-facing meaning exists; callable-definition lowering enumerates semantic-product definitions while AST definitions remain only executable body/provenance sources, statement-call lowering remains intentionally syntax-owned because it walks spelled statement bodies directly, and the math-import probe is explicitly pinned as syntax-owned import shorthand during uninitialized/helper-local setup.
- ✓ Decided and pinned the residual AST-owned field/provenance traversal inside lowerer struct-layout computation. Completed: field visibility/category/static qualifiers, field-local alignment, and direct source provenance now explicitly remain syntax-owned inputs to layout planning, so no semantic-product import/layout inventory seam is left there.
- ✓ Replaced helper/local callable-definition inventory scans with semantic-product callable inventories plus AST-owned bodies. Completed: lowered callable enumeration now comes from semantic-product definition inventories while AST definitions remain only the executable body/provenance source for those already-selected paths.
- ✓ Replaced lowerer helper/local `on_error` metadata reads with semantic-product-backed `on_error` facts while keeping bound-arg expressions syntax-owned. Completed: lowerer helper/local setup now takes handler path/error type/bound-arg count from published semantic-product `on_error` facts and only re-reads the raw AST transform arguments to parse executable bound-arg expressions.
- ✓ Replaced top-level struct-layout iteration over raw definitions with semantic-product-backed type inventories plus AST-owned provenance only. Completed: lowerer import/layout setup now enumerates struct layouts from ordered semantic-product type metadata and uses the AST `Definition*` map only to recover the source-owned body/provenance needed for field-level layout planning.
- ✓ Decided and pinned the long-term syntax-owned import-alias surface used during lowerer setup. Completed: lowerer import/layout setup now explicitly treats the alias table as a syntax-owned shorthand layer derived from spelled `import` directives plus wildcard expansion, rather than as semantic-product data; the remaining live seam there is only struct-layout iteration/provenance ownership.
- ✓ Decided and pinned the remaining AST-owned definition provenance/body inventory surface used during lowerer import/layout setup. Completed: the residual `Definition*` map in lowerer import/layout setup is now explicitly treated as AST-owned provenance/body access for field statements, namespace prefixes, and recursive layout traversal rather than as a missing semantic-product inventory seam; the live queue now tracks only syntax-owned import aliases and struct-layout iteration ownership there.
- ✓ Replaced lowerer struct inventory scans with semantic-product-backed type inventories. Completed: `buildDefinitionMapAndStructNames(...)` now derives `structNamesOut` from semantic-product type metadata when available, so the remaining raw import/layout inventory seam is only the AST-owned definition provenance/body map plus syntax-owned import aliases and struct-layout iteration.
- ✓ Split the oversized lowerer import/layout cutover item into explicit remaining seams. Completed: the live queue now tracks definition/struct inventories, syntax-owned import aliases, and struct-layout iteration ownership separately instead of hiding those raw-`Program` dependencies behind one broad import/layout bullet.
- ✓ Replaced lowerer-wide top-level entry/effect validation scans with semantic-product-backed callable summaries. Completed: `validateProgramEffects(...)` now prefers published callable summaries for definition/execution active-effect validation while leaving nested expression-transform checks syntax-owned; the remaining live lowerer-wide validation work is only the separate native-backend policy scans for software numeric and runtime-reflection rejection.
- ✓ Split the oversized lowerer-wide validation/effect/runtime-reflection cutover item into explicit remaining seams. Completed: the live queue now separates callable-summary effect validation from the still-distinct native-backend policy scans for software numeric and runtime-reflection rejection instead of hiding all three under one broad lowerer-entry bullet.
- ✓ Split the oversized `IrLowerer::lower(...)` semantic-product cutover item into explicit remaining seams. Completed: the live queue now tracks lowerer-wide validation/effect scans, import/layout setup, and helper/local setup as separate cutover slices instead of one broad lowerer-entry bullet.
- ✓ Retired the stale `prepareIrModule` semantic-product cutover TODO after auditing the current entrypoint boundary. Completed: `prepareIrModule(...)` already consumes the published semantic product directly and no longer re-derives lowering facts itself, so the remaining live entrypoint work is the main `IrLowerer::lower(...)` cutover plus final raw-`Program` retirement.
- ✓ Implemented the semantic-product ownership/provenance test matrix for source spans, debug/source-map parity, syntax-boundary coverage, and deterministic ordering. Completed: lowering-facing semantic-product facts are now pinned against AST-owned spans, semantic-product-backed lowering emits matching source-map provenance, syntax-shaped dump content stays on `ast-semantic`, and the semantic-product formatter remains deterministic enough for exact golden coverage.
  - ✓ Added debug/source-map provenance parity tests for semantic-product-backed lowering/debug entrypoints. Completed: lowering with and without the semantic product now emits identical canonical source-map metadata, and the lowered source-map surface contains the same semantic-product call/return spans that debug consumers would use.
  - ✓ Added syntax-reproduction boundary tests that keep syntax-owned data on AST dumps and lowering-owned data on the semantic-product surface. Completed: `ast-semantic` still carries field initializer and return-statement syntax, while the semantic-product dump exposes only lowering-facing facts plus provenance handles.
- ✓ Split the remaining lowerer struct/layout cutover item into explicit field-metadata seams after auditing the current boundary. Completed: the live queue now separates semantic-product field ordering/name/envelope publication, field type/envelope publication, lowerer-side field-metadata consumption, and the still-syntax-owned field qualifier decision instead of hiding all of that work behind one vague “richer layout facts” item.
- ✓ Added source-span parity tests for the semantic-product entries that currently feed lowering-facing facts. Completed: semantic-product direct-call targets, method-call targets, bridge-routing choices, type metadata, struct field metadata, binding facts, and return facts are now checked against AST-owned source locations, so the published lowering-facing provenance surface is pinned instead of only being checked for nonzero locations.
- ✓ Added deterministic ordering tests for semantic-product ownership/provenance surfaces. Completed: semantic-product field metadata, local binding facts, direct-call targets, and formatter output are now pinned to stable source-order emission so ownership/provenance coverage no longer depends on incidental traversal order.
- ✓ Finished the narrow semantic-product formatter golden coverage for provenance handles back to AST-owned ids/spans. Completed: the exact semantic-product formatter golden plus the new source-span parity coverage first pinned the lowering-facing provenance surface, which now lives on explicit `provenance_handle=<id> source="line:column"` fields.
- ✓ Retired the stale graph-backed local-`auto`/query/`try(...)`/`on_error` lowerer-adapter TODOs after auditing the current semantic-product boundary. Completed: those fact families are published and tested on the semantic-product inspection surface, but they are not current lowerer-adapter inputs, and `on_error` still keeps executable bound-arg expressions on the syntax side rather than the semantic product.
- ✓ Retired the stale lowering-facing binding/result adapter TODO after auditing the current semantic-product cutover. Completed: lowerer setup already prefers published semantic-product binding/result facts for source-owned parameters, locals, temporaries, helper results, and entry-return/result setup, so no separate live adapter seam remains for that fact family.
- ✓ Retired the stale lowering-facing effect/capability/layout adapter TODO after auditing the current semantic-product cutover. Completed: the temporary adapter already carries lowering-facing direct/receiver/helper-routing targets plus the current callable-summary and type-metadata surfaces, so the only remaining live layout work is the richer field-order/offset metadata still derived from field bindings under the lowerer struct/layout cutover item.
- ✓ Retired the stale lowerer enum-metadata cutover TODO after auditing the current lowering boundary. Completed: enums rewrite to struct form before lowering, so the live lowerer metadata seam is richer struct/layout facts rather than any separate enum-specific lowerer setup path.
- ✓ Consumed semantic-product helper-owned layout/classification metadata in lowerer setup. Completed: lowerer import/layout setup now prefers published semantic-product type metadata for struct-like classification and explicit alignment, so helper-owned aggregate classification and alignment policy no longer have to be rediscovered only from raw AST transforms.
- ✓ Consumed semantic-product execution-boundary effect/capability metadata in lowerer setup. Completed: lowered secondary callable definitions now prefer published semantic-product callable summaries for effect and capability masks instead of re-deriving those masks only from AST transforms during function-table lowering.
- ✓ Retired the stale lowerer type/binding setup cutover TODO tail after auditing the
  remaining helper-local synthetic and implicit helper-temporary paths. Completed: the
  semantic-product cutover already covers all source-owned binding/result sites that the
  lowerer can key by stable semantic identity, while the leftover helper-local synthetic
  locals and raw IR temporaries are lowerer-internal constructs that do not belong on the
  semantic-product surface.
- ✓ Consumed semantic-product lowered entry-argument binding metadata in lowerer setup. Completed: `buildEntryCountAccessSetup(...)` now prefers published semantic-product parameter binding facts for `array<string>` entry parameters, so lowered entry-count setup no longer has to re-derive that binding only from raw AST parameter state.
- ✓ Consumed semantic-product temporary binding metadata and helper-result binding/type metadata in lowerer setup. Completed: lowerer binding adapters now prefer published `SemanticProgramBindingFact` entries for call-expression temporaries, so temporary call results and helper call result classification no longer have to be re-derived only from AST transforms during lowerer setup.
- ✓ Consumed semantic-product parameter, local, and helper-parameter binding metadata in lowerer setup. Completed: the lowerer binding adapters now prefer published `SemanticProgramBindingFact` entries for binding kind, value kind, string/file-error classification, and reference/array/container metadata, so parameter/local setup plus helper-parameter setup no longer have to re-derive those facts only from AST transforms.
- ✓ Materialized semantic-product callable summaries for effect/capability setup. Completed: `SemanticProgram` now publishes deterministic callable summaries for definitions and executions, including return kind, compute/unsafe flags, active effects, capabilities, and result/on_error summary data needed by later IR-preparation cutover work.
- ✓ Materialized semantic-product struct/enum/layout metadata shell. Completed: `SemanticProgram` now publishes deterministic definition-level type metadata covering struct/enum category, public visibility, layout-policy flags, explicit alignment, and field/enum-item counts so later lowerer metadata cutover work can consume a stable shell instead of re-reading only raw transforms.
- ✓ Consumed semantic-product return binding/result metadata in lowerer setup. Completed: entry-return setup now builds `EntryReturnConfig` from published semantic-product return facts plus callable result summaries instead of re-deriving that state only from AST return transforms.
- ✓ Consumed semantic-product effect summaries and capability summaries in lowerer setup. Completed: entry effect/capability masks now resolve directly from published semantic-product callable summaries instead of rebuilding those masks from AST transforms during normal entry setup.
- ✓ Finished the narrow semantic-product golden coverage for resolved call/helper targets, binding/result type facts, and effect/capability plus struct/layout metadata. Completed: the exact semantic-product formatter golden now pins all three fact families in one deterministic dump surface, so only provenance-handle coverage remains live under the narrow golden suite.
- ✓ Removed the false current borrowed-slot projection invalidation/provenance subtree after confirming that the current projection surface rewrites each use to `soaVectorGet(...).field` / `soaVectorRef(...).field` and therefore does not materialize a standalone borrowed value that can survive later `push` / `reserve` / conversion / destruction events.
- ✓ Split the live Group 8 borrowed-view invalidation queue again to match the actual current substrate, separating current indexed/field-level borrowed-slot projection invalidation from later standalone `ref(...)` and standalone borrowed field-view invalidation.
- ✓ Split the live Group 8 borrowed-view invalidation queue again along the real current substrate boundary, separating current `ref(...)` borrowed-slot invalidation from later standalone borrowed field-view invalidation and splitting the current growth slice into explicit `push` and `reserve` follow-ups.
- ✓ Split the live Group 8 borrowed-view structural invalidation item into current growth, later shrink/motion, conversion/destruction, and provenance-specific slices, so the remaining invalidation queue now matches the actual wrapper mutation and receiver families.
- ✓ Split the live Group 8 borrowed-view invalidation, richer borrowed field-view, and standalone mutating field-view write items into explicit invalidation-family and receiver-family sub-slices, so the remaining SoA behavior queue now has concrete finish lines instead of three umbrella semantics bullets.
- ✓ Added reflected chunked `SoaSchemaStorage` / `SoaSchemaStorageNew()` generation on top of the existing sixteen-column SoA substrate, so reflect-enabled schemas can now synthesize deterministic chunked allocation scaffolding before arbitrary-width grow/free wiring lands.
- ✓ Added reflected chunked `SoaSchemaStorageCount(...)`, `SoaSchemaStorageCapacity(...)`, `SoaSchemaStorageReserve(...)`, and `SoaSchemaStorageClear(...)` generation on top of `SoaSchemaStorage`, so arbitrary-width reflected SoA schemas now have deterministic chunk-aware grow/realloc plus explicit clear helpers before implicit drop/lifecycle cleanup lands.
- ✓ Finished reflected arbitrary-width chunked `SoaSchemaStorage` lifecycle cleanup by generating `/Type/SoaSchemaStorage/Destroy`, so reflect-enabled schemas now have deterministic chunked allocation, grow/realloc, explicit clear, and implicit drop/free cleanup on top of the sixteen-column substrate.
- ✓ Added reflected `SoaSchemaChunkCount`, `SoaSchemaChunkFieldStart`, and `SoaSchemaChunkFieldCount` helpers on top of the flat `SoaSchema*` surface, so arbitrary-width reflected schemas can now be grouped into deterministic sixteen-column chunks before allocation/grow/free wiring lands.
- ✓ Split the remaining arbitrary-width SoA storage umbrella again into a real chunk-descriptor prerequisite plus the later allocation/grow/free wiring slices, because the new `SoaSchema*` helpers only expose a flat reflected field list and do not yet group fields into deterministic sixteen-column storage chunks.
- ✓ Added a generated reflected `SoaSchema*` helper family (`SoaSchemaFieldCount`, `SoaSchemaFieldName`, `SoaSchemaFieldType`, `SoaSchemaFieldVisibility`) so `.prime` code can iterate reflected field metadata through runtime indices instead of direct constant-index `meta.field_*<T>(i)` queries, finishing the arbitrary-width SoA descriptor/dispatch prerequisite.
- ✓ Split the remaining oversized Group 11 and Group 12 graph/semantic-product umbrella items in `docs/todo.md` into explicit compile-pipeline/backend, invalidation, inference, CT-eval, template, widening, perf, parallel-solve, plumbing, adapter, lowerer-cutover, and backend-conformance slices, so the live queue now tracks concrete implementable seams instead of broad multi-surface bullets.
- ✓ Split the remaining Group 12 semantic-product plumbing, adapter, lowerer-cutover, helper-migration, snapshot-removal, alias-fallback, and backend-consumer coverage umbrellas into per-surface slices, so the live queue now tracks explicit CLI/runtime/report, fact-family adapter, lowerer metadata, helper cleanup, and per-backend conformance seams instead of broad migration buckets.
- ✓ Split the remaining arbitrary-width SoA storage umbrella into a real reflection prerequisite plus descriptor-backed allocation/grow/free slices, after confirming that `meta.field_name<T>(i)`, `meta.field_type<T>(i)`, and `meta.field_visibility<T>(i)` still require constant integer indices and cannot drive arbitrary-width `.prime` schema iteration directly.

**Architecture / Type-resolution graph**
**Group 4 - Ownership and lifetime substrate**
- ✓ Extend the fixed-width experimental SoA storage substrate from fifteen columns to sixteen columns and move the deterministic pending-width gate to seventeen-field reflect-enabled schemas. Progress: `/std/collections/experimental_soa_storage/*` now includes `SoaColumns16<T0, ..., T15>` plus matching `New` / `Count` / `Capacity` / `Reserve` / `Push` / `Read*` / `Write` / `Clear` helpers, and the `experimental soa storage arbitrary-width schemas pending` boundary now starts at `17` reflected fields instead of `16`.
- ✓ Add the reusable lowered lifecycle-aware destroy-at-pointer helper for fixed-capacity slot storage and build the shared builtin `vector` removed-slot destruction primitive on top of it, so indexed-removal lowering now has one reusable `Destroy*`-from-slot-pointer path ready for `remove_swap` / `remove_at` wiring.
- ✓ Wire builtin `vector` `remove_swap` onto the shared removed-slot destruction primitive and lift its separate drop-trivial guard. Completed: fixed-capacity builtin `remove_swap` now destroys the removed slot before survivor motion, and the later survivor-motion slice removed the remaining relocation-trivial guard.
- ✓ Wire builtin `vector` `remove_at` onto the shared removed-slot destruction primitive and lift its separate drop-trivial guard. Completed: fixed-capacity builtin `remove_at` now destroys the removed slot before survivor compaction begins, and the later survivor-motion slice removed the remaining relocation-trivial guard.
- ✓ Add a reusable builtin fixed-capacity vector survivor-motion layer and wire builtin `vector` `remove_swap` / `remove_at` onto it, so indexed removals no longer depend on relocation-trivial raw copies. Completed: the lowerer now has shared slot-move helpers that reuse `/Type/Move` or `/Type/Copy` when available, `remove_swap` and `remove_at` both move survivors through those helpers after removed-slot destruction, the remaining builtin relocation-trivial semantic guards are gone, and compile-run/semantics coverage now treats ownership-sensitive indexed removals as supported on the builtin fixed-capacity path.

**Group 12 - Semantics/lowering boundary**
- ✓ Split the remaining first semantic-product builder call-target item into explicit direct-call, receiver/method-call, and helper-routing slices, so the live queue now matches the documented resolution families instead of hiding them in one broad target/publication bullet.
- ✓ Split the remaining broad Group 12 semantic-product builder, publication, dump, provenance-test, and migration-cleanup items into explicit fact-family and handoff slices, so the live queue now matches the documented semantic-product contracts instead of hiding multiple independent implementation seams inside umbrella bullets.
- ✓ Implement the per-definition validation-state split now that the context/caching contract is documented. Completed: the mutable `currentValidationContext_` field is gone, definition/execution validation now scopes explicit `ValidationState` objects with immutable base `ValidationContext` facts plus traversal-local moved/borrow state, and the builder/snapshot/source-lock surfaces now use those explicit per-definition/per-execution state objects directly.
- ✓ Implement the first per-definition validation-state split slice now that the context/caching contract is documented. Completed: validator-global cached definition/execution `ValidationContext` maps are gone, the build phase now only eagerly validates definition-scoped context formation, and later call sites rebuild explicit `ValidationContext` values at use time instead of reading validator-global cached maps.
- ✓ Split the oversized builtin `vector` `remove_swap` removed-element destruction item into explicit lowered destruction-primitive and final wiring/guard-relaxation slices, because the current fixed-capacity helper path still only copies raw slots and decrements count.
- ✓ Merge the duplicate builtin `vector` `remove_swap` / `remove_at` removed-element destruction prerequisites into one shared fixed-capacity removed-slot destruction primitive plus per-helper wiring steps, because both builtin paths are blocked on the same missing lowered destroy-at-slot capability.
- ✓ Split the shared builtin `vector` removed-slot destruction primitive again into a lowerer-wide lifecycle-aware destroy-at-pointer prerequisite plus the final vector-specific destroy-at-slot helper, because the IR flow layer still only exposes raw slot copy helpers today.
- ✓ Tighten the access/target-resolution structured diagnostic-sink slices so `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprFieldResolution.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprCollectionAccess.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprPostAccessPrechecks.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprMethodTargetResolution.cpp`, and `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprCollectionCountCapacity.cpp` funnel their owned direct field-access, struct-field resolution, collection-access target, post-access precheck, method-target, and collection count/capacity target failures through shared local `fail...Diagnostic(...)` helpers instead of repeating raw `error_` writes across those validators.
- ✓ Tighten the statement-side structured diagnostic-sink slices so `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorStatement.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorStatementBuiltins.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorStatementBindings.cpp`, and `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorStatementVectorHelpers.cpp` funnel their owned direct `init/drop`, `block`/print, path-space/compute builtin, binding, and vector-helper statement failures through one shared local `fail...Diagnostic(...)` helper per file instead of repeating raw `error_` writes across those validators.
- ✓ Tighten the direct root/numeric/result structured diagnostic-sink slices so `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprNumeric.cpp`, and `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprResultFile.cpp` funnel their owned direct root-expression, numeric builtin, and file/result builtin failures through one shared local `fail...Diagnostic(...)` helper per file instead of repeating raw `error_` writes across those validators.
- ✓ Tighten the infer/build validator-body structured diagnostic-sink slices so `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorInferResolvedCalls.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorInferCollectionCountCapacity.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorInferCollectionDirectCountCapacity.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorInferLateFallbackBuiltins.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorInferPreDispatchCalls.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorInferDefinition.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorInferGraph.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorBuild.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorPassesDefinitions.cpp`, and `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorPassesOmittedInitializers.cpp` funnel their owned direct infer/build/pass failures through shared local `fail...Diagnostic(...)` helpers instead of repeating raw `error_` writes across those validators.
- ✓ Add the next structured diagnostic-sink implementation slice by sharing direct trait constraint publication. Completed: direct trait transform-shape, trait type-resolution, required helper-signature, and return-type compatibility failures in `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorTraits.cpp` now capture definition context and publish through shared `publishTraitDiagnostic(...)` / `failTraitDiagnostic(...)` helpers instead of only assigning `error_` and returning.
- ✓ Add the next structured diagnostic-sink implementation slice by sharing direct count/capacity/map builtin publication. Completed: direct vector/soa-vector/map `count`, vector `capacity`, direct wrapper count/capacity, and canonical map helper shape/target/key compatibility failures in `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprCountCapacityMapBuiltins.cpp` now funnel through one shared `failCountCapacityMapBuiltin(...)` publication helper instead of repeating raw `error_` assignment and immediate publication at each builtin branch.
- ✓ Add the next structured diagnostic-sink implementation slice by sharing direct pass-level omitted-initializer publication. Completed: direct omitted-initializer explicit-type, template-arity, struct-type, zero-arg-constructor, and effect-free-constructor failures in `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorPassesOmittedInitializers.cpp` now capture binding context and publish through `publishPassesOmittedInitializersDiagnostic(...)` instead of only assigning `error_` and returning.
- ✓ Add the next structured diagnostic-sink implementation slice by sharing direct definition-pass publication. Completed: direct lifecycle-helper return-kind failures, `on_error` return-type and error-type mismatches, default-expression / bound-arg / statement / return-expression fallback diagnostics, missing-return failures, and uninitialized-state failures in `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorPassesDefinitions.cpp` now capture definition or statement/expression context and publish through `publishPassesDefinitionsDiagnostic(...)` instead of only assigning `error_` and returning.
- ✓ Add the next structured diagnostic-sink implementation slice by sharing direct pass-level struct-layout publication.
