**Group 8 - SoA de-builtinization**
- ✓ Document the borrowed-view invalidation contract on top of the current single-column borrowed-slot substrate. Completed: `docs/PrimeStruct.md` and `docs/PrimeStruct_SyntaxSpec.md` now define the intended non-owning borrowed-view boundary for `ref(...)`, field-view borrows, `location(...)`, helper-return receivers, and method-like helper-return receivers, plus the structural mutations that invalidate those views. The live TODO now tracks only implementing those invalidation rules.
- ✓ Document the richer borrowed field-view contract on top of the current SoA borrowed-view substrate. Completed: `docs/PrimeStruct.md` and `docs/PrimeStruct_SyntaxSpec.md` now define standalone borrowed field-view values as planned non-owning column views over wrapper-owned SoA storage, covering direct borrowed locals, explicit dereference, borrowed helper-return receivers, method-like helper-return receivers, and inline `location(...)`-wrapped borrowed receivers. The live TODO now tracks only implementing those richer borrowed-view surfaces on top of the existing `soaVectorGet(...)` / `soaVectorRef(...)` substrate.
- ✓ Document the standalone mutating field-view write contract on top of the current SoA wrapper substrate. Completed: `docs/PrimeStruct.md` and `docs/PrimeStruct_SyntaxSpec.md` now define the intended writable column-view behavior for `assign(value.field(), next)` / `assign(field(value), next)` on mutable wrapper receivers, covering direct locals, borrowed locals, borrowed helper-return receivers, method-like helper-return receivers, and inline `location(...)`-wrapped variants. The live TODO now tracks only implementing those standalone mutating writes on top of the existing `soaVectorRef(...)` substrate.

Completed: `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now includes an explicit ownership split by responsibility, clarifying which syntax/provenance data stays AST-owned, which lowering facts move into the semantic product, and how lowering/debuggers must combine semantic meaning with AST-backed provenance through stable ids/spans.
- ✓ Document the planned ownership-split test matrix before implementing semantic-product provenance tests. Completed: `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now defines the intended ownership/parity checks for source spans, debug/source-map recovery, syntax-reproduction boundaries, ownership isolation, and deterministic ordering so the remaining item can focus only on test implementation.
- ✓ Update dump-stage docs/spec text to document the semantic-product inspection surface and its relation to existing AST/IR dumps. Completed: `docs/PrimeStruct.md` and `docs/PrimeStruct_SyntaxSpec.md` now define the planned inspection-surface order (`pre_ast` → `ast` → `ast-semantic` → planned `semantic-product` → `ir`) and clarify that the future semantic-product dump is the lowering-facing inspection boundary that will sit between the current canonicalized AST dump and IR.
- ✓ Update CLI help to document the semantic-product inspection surface and its relation to existing AST/IR dumps. Completed: `primec` and `primevm` usage text now explains that current lowering-facing dump stages stop at `ast-semantic` and `ir`, and that a planned semantic-product dump will sit between them once the boundary cutover lands.
- ✓ Document the planned semantic-product dump contract before implementing the formatter/golden coverage. Completed: `docs/PrimeStruct.md` and `docs/PrimeStruct_SyntaxSpec.md` now define what the future semantic-product dump must expose, what remains syntax-owned, and the deterministic ordering expectations that formatter and golden tests will need to satisfy.
- ✓ Document the planned semantic-product unit/golden suite before implementing the coverage. Completed: `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now defines the intended narrow golden corpus, the exported fact families it should pin, and the boundary between semantic-product golden coverage and broader end-to-end compile-pipeline conformance.
- ✓ Document the planned pipeline-facing semantic-product conformance matrix before implementing those tests. Completed: `docs/PrimeStruct.md` and `docs/PrimeStruct_SyntaxSpec.md` now define how pipeline-facing tests should cover the semantic-product inspection surface relative to `ast-semantic` and `ir`, and how that matrix should stay separate from the narrow semantic-product golden suite.
- ✓ Document the planned testing-only semantic snapshot removal contract before deleting that plumbing. Completed: `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now defines the migration/removal order for snapshot-only lowering-facing helpers, the boundary where AST/testing helpers may remain for syntax/provenance assertions, and the completion criteria for converging on one canonical semantic-product inspection surface.
- ✓ Document the planned public testing-helper migration contract before moving `primec/testing/SemanticsValidationHelpers.h` users onto the semantic-product surface. Completed: `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now defines how public testing helpers should split into semantic-product-owned versus AST-owned surfaces, the intended migration order for lowering-facing assertions, and the completion criteria for narrowing `primec/testing/SemanticsValidationHelpers.h` to syntax/provenance-only use.
- ✓ Document the planned lowerer stdlib/helper alias-fallback removal contract before deleting those fallback paths. Completed: `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now defines the semantic-product ownership of canonical-or-same-path helper targets, the migration order for removing lowerer-local alias recovery, and the completion criteria for proving parity through backend-facing conformance.
- ✓ Add the first structured diagnostic-sink implementation slice by sharing definition/execution pass diagnostic batching. Completed: `SemanticsValidatorDiagnostics.cpp` now owns shared helpers for “should collect”, context reset, record capture, and final report publication, and both definition-pass and execution-pass validation use that shared path instead of duplicating the same first-error batching closures.
- ✓ Add the next structured diagnostic-sink implementation slice by sharing import/transform/return-kind diagnostic publication. Completed: import-alias diagnostics, transform-build diagnostics, and return-kind diagnostics now all use the shared structured-diagnostic collection/finalization path instead of open-coding their own `collectDiagnostics_ && diagnosticInfo_` publication branches.
- ✓ Add the next structured diagnostic-sink implementation slice by sharing immediate top-level build/entry diagnostic publication. Completed: `validateEntry()` plus the immediate `buildDefinitionMaps()` failures for invalid default effects, invalid entry default effects, duplicate definitions, and top-level import-name conflicts now publish through one shared structured-diagnostic helper instead of only setting `error_` and returning.
- ✓ Document the temporary migration adapter contract before implementing the cutover shim. Completed: `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now defines the adapter's temporary role, its precedence rules when semantic-product data is available, its intended consumers, and the explicit criteria for removing the fallback once lowering entrypoints are fully cut over.
- ✓ Document the compile-pipeline success-artifact contract before publishing the semantic product through `Semantics::validate` and `CompilePipelineOutput`. Completed: `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now defines what the semantic-product success result must contain, how it stays paired with the raw AST, and how dump/backend/runtime entrypoints should consume that published artifact.
- ✓ Publish an initial semantic-product shell through `Semantics::validate` and `CompilePipelineOutput`. Completed: successful semantic validation now emits a deterministic `SemanticProgram` shell with entry path, import lists, and definition/execution inventories, and successful compile-pipeline results publish that shell alongside the raw AST while the lowering-facing fact-family items remain live.
- ✓ Thread semantic-product success results through `primec` compile/emit entrypoints, `primevm`, backend registry/dispatch glue, and the shared runtime/backend handoff path. Completed: successful compile-pipeline results now carry the semantic product through both CLI consumers and into `prepareIrModule(...)` / `IrLowerer::lower(...)`, so shared IR preparation no longer loses the published lowering artifact on production success paths.
- ✓ Materialize resolved direct-call targets and canonical callee paths into the semantic product. Completed: successful semantic validation now publishes a deterministic `directCallTargets` inventory on `SemanticProgram`, covering non-method call sites with scope path, source location, and resolved canonical callee path drawn from the existing semantics resolution machinery.
- ✓ Materialize resolved receiver/method-call targets and receiver-side helper-family choices into the semantic product. Completed: successful semantic validation now publishes a deterministic `methodCallTargets` inventory on `SemanticProgram`, covering method-call sites with scope path, receiver binding text, source location, and the resolved receiver-side target path chosen by the existing method resolution machinery.
- ✓ Materialize helper-vs-canonical path choices for collection/builtin bridge surfaces into the semantic product. Completed: successful semantic validation now publishes a deterministic `bridgePathChoices` inventory on `SemanticProgram`, covering direct collection/builtin bridge call sites with scope path, source location, collection family, helper name, and the exact chosen same-path/canonical helper path that semantics settled on.
- ✓ Thread semantic-product success results through `primec` dump-stage selection/emission and CLI help text. Completed: `primec` and `primevm` now expose a real `semantic-product` dump stage, compile-pipeline dump selection routes that stage through the published `SemanticProgram`, and both CLI help surfaces document the lowering-facing stage between `ast-semantic` and `ir`.
- ✓ Thread semantic-product-aware dump/report plumbing through the compile pipeline without duplicating semantic state. Completed: semantic-product dump emission now formats `CompilePipelineOutput.semanticProgram` directly instead of constructing any parallel semantic-report object.
- ✓ Thread semantic-product-aware failure objects through compile-pipeline result plumbing. Completed: `CompilePipelineOutput` now preserves a first-class failure object with stage/message/diagnostic payload on post-semantics failures, and the semantic product remains published even when later compile-pipeline validation rejects the target.
- ✓ Thread semantic-product-aware user-visible diagnostic ordering/reporting through the remaining CLI/runtime consumers. Completed: `primec` and `primevm` now report compile-pipeline failures directly from `CompilePipelineOutput.failure`, and post-semantics diagnostics explicitly note when a semantic product is already available.
- ✓ Implement the deterministic semantic-product dump stage and text formatter. Completed: `SemanticProduct.cpp` now provides a deterministic text formatter over semantic-product fact inventories, and compile-pipeline dump routing plus focused snapshot/dump tests cover that lowering-facing inspection surface.
- ✓ Add golden coverage for the semantic-product dump surface. Completed: the semantic-product formatter now has an exact text golden that pins the full lowering-facing dump layout and field spelling, while the existing `primec`/`primevm` dump-stage tests continue to lock the runtime dump entrypoints and alias behavior against that formatter surface.
- ✓ Materialize final binding/result type facts for parameters, locals, temporaries, and returns into the semantic product. Completed: successful semantic validation now publishes deterministic `bindingFacts` and `returnFacts` inventories on `SemanticProgram`, covering parameters, locals, temporary call results, and return/result summaries with scope path, source location, binding name where applicable, and resolved binding/result type text.
- ✓ Materialize graph-backed local `auto` facts into the semantic product. Completed: successful semantic validation now publishes deterministic `localAutoFacts` entries on `SemanticProgram`, covering graph-backed local `auto` bindings with scope path, source location, binding name, and resolved type text.
- ✓ Materialize graph-backed query metadata into the semantic product. Completed: successful semantic validation now publishes deterministic `queryFacts` entries on `SemanticProgram`, covering query-shaped call sites with scope path, source location, resolved query kind/type text, result kind text, result type text, and any discovered receiver binding text.
- ✓ Materialize graph-backed `try(...)` metadata into the semantic product. Completed: successful semantic validation now publishes deterministic `tryFacts` entries on `SemanticProgram`, covering `try(...)` sites with scope path, source location, the queried binding text, result kind text, and resolved success/error type text.
- ✓ Materialize graph-backed `on_error` metadata into the semantic product. Completed: successful semantic validation now publishes deterministic `onErrorFacts` entries on `SemanticProgram`, covering `on_error`-annotated definitions with scope path, source location, result kind text, and resolved success/error type text.
- ✓ Document the CLI/runtime semantic-product handoff contract before implementing the end-to-end plumbing cutover. Completed: `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now defines how `primec`, `primevm`, dump-stage handling, failure/report paths, and backend/runtime consumers should receive and treat the published semantic-product artifact without creating parallel semantic state.
- ✓ Add a staged migration note in `docs/PrimeStruct.md` for the semantics-to-lowering boundary, including exit criteria for removing AST-dependent lowerer logic. Completed: `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now documents the planned semantic-product boundary, its staged cutover path, and the explicit exit criteria for deleting AST-dependent lowerer re-derivations.
- ✓ Document the planned `prepareIrModule` / `IrLowerer::lower` entrypoint cutover before implementing it. Completed: `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now defines the production lowerer entry contract, the semantic-product versus AST ownership boundary at that handoff, the temporary adapter role, and the completion criteria for retiring the raw-`Program` lowering path.
- ✓ Document the planned lowerer type/binding handoff before implementing that cutover. Completed: `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now defines the semantic-product contract for lowerer binding/result types, binding/storage classification, helper-family routing facts, provenance requirements, and completion criteria, so the live TODO can focus only on implementing the lowerer type/binding setup cutover.
- ✓ Document the planned lowerer effect/capability and struct-layout handoff before implementing that cutover. Completed: `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now defines the semantic-product contract for lowerer effect summaries, capability metadata, struct/layout facts, canonical layout classifications, provenance requirements, and completion criteria, so the live TODO can focus only on implementing the lowerer effect/capability and struct-layout setup cutover.
- ✓ Implement the lowerer effect/capability and struct-layout setup cutover. Completed: entry and lowered-callable effect/capability setup now consume semantic-product callable summaries, lowerer import/layout setup now consumes semantic-product type metadata plus ordered struct field name/envelope/type metadata, and the remaining field-level qualifiers are now explicitly pinned as syntax-owned (`public/private`, `static`, `pod/handle/gpu_lane`, field-local alignment) rather than left as an open cutover seam.
- ✓ Implement the first semantic-product builder slice. Completed: the published semantic product now includes resolved call targets, helper-vs-canonical bridge-path choices, final binding/result facts, effect/capability summaries, and struct/type metadata needed by lowering.
- ✓ Implement the second semantic-product builder slice. Completed: graph-backed local `auto`, query, `try(...)`, and `on_error` facts are now published on the semantic product in deterministic order.
- ✓ Implement the CLI/runtime plumbing cutover for the semantic product. Completed: `primec`, `primevm`, dump stages, runtime/backend handoff, and failure/report plumbing now thread the published semantic product and semantic-product-aware failure objects without constructing parallel semantic state.
- ✓ Implement the temporary migration adapter. Completed: lowerer entry/setup now prefers semantic-product resolved targets, source-owned binding/result facts, callable summaries, and current layout/type metadata through the temporary adapter; the remaining live work is now only entrypoint retirement and later cleanup/removal passes.
- ✓ Implement the deterministic semantic-product dump/formatter plus golden coverage. Completed: `semantic-product` is now a real dump stage for `primec` and `primevm`, backed by a deterministic formatter with exact golden coverage.

**Todo Cleanup (April 2, 2026)**

**Types & Semantics**
**Group 8 - SoA de-builtinization**
- ✓ Extend experimental wrapper field-view semantics to richer mutating field-view write surfaces beyond the remaining standalone method/call pending-diagnostic contract. Completed: richer borrowed helper-return, method-like struct-helper return, and inline location-wrapped borrowed or method-like indexed writes such as `assign(dereference(pickBorrowed(...)).field()[i], next)`, `assign(holder.pickBorrowed(...).field()[i], next)`, and `assign(field(location(holder.pickBorrowed(...)))[i], next)` now rewrite and lower through the existing `soaVectorRef<T>(..., i).field` substrate across C++/native/VM, leaving only standalone method/call writes on the deterministic pending-diagnostic contract.
- ✓ Stabilize nested struct-body experimental `SoaVector<T>` helper returns beyond the current constructor-light foothold. Completed: global helper-return and explicit `/Type/helper` method-like `SoaVector<T>` returns already cleared the working helper/conversion substrate when they materialized values through `soaVectorSingle<T>(T(...))`, and nested struct-body helper returns now match that surface too: constructor-bearing helper bodies clear semantic validation, unused-module compilation, direct and bound default helper/conversion expressions, and same-path `/soa_vector/*` plus `/to_aos` method-shadow compile-run paths instead of failing during specialization or later expression lowering.
- ✓ Fix the native backend/runtime path for bridged/imported builtin bare/direct/method/slash-method `to_aos` forms so they stop trapping after canonical lowering. Completed: builtin `soa_vector<T>` bare/direct/method/slash-method `to_aos` calls now bridge through the canonical `/std/collections/soa_vector/to_aos` shim without trapping on native once lowering materializes the experimental `SoaVector<T>` storage explicitly instead of generic-copying mismatched struct slots.
- ✓ Delete the remaining runtime-code conversion `soa_vector` special cases once `to_aos` routing is fully stdlib-owned. Completed: direct-canonical `/std/collections/soa_vector/to_aos<T>(...)` calls plus imported-helper `to_aos` routing no longer rely on dedicated runtime-side conversion branches, stale post-semantics bare `to_aos(...)` target classification is gone from the shared emitter/lowerer path too, and root/imported builtin bare/direct/method/slash-method `to_aos` forms now bridge through the canonical stdlib shim during lowering and run across C++/native/VM.
- ✓ Delete the remaining mutating builtin `soa_vector` assign-target indexed field-view pending path in `SemanticsValidatorExprMutationBorrows.cpp`. Completed: indexed field-view writes such as `assign(values.y()[i], next)` and explicit borrowed-slot writes such as `assign(ref(values, i).field, next)` now leave the pending-only branch and lower through the experimental `soaVectorRef<T>(..., i).field` substrate across C++/native/VM.
- ✓ Delete the remaining diagnostic/test indexed mutating field-view `soa_vector` special cases. Completed: focused semantics, dump, and compile-run coverage now pin the successful indexed/ref-field write path instead of only the earlier pending-diagnostic branch.
- ✓ Delete the remaining mutating builtin `soa_vector` assign-target method/call field-view pending path in `SemanticsValidatorExprMutationBorrows.cpp`. Completed: standalone assign-target method/call field-view writes such as `assign(values.x(), next)` and `assign(x(values), next)` now reach the shared pending field-view diagnostic through ordinary field-view validation instead of a dedicated MutationBorrows helper.
- ✓ Delete the remaining diagnostic/test method/call mutating field-view `soa_vector` special cases. Completed: the source-lock now pins that standalone mutating field-view coverage to ordinary field-view validation instead of the older `extractExperimentalSoaAssignFieldViewName(...)` helper branch.

- ✓ Collection runtime-contract slice: align builtin `reserve` receiver-shape diagnostics with the fixed-size `array<T>` vs dynamic `vector<T>` contract while canonical helper routing is still mixed-mode. Completed via semantics coverage for call/method `array<T>` rejects, IR-validation coverage for non-vector receiver rejects, and shared compile-run compile-reject coverage across VM/native/exe for the same `reserve requires vector binding` contract.
- ✓ Collection runtime-contract slice: enforce deterministic numeric-argument validation for vector mutators while canonical helper routing is still mixed-mode. Completed via semantics coverage for boolean `reserve` / `remove_at` / `remove_swap` rejects in call and method forms, IR-validation coverage for `reserve` / `remove_at` / `remove_swap` non-integer argument rejects, and shared compile-run compile-reject coverage across VM/native/exe for the same boolean mutator cases.
- ✓ Collection runtime-contract slice: stabilize named-argument diagnostic ordering and receiver probing for builtin mutator calls while canonical helper routing is still mixed-mode. Completed via semantics coverage for expression-context named-argument mutator calls staying statement-only plus statement-context array receiver rejects, and shared compile-run compile-reject coverage across VM/native/exe for the same statement-only and `requires vector binding` ordering contracts.
- ✓ Dynamic vector-runtime substrate slice: emit deterministic compile-time overflow/limit diagnostics for `reserve` expressions. Completed via VM/native compile-run coverage plus IR-validation coverage for folded signed/unsigned `plus` / `minus` / `negate` literal expressions producing deterministic non-negative, overflow, and local-capacity-limit diagnostics.
- ✓ Dynamic vector-runtime substrate slice: replace `push` growth overflow abort behavior with deterministic allocation-failure runtime semantics. Completed via IR-validation coverage for emitted `HeapAlloc` plus allocation-failure checks, runtime-helper coverage for the canonical out-of-memory diagnostic, and VM/native compile-run coverage asserting the exact `vector push allocation failed (out of memory)` failure.
- ✓ Dynamic vector-runtime substrate slice: replace `reserve` growth overflow abort behavior with deterministic allocation-failure runtime semantics. Completed via IR-validation coverage for emitted `HeapAlloc` plus allocation-failure checks, runtime-helper coverage for the canonical out-of-memory diagnostic, and VM/native compile-run coverage asserting the exact `vector reserve allocation failed (out of memory)` failure.
- ✓ Dynamic vector heap-backing substrate slice: route VM/native vector literal `data_ptr` storage to runtime heap allocation instead of frame-local slots. Completed via IR-validation coverage for vector record headers emitting heap-backed `data_ptr` storage, plus VM/native compile-run coverage executing numeric vector literals through that heap-backed layout.
- ✓ Dynamic vector heap-backing substrate slice: route `reserve` growth reallocation paths through runtime heap allocation instead of frame-local max-capacity slots. Completed via IR-validation coverage for `reserve` helper emission using heap allocation, plus VM/native compile-run coverage proving values survive reserve-triggered growth through the heap-backed reallocation path.
- ✓ Dynamic vector heap-backing substrate slice: route `push` growth copy/reallocation paths through runtime heap allocation instead of frame-local max-capacity slots. Completed via IR-validation coverage for `push` helper emission using heap allocation, plus VM/native compile-run coverage proving values survive push-triggered growth through the heap-backed copy/reallocation path.

**Group 10B - Test translation unit migration**
Test TU migration note: Group 10A finished the oversized test/support splits. The next maintainability step is to eliminate include-only test bodies by moving test suites into standalone `.cpp` translation units while keeping each resulting `.cpp` at a reasonable size so host compiler memory use and parse time stay bounded.
Test TU sizing note: prefer many small/medium `.cpp` files over recreating giant aggregate translation units. When a suite is still too large after the first move out of `.h`, split it into multiple themed `.cpp` files that share only narrow helper headers.
- ✓ Migrate the remaining `tests/unit/test_compile_run.cpp` include-driven suites into standalone `.cpp` translation units, starting with the largest headers first and keeping each new translation unit comfortably below the old `700`-line oversized-file target where practical.
- ✓ Migrate the remaining `tests/unit/test_semantics.cpp` include-driven suites into standalone `.cpp` translation units, using `tests/unit/test_semantics_helpers.h` only for shared parser/validator setup and not as a new place to hide test bodies.

**Todo Cleanup (March 28, 2026)**

**Types & Semantics**
**Group 1 - Roadmap framing**
- ✓ Field-bound experimental map wrapper helpers accept struct field receivers. Template monomorph binding inference now reuses the existing field-binding resolver for `receiver.field` expressions, so wrapper helpers such as `/std/collections/mapCount(...)` and `/std/collections/mapAt(...)` infer `Map<K, V>` receiver template arguments from struct fields.
- ✓ Direct inferred experimental-map struct fields accept canonical constructor routing. Re-enabled the stale `stdlib map constructors accept inferred experimental map struct fields` semantics coverage in `tests/unit/test_semantics_calls_and_flow_collections.h`; the current frontend already rewrites `Holder(/std/collections/map/map(...))` plus subsequent `/std/collections/mapPair(...)` assignment onto inferred experimental `Map<K, V>` struct fields without additional compiler changes.

**Group 2 - Hybrid surfaces**
Hybrid-surface note: do not treat `Result`, `File`, `Buffer`, or `/std/gfx/*` follow-up work as candidates for full de-builtinization in the same sense as `vector`/`map`; the target there is a smaller builtin substrate with stdlib-owned public surface, not zero compiler/runtime knowledge.
Hybrid-surface archive note: completed `Result`, `File`, `Buffer`, and gfx parity/cleanup slices now live in `docs/todo_finished.md`. Keep this section focused on checkpoint notes plus any genuinely unfinished hybrid-surface work.
File facade checkpoint: the public `File<Mode>` helper surface is now primarily stdlib-owned. `/std/file/*` provides `.prime` wrappers for constructor-shaped opens plus `read_byte`, `write`, `write_line`, `write_byte`, `write_bytes`, `flush`, and `close`; broader multi-value `write(...)` / `write_line(...)` calls above the zero-to-nine stdlib wrapper ladder now fall back to builtin `/file/*` variadics instead of requiring further fixed-arity wrapper growth. Keep any new `File` follow-up work split into concrete parity or cleanup slices instead of reopening one umbrella item.
Buffer/gfx facade checkpoint: canonical and experimental gfx packages now expose `.prime`-authored `Buffer<T>` inspection/allocation/storage helpers plus `.prime` helper layers for the canonical `Window(...)`, `Device()`, swapchain, mesh, pipeline, frame, render-pass, submit, and present entrypoints. Keep any new `Buffer<T>` or `/std/gfx/*` follow-up work split into concrete parity, runtime, or cleanup slices instead of reopening one umbrella item.
Hybrid-surface follow-up note: the `Result` work plus the `File` / `Buffer` checkpoints above are already too broad to extend directly. Land any remaining work through concrete slices instead of reopening those broader buckets.


**Group 3 - Arg-pack runtime materialization**
Arg-pack status note: the current runtime-materialization queue is clear. Completed parser/body-API work, the finished IR element-kind slices, and the now-finished parity/diagnostic checkpoints below are archived in `docs/todo_finished.md`. Add new Group 3 items only for newly reproduced unsupported kinds or fresh runtime-materialization regressions.
Legacy backend arg-pack checkpoint: the C++ emitter already materializes trailing concrete `[args<T>]` parameters as `std::vector<T>` values, supports direct variadic calls plus mixed explicit-prefix + final-spread forwarding, and executes the read-only `count` / `at` / `at_unsafe` / indexing body API including downstream method resolution on indexed values.
IR-backed arg-pack checkpoint: IR/VM/native now cover concrete value packs across direct, pure-spread, and mixed forwarding for numeric, bool, string, struct, `Result`, `FileError`, `File<Mode>`, `Buffer<T>`, `array`, `vector`, `soa_vector`, `map`, and many `Reference<T>` / `Pointer<T>` forms, including indexed downstream helper/method access for the already-landed kinds.
Arg-pack completion note: do not keep another catch-all "remaining non-string element kinds" umbrella once only scattered gaps remain. Add new arg-pack TODOs only for concrete unsupported element kinds backed by a reproducible semantics/lowering/backend failure.
Detailed finished arg-pack IR element-kind checkpoints were moved to `docs/todo_finished.md`. Keep this file focused on the active materialization slices plus any truly remaining unsupported kinds.


**Group 5 - Container bring-up and de-builtinization**
- ✓ Experimental stdlib `vector` growth helper slice: add `/std/collections/experimental_vector/vectorReserve` and `vectorPush` parity on the pointer-backed `.prime` storage path. Completed: experimental vector now grows via stdlib-owned allocate-and-move logic built on `take(...)` / `init(...)` instead of raw buffer reallocation, shared C++/VM/native conformance covers `vectorReserve`, `vectorPush`, and element preservation across non-trivial reserve-then-grow sequences, and both `vectorReserve` capacity overflow plus `vectorPush` growth-capacity overflow now route through shared deterministic runtime-contract paths instead of falling through to unchecked allocation growth.

**Architecture / Type-resolution graph**
**Group 13 - Rollout constraints**
Type-resolution graph rollout note: keep the graph migration blocked on the remaining collection-helper/runtime predecessor items so solver work does not target moving call-target semantics. Near-term graph work should stop at a return-kind pilot plus parity; query, CT-eval, parallelization, and broad inference migration stay deferred until that path is stable.
Staged graph rollout note: the graph builder, deterministic dump, return-kind pilot, default-on flip, and temporary rollback path are complete. Remaining graph work should build directly on the single default graph resolver; do not reintroduce a second resolver mode while expanding template/local inference, query/invalidation work, CT-eval consumers, or optional parallel solve.

**Hybrid Surfaces (March 26, 2026)**
- ✓ Refactor `Result<T, Error>` toward the documented hybrid end-state: keep `?` propagation/minimal layout builtin, but move more constructors/helpers and error-domain behavior into stdlib `.prime`. Progress: `/std/file/*` now provides `.prime`-authored `fileReadEof()` and `fileErrorIsEof(err)` helpers so FileError-domain EOF construction/classification no longer needs raw literals at call sites, the type-owned `FileError.why(err)`, `FileError.status(err)`, `FileError.result<T>(err)`, `FileError.eof()`, and `FileError.is_eof(err)` surface now lives in stdlib `.prime` through `/std/file/FileError/*` instead of depending on ad hoc builtin-only helper resolution, those static plus receiver-style `FileError.status/result` helpers now also resolve in direct nested `Result.error(...)` / `Result.why(...)` expressions, receiver-style `err.why()` / `err.is_eof()` continue to use that same stdlib-owned FileError helper surface, and the old root `/FileError/*` wrappers plus package-level `fileErrorStatus(err)` / `fileErrorResult<T>(err)` aliases are now removed; `/std/image/*` now provides `.prime`-authored `imageReadUnsupported()`, `imageWriteUnsupported()`, `imageInvalidOperation()`, `imageErrorStatus(err)`, and `imageErrorResult<T>(err)` helpers so `ImageError` result construction no longer needs raw code packing at call sites, the public `/ImageError/why([ImageError] err)` wrapper now lives in stdlib `.prime` for explicit type-owned access to the current image error strings, the public `/ImageError/read_unsupported()`, `/ImageError/write_unsupported()`, and `/ImageError/invalid_operation()` wrappers now expose the three current ImageError constructors as explicit type-owned stdlib behavior instead of leaving them only on package-level image helpers, the public `/ImageError/status([ImageError] err)` plus `/ImageError/result<T>([ImageError] err)` wrappers now expose explicit root-level access to the type-owned image result packers while `ImageError.status(err)` / `ImageError.result<T>(err)` own the underlying namespace surface and `imageErrorStatus(err)` / `imageErrorResult<T>(err)` remain compatibility helpers, and receiver-style `err.why()` / `err.status()` / `err.result<T>()` now explicitly prefer that same stdlib-owned ImageError helper surface; `/std/collections/*` now provides `.prime`-authored `containerErrorStatus(err)` alongside `containerErrorResult<T>(err)` so status-only `ContainerError` results no longer need manual `err.code` packing at call sites, the public `/ContainerError/why([ContainerError] err)` wrapper now exposes explicit type-owned access to the stable container error strings, the public `/ContainerError/missing_key()`, `/ContainerError/index_out_of_bounds()`, `/ContainerError/empty()`, and `/ContainerError/capacity_exceeded()` wrappers now expose the four current ContainerError constructors as explicit type-owned stdlib behavior instead of leaving them only on package-level collection helpers, the public `/ContainerError/status([ContainerError] err)` plus `/ContainerError/result<T>([ContainerError] err)` wrappers now expose explicit root-level access to the type-owned container result packers while `ContainerError.status(err)` / `ContainerError.result<T>(err)` own the namespace surface and `containerErrorStatus(err)` / `containerErrorResult<T>(err)` remain compatibility helpers, and receiver-style `err.why()` / `err.status()` / `err.result<T>()` now explicitly prefer that same stdlib-owned ContainerError helper surface; both `/std/gfx/*` plus `/std/gfx/experimental/*` now route status-only and value-carrying `GfxError` results through `GfxError.status(err)`, the public `windowCreateFailed()` / `deviceCreateFailed()` / `swapchainCreateFailed()` / `meshCreateFailed()` / `pipelineCreateFailed()` / `materialCreateFailed()` / `frameAcquireFailed()` / `queueSubmitFailed()` / `framePresentFailed()` wrappers, and receiver-style `err.result<T>()` instead of package-level aliases, the old canonical root `/GfxError/*` wrappers plus `gfxErrorStatus(err)` / `gfxErrorResult<T>(err)` package helpers are removed, `GfxError.why(err)` / `GfxError.status(err)` plus receiver-style `err.why()` / `err.status()` / `err.result<T>()` now explicitly prefer the matching canonical or experimental `GfxError` helper surface instead of depending on incidental generic struct-method lookup. Builtin `Result.map(...)`, `Result.and_then(...)`, and `Result.map2(...)` now preserve nested `Result` metadata through semantics plus the C++ emitter so downstream `Result.error(...)`, `Result.why(...)`, `try(...)`, and `[auto]` bindings can consume direct combinator expressions instead of requiring intermediate explicit `Result<...>` annotations, direct `Result.ok(...)` expressions now participate in that same metadata flow so `Result.error(Result.ok(...))`, `Result.why(Result.ok(...))`, `try(Result.ok(...))`, and `Result.and_then(...)` lambdas that return `Result.ok(...)` can keep the surrounding error domain without depending on unrelated outer-context inference, and IR-backed lowering now also executes `Result.map(...)`, `Result.and_then(...)`, and `Result.map2(...)` lambdas while accepting the current `i32`/`bool`/`f32`/`string` plus ordinary user-struct payload subset on shared combinator paths, including direct `Result.ok(...)` source expressions instead of only local- or definition-backed inputs. Direct `Result.ok(...)` plus downstream `try(...)` now also preserve the current packed `File<Mode>` handles and single-slot int-backed stdlib error-struct payload subset on VM/native paths, and the IR-side result resolver now recognizes direct `Result.map(...)`, `Result.and_then(...)`, and `Result.map2(...)` expressions themselves so VM/native `try(...)`, `Result.error(...)`, and `Result.why(...)` can consume those combinators without intermediate bindings, while preserving already-encoded source errors unchanged and rejecting unsupported wider `Result.ok(...)` plus combinator payloads through the same explicit IR-backed 32-bit-or-string contract. IR auto bindings now also preserve that same `Result` metadata for direct `Result.ok(...)`, `Result.map(...)`, `Result.and_then(...)`, and `Result.map2(...)` initializer expressions, so later `try(...)`, `Result.error(...)`, and `Result.why(...)` consumers on VM/native no longer need explicit `Result<...>` annotations to keep the bound local on the shared result path.
  IR-backed lowering now also resolves simple block-bodied value-carrying `Result.and_then(...)` lambda returns that end in an explicit `return(Result...)`, so VM/native no longer require those lambdas to stay single-expression-only for that supported shape.
  IR inference now also recognizes direct string `try(Result.map(...))`, `try(Result.and_then(...))`, and `try(Result.map2(...))` expressions for downstream string consumers such as `count(...)` and `print_line(...)` on VM/native without requiring an intermediate binding, and the call-base inference helper now resolves those same direct combinators for `try(...)` while reusing the configured inference state for ordinary mapped/chained arithmetic bodies instead of depending on the later dispatch-only path; that same call-base path now also resolves definition-backed and receiver-method `Result` sources inside those combinators, so named helpers such as `greeting()` and method-sugar helpers such as `reader.read()` can feed direct `Result.map(...)`, `Result.and_then(...)`, and `Result.map2(...)` consumers on IR-backed VM/native paths without an intermediate local.
  The internal call-parameter metadata helper now also routes named-helper and receiver-method `Result` combinator initializer expressions through the shared definition/method return-info adapters, keeping lowerer helper behavior aligned with the main `Result` metadata path without re-claiming unsupported user-written parameter-default call syntax.
  The inline-call parameter lowering path now forwards those same definition/method/return-info adapters when it infers caller-scoped parameter-initializer metadata, so internal inline lowering no longer falls back to narrower local-only `Result` inference for named-helper and receiver-method combinator defaults.
- ✓ Six-value `File<Mode>` helper-arity slice: extend `.prime`-authored `/File/write(...)` and `/File/write_line(...)` wrappers through six heterogenous values so imported method/free-call sugar no longer depends on builtin-only routing at that next arity band.
- ✓ Seven-value `File<Mode>` helper-arity slice: extend `.prime`-authored `/File/write(...)` and `/File/write_line(...)` wrappers through seven heterogenous values so imported method/free-call sugar no longer depends on builtin-only routing at that next arity band.
- ✓ Eight-value `File<Mode>` helper-arity slice: extend `.prime`-authored `/File/write(...)` and `/File/write_line(...)` wrappers through eight heterogenous values so imported method/free-call sugar no longer depends on builtin-only routing at that next arity band.
- ✓ Nine-value `File<Mode>` helper-arity slice: extend `.prime`-authored `/File/write(...)` and `/File/write_line(...)` wrappers through nine heterogenous values so imported method/free-call sugar no longer depends on builtin-only routing at that next arity band.
- ✓ File facade arity-cap decision slice: keep the stdlib-owned fixed overload ladder capped at zero-to-nine `write(...)` / `write_line(...)` values for now, and make broader calls fail with an explicit diagnostic until concrete `[args<T>]` runtime support lands.
- ✓ Result IR explicit block-bodied `Result.and_then(...)` lowering slice: VM/native now support simple value-returning lambda bodies that end in an explicit block-bodied `return(Result...)`, while staying within the current explicit packed-payload contract.
- ✓ Result IR final-`if(...)` `Result.and_then(...)` lowering slice: IR-backed value-carrying `Result.and_then(...)` lambdas on VM/native now also accept a final `if(...)` expression whose `then(){...}` / `else(){...}` branches each produce a `Result`, while staying within the current explicit packed-payload contract.
- ✓ Result IR payload-contract expansion slice: broaden `Result.ok(...)` plus combinator payload support beyond the current `i32`/`bool`/`f32`/`string` subset only with matching runtime and diagnostic coverage. VM/native now support generic single-slot user structs and ordinary multi-slot user structs across direct `Result.ok(...)`, `Result.map(...)`, `Result.and_then(...)`, and `Result.map2(...)` flows. Direct IR-backed `Result.ok(...)` plus downstream `try(...)` also preserve packed `File<Mode>` handles, the current single-slot int-backed stdlib error structs (`FileError`, `ImageError`, `ContainerError`, `GfxError`), and `array<T>` / `vector<T>` and `map<K, V>` handles when their element or key/value kinds fit the current collection contract. Combinator payload flows now also cover those same stdlib packed error structs, packed `File<Mode>` handles through explicit typed downstream `try(...)` consumers, plus `array<T>` / `vector<T>` and `map<K, V>` handles. `Buffer<T>` payloads now work on VM/native across direct plus combinator flows, including native executable `try(...)`, `Result.error(...)`, and success/error `Result.why(...)` through explicit typed downstream `try(...)` consumers.
  - ✓ Packed error-struct direct payload slice: accept the current stdlib int-backed single-slot error values through direct IR-backed `Result.ok(...)` plus downstream `try(...)` on VM/native, with runtime coverage and a focused non-packed struct rejection.
  - ✓ Packed error-struct combinator slice: extend IR-backed `Result.map(...)`, `Result.and_then(...)`, and `Result.map2(...)` payload flows to those same stdlib int-backed single-slot error values on VM/native.
  - ✓ Direct single-slot struct payload slice: accept generic single-slot struct values through direct IR-backed `Result.ok(...)` construction plus downstream `try(...)` reconstruction on VM/native, with runtime coverage and a focused multi-slot rejection.
  - ✓ Combinator single-slot struct payload slice: extend IR-backed `Result.map(...)`, `Result.and_then(...)`, and `Result.map2(...)` payload flows to those same generic single-slot struct wrappers with matching runtime and diagnostic coverage.
  - ✓ Packed `File<Mode>` direct payload slice: accept current packed file handles through direct IR-backed `Result.ok(...)` plus downstream `try(...)` on VM/native, with runtime coverage and focused diagnostics that still reject non-packed wide payloads.
  - ✓ Packed `File<Mode>` combinator slice: extend IR-backed `Result.map(...)`, `Result.and_then(...)`, and `Result.map2(...)` payload flows to current packed file handles on VM/native.
  - ✓ Multi-slot struct payload slice: extend the IR-backed payload contract to generic user-defined multi-slot struct values across direct `Result.ok(...)`, `Result.map(...)`, `Result.and_then(...)`, and `Result.map2(...)` flows on VM/native, with runtime coverage and focused diagnostics that still reject unsupported non-struct wider payloads.
  - ✓ Array/vector payload slice: accept `array<T>` / `vector<T>` handle values through IR-backed `Result.ok(...)`, `Result.map(...)`, `Result.and_then(...)`, and `Result.map2(...)` flows on VM/native, with downstream `try(...)` preservation and focused diagnostics that still reject unsupported non-struct wider payloads.
  - ✓ Map payload slice: extend the IR-backed payload contract to `map<K, V>` values across direct plus combinator `Result` flows on VM/native, with runtime coverage and focused diagnostics that still reject unsupported wider payloads.
  - ✓ `Buffer<T>` VM/native-codegen payload slice: extend the IR-backed payload contract to current `Buffer<T>` handles across direct plus combinator `Result` flows on VM plus native codegen, with explicit typed downstream `try(...)` consumers and focused diagnostics that still reject unsupported wider payloads.
  - ✓ `Buffer<T>` native runtime unpack slice: preserve those same direct plus combinator `Result<Buffer<T>, GfxError>` flows through native executable `try(...)`, `Result.error(...)`, and `Result.why(...)` paths.
- ✓ File facade constructor-routing slice: route the common `File<Mode>(path)` open surface through a stdlib-owned wrapper while leaving host I/O substrate builtin and effect-gated. Imported constructor-shaped file opens now rewrite through stdlib `/File/open_read(...)`, `/File/open_write(...)`, and `/File/open_append(...)` helpers while the underlying host file substrate remains builtin/effect-gated.
- ✓ Buffer/gfx facade public-entrypoint slice: move the remaining public `Buffer<T>` and `/std/gfx/*` allocation/lifecycle entry points behind `.prime` wrappers over the intrinsic device/dispatch substrate. Canonical and experimental gfx packages now expose `.prime`-authored `Buffer<T>` inspection/allocation/storage helpers, canonical constructor-shaped `Window(...)` plus `Device()` now route through a private `.prime` `GraphicsSubstrate.createWindow(...)` / `createDevice(...)` / `createQueue(...)` boundary, and the remaining canonical `create_swapchain(...)`, `create_mesh(...)`, `create_pipeline(...)`, `frame()`, `render_pass(...)`, `draw_mesh(...)`, `end()`, `submit(...)`, and `present()` wrappers now also route through that same private helper layer.
  - ✓ Canonical gfx constructor substrate slice: route canonical `Window(...)` and `Device()` entry points through private `.prime` `GraphicsSubstrate` helpers while preserving current deterministic tokens and diagnostics.
  - ✓ Canonical gfx resource/lifecycle substrate slice: route the remaining canonical `create_swapchain(...)`, `create_mesh(...)`, `create_pipeline(...)`, `frame()`, `render_pass(...)`, `draw_mesh(...)`, `end()`, `submit(...)`, and `present()` wrappers through that same private substrate boundary.
- ✓ Hybrid-surface compatibility-cleanup slice: remove remaining builtin-only helper routing and compatibility aliases once the stdlib-owned `Result`/`File`/`Buffer`/gfx surfaces have parity. Progress: canonical root `/GfxError/*` wrappers plus the `gfxErrorStatus(...)` / `gfxErrorResult<T>(...)` package aliases are now removed, so gfx callers use `GfxError.*` and receiver helpers directly, and canonical root `/FileError/*` wrappers plus the package-level `fileErrorStatus(...)` / `fileErrorResult<T>(...)` aliases are now removed so file callers use `FileError.*`, receiver helpers, and the remaining EOF convenience helpers directly.
  - ✓ Canonical root `GfxError` alias cleanup slice: remove `/GfxError/*` compatibility wrappers once `GfxError.*` namespace helpers and receiver/package helpers have parity.
  - ✓ Root `FileError` alias cleanup slice: remove `/FileError/*` compatibility wrappers once callers are migrated to `FileError.*` plus receiver/package helpers.
  - ✓ Package-level error-helper compatibility slice: retire remaining compatibility aliases once direct callers no longer depend on them. Progress: `fileErrorStatus(...)` and `fileErrorResult<T>(...)` are now removed, so file callers use `FileError.*`, receiver helpers, and the remaining `fileReadEof()` / `fileErrorIsEof(...)` convenience helpers directly, and `gfxErrorStatus(...)` / `gfxErrorResult<T>(...)` are now removed so gfx callers use `GfxError.*` plus receiver helpers directly.
    - ✓ `FileError` package-helper cleanup slice: retire `fileErrorStatus(...)` and `fileErrorResult<T>(...)` once direct callers are migrated to `FileError.*` plus receiver helpers.
    - ✓ `GfxError` package-helper cleanup slice: retire `gfxErrorStatus(...)` and `gfxErrorResult<T>(...)` once direct callers are migrated to `GfxError.*` plus receiver helpers.

**Architecture follow-up (March 23, 2026)**
- ✓ Graph parity follow-up: make IR-lowerer return-info inference honor the type-resolution dependency order for grounded mutual recursion so compile-pipeline lowering matches graph-mode semantics instead of falling back to legacy recursive return inference. Progress: IR lowering now precomputes return info over the type-resolution dependency SCCs with deferred unknown-dependency handling, so grounded mutual recursion succeeds through VM IR preparation on the default graph path.
- ✓ Graph rollout follow-up: remove the temporary `--type-resolver=legacy` rollback path once the default graph resolver and IR-lowering parity slices are stable. Progress: CLI/options/compile-pipeline/semantics entrypoints are graph-only now, the validator no longer carries legacy recursive return-inference toggles, and the semantics/IR pipeline coverage now asserts the single default graph path instead of keeping dual-resolver parity scaffolding.
- ✓ Graph SCC follow-up: extract a shared dependency-condensation helper from `TypeResolutionGraph` so semantics and IR lowering consume the same dependency-only SCC order instead of rebuilding it separately. Progress: the graph layer now exposes a shared dependency-DAG helper plus testing API, the semantics solver and IR-lowerer return-info precompute both call that shared helper, and graph-level coverage now pins both mutual-recursion SCC grouping and acyclic dependency ordering through the shared path.
- ✓ Graph return-binding follow-up: cache full inferred return bindings on the graph-solved path so query/direct-call consumers reuse template-rich return metadata instead of reconstructing it from coarse return-kind plus struct-path state. Progress: semantics return solving now persists inferred `BindingInfo` alongside return kinds/structs, direct-call binding resolution consults that cache first, and coverage now pins both auto map-return binding reuse and conflicting collection-template diagnostics.
- ✓ Graph return-metadata follow-up: expose graph-solved return metadata through a semantics testing snapshot and keep binding-cache updates coupled to struct-backed returns so conformance can assert cached bindings directly. Progress: the testing API now exports per-definition graph return kind/struct/binding snapshots, the graph updater no longer skips `returnBindings_` when `returnStructs_` is present, and graph-level coverage now pins inferred experimental-map binding metadata directly.
- ✓ Graph preparation follow-up: route graph-backed testing helpers through one shared canonical preparation pipeline so graph dumps, SCC snapshots, and return snapshots cannot drift on transform/monomorph ordering. Progress: the semantics frontend now has a shared type-resolution preparation helper, both graph construction and return-snapshot helpers call it, and graph-level specialization coverage now pins that the dump and return snapshot see the same monomorphized helper shape.
- ✓ Graph local-binding follow-up: expose graph-solved local `auto` binding metadata through a semantics testing snapshot so conformance can assert broader inference consumers directly instead of only inferring success through downstream validation. Progress: the validator now exports deterministic local-binding snapshots from the graph-local cache, that snapshot also records cached initializer query type text beside the binding metadata, the semantics testing API exposes it beside the return snapshot helpers, and graph-level coverage now pins control-flow collection binding inference directly.
- ✓ Graph query-metadata follow-up: expose graph-solved query call type texts through a semantics testing snapshot so conformance can assert shared query inference directly instead of only through downstream return/local binding effects. Progress: the validator now exports deterministic query-call snapshots with resolved paths plus inferred type texts, the semantics testing API exposes that graph-backed query snapshot beside the return/local helpers, and graph-level coverage now pins canonical map receiver query types directly.
- ✓ Graph call-binding follow-up: expose graph-solved call-site binding metadata through a semantics testing snapshot so conformance can assert cached return-binding reuse directly at helper call sites instead of only through return/local/query side effects. Progress: the validator now exports deterministic call-binding snapshots with resolved callee paths plus inferred binding metadata, the semantics testing API exposes that graph-backed call snapshot beside the other graph snapshots, and graph-level coverage now pins helper-returned experimental-map bindings directly.
- ✓ Graph receiver-binding follow-up: expose graph-solved collection-helper receiver binding metadata through a semantics testing snapshot so conformance can assert method/query receiver inference directly instead of only through call-binding plus query-result side effects. Progress: the validator now exports deterministic receiver-binding snapshots for receiver-style helper calls, the semantics testing API exposes that graph-backed receiver snapshot beside the other graph snapshots, and graph-level coverage now pins wrapped experimental-map receiver bindings directly for canonical `count` and `tryAt` queries.
- ✓ Graph snapshot-traversal follow-up: route graph-backed query snapshot helpers through one shared local-aware call traversal so query-type and receiver-binding conformance cannot drift on local binding flow or validation scope order. Progress: the validator now centralizes the local-aware snapshot call walk in one helper, both query-type and receiver-binding snapshots consume that shared traversal, and graph-level coverage now pins wrapped map receiver inference through a local `auto` binding on the shared path.
- ✓ Graph snapshot-runner follow-up: route graph-backed semantics snapshot helpers through one shared preparation-and-validator runner so return/local/query/call/receiver conformance cannot drift on setup defaults or validator execution order. Progress: `SemanticsValidate.cpp` now centralizes snapshot setup in one helper that owns type-resolution preparation plus validator execution, every graph-backed semantics snapshot uses that shared runner, and graph-level specialization coverage now pins a non-return call-binding snapshot through the same shared path.
- ✓ Graph call-snapshot traversal follow-up: route graph-backed call-binding snapshots through the shared local-aware call traversal so call-binding and query/receiver conformance cannot drift on validation scope or local flow order. Progress: the validator now runs call-binding snapshots through the same local-aware traversal helper as the query snapshots, source-wiring coverage pins that shared helper use, and graph-level call-binding coverage now exercises a helper-returned experimental map flowing through a local `auto` binding on the shared path.
- ✓ Graph query-binding follow-up: expose graph-solved query-result binding metadata through a semantics testing snapshot so conformance can assert downstream initializer/binding consumers directly instead of only through query type text. Progress: the validator now exports deterministic query-binding snapshots with resolved paths plus inferred binding metadata, the semantics testing API exposes that graph-backed query-binding snapshot beside the other graph snapshots, and graph-level coverage now pins canonical map query-result bindings directly.
- ✓ Graph query-result follow-up: expose graph-solved `Result` decomposition metadata through a semantics testing snapshot so conformance can assert `try`/`on_error` consumers directly instead of only through query binding text. Progress: the validator now exports deterministic query-result snapshots with resolved paths plus value/error type metadata, the semantics testing API exposes that graph-backed result snapshot beside the other graph snapshots, and graph-level coverage now pins canonical map `tryAt` result decomposition directly.
- ✓ Graph local-result follow-up: cache graph-solved local `auto` initializer `Result` metadata in the semantics testing snapshot so conformance can assert `try`/`on_error` consumers after local flow instead of only at direct query sites. Progress: graph local-auto capture now caches initializer result decomposition beside binding/query text metadata, the local-binding snapshot exports that result metadata through the semantics testing API, and graph-level coverage now pins a local `tryAt` result flowing through the graph-captured `auto` path.
- ✓ Graph local-call-path follow-up: cache graph-solved local `auto` initializer resolved call paths in the semantics testing snapshot so conformance can assert canonical local initializer resolution directly instead of only through separate query/call snapshot surfaces. Progress: graph local-auto capture now caches initializer resolved paths beside binding/query/result metadata, the local-binding snapshot exports that canonical path through the semantics testing API, and graph-level coverage now pins both empty control-flow local initializers and canonical `/std/collections/map/tryAt` resolution on the local path.
- ✓ Graph local-receiver follow-up: cache graph-solved local `auto` initializer receiver binding metadata in the semantics testing snapshot so conformance can assert receiver-side query inference after local flow instead of only through separate query-receiver snapshot surfaces. Progress: graph local-auto capture now caches initializer receiver bindings beside binding/path/query/result metadata, the local-binding snapshot exports that receiver binding through the semantics testing API, and graph-level coverage now pins `Map<string, i32>` receiver inference on a local `/std/collections/map/tryAt` initializer.
- ✓ Graph local-try follow-up: cache graph-solved local `auto` initializer `try` metadata in the semantics testing snapshot so conformance can assert shared `try` consumers after local flow instead of only through the standalone `try` snapshot. Progress: graph local-auto capture now caches initializer `try` operand path plus value/error/context metadata, the local-binding snapshot exports that shared `try` state through the semantics testing API, and graph-level coverage now pins both a local `try(values.tryAt(...))` binding and a non-`try` control-flow local on the graph path.
- ✓ Graph local-try operand follow-up: normalize graph-solved local `auto` initializer query metadata for `try(...)` wrappers so conformance can assert wrapped receiver/path/result state after local flow without joining the standalone `try` and query snapshots. Progress: graph local-auto capture now analyzes wrapped `try` operands for the generic initializer resolved-path/receiver/query/result caches, the local-binding snapshot reuses those normalized operand details beside the dedicated `try` metadata, and graph-level coverage now pins a local `try(values.tryAt(...))` binding through both surfaces at once.
- ✓ Graph local-try parity follow-up: route graph local `try(...)` capture through the shared `try` snapshot helper so local and standalone `try` conformance cannot drift on wrapper detection, unwrapped result metadata, or cached `on_error` context. Progress: the validator now centralizes `try` snapshot inference in one helper, the standalone `try` snapshot and graph local-auto capture both call it, and graph-level coverage now asserts that a local `try(values.tryAt(...))` binding matches the standalone `try` snapshot metadata exactly.
- ✓ Graph local-query parity follow-up: route graph local initializer query metadata and standalone query snapshots through one shared helper so local/query conformance cannot drift on resolved path, receiver inference, query type text, or `Result` decomposition. Progress: the validator now centralizes query snapshot inference in one helper, the query-call/query-result/query-receiver snapshots and graph local-auto capture all call it, and graph-level coverage now asserts that a local `try(values.tryAt(...))` binding matches the standalone query snapshots across path, type, receiver, and result metadata.
- ✓ Graph local-initializer-binding follow-up: cache graph-solved local initializer binding metadata in the semantics testing snapshot so conformance can assert wrapped query bindings after local flow without joining the standalone query-binding snapshot. Progress: graph local-auto capture now caches initializer `BindingInfo` alongside the other normalized query metadata, the local-binding snapshot exports that initializer binding through the semantics testing API, and graph-level coverage now pins a local `try(values.tryAt(...))` binding against the standalone query-binding snapshot.
- ✓ Graph try-handler-context follow-up: expose cached `on_error` error-type and bound-arg metadata through the graph-backed `try` and local-binding snapshots so conformance can assert the full shared `try` handler context without joining the standalone `on_error` snapshot. Progress: shared `try` snapshot inference now threads cached `on_error` error type and bound-arg count into both standalone and local-flow `try` metadata, the semantics testing API exports those handler-signature fields on both surfaces, and graph-level coverage now pins local `try(values.tryAt(...))` parity against the standalone `try` snapshot across the full handler context.
- ✓ Graph try-operand-binding follow-up: expose graph-fed `try` operand binding metadata through the standalone `try` and local-binding snapshots so conformance can assert the full shared `try` input without joining query-binding snapshots. Progress: shared `try` snapshot inference now captures operand `BindingInfo` beside operand path plus unwrapped value/error metadata, the semantics testing API exports that operand binding on both standalone and local-flow `try` surfaces, and graph-level coverage now pins local `try(values.tryAt(...))` parity against the standalone `try` snapshot across operand binding plus handler context.
- ✓ Graph try-call parity follow-up: route graph-backed `try` operand resolved-path and binding capture through the same shared direct-call helper as the standalone call-binding snapshot so `try` conformance cannot drift on helper-call resolution or cached return-binding reuse. Progress: the validator now centralizes direct-call snapshot inference in one helper, the standalone `try` snapshot uses that shared path for operand resolved-path plus binding metadata, and graph-level coverage now pins `try(makeValue())` against the standalone call-binding snapshot on a non-query helper call.
- ✓ Graph try-query parity follow-up: route graph-backed `try` operand binding and `Result` decomposition through the same shared query helper as the query snapshots so `try`/query conformance cannot drift on operand metadata. Progress: the validator now reuses the shared query snapshot helper inside `try` inference for operand resolved-path plus binding and result metadata, and graph-level coverage now pins `try(makeValue())` against the standalone query-binding and query-result snapshots on a non-query helper call.
- ✓ Graph try-query-context follow-up: expose graph-backed `try` operand query type text and receiver binding through the standalone and local-flow `try` snapshots so conformance can assert the full shared query context consumed by `try`. Progress: shared `try` snapshot inference now threads operand receiver binding and query type text from the query snapshot helper into both standalone and local-flow `try` metadata, the semantics testing API exports those fields on both surfaces, and graph-level coverage now pins `try(values.tryAt(...))` against the standalone query-call and query-receiver snapshots.
- ✓ Graph local-call parity follow-up: route graph local initializer resolved-path and binding capture through the same shared direct-call helper as the standalone call-binding snapshot so non-query helper conformance cannot drift on call resolution or cached return-binding reuse. Progress: the validator now centralizes direct-call snapshot inference in one helper, graph local-auto capture and the standalone call-binding snapshot both use that shared path for initializer resolved-path plus binding metadata, and graph-level coverage now pins a local `id(1i32)` helper call against the standalone call-binding snapshot.
- ✓ Graph query-call parity follow-up: route graph-backed query snapshot resolved-path and binding capture through the same shared direct-call helper as the standalone call-binding snapshot so query/call/local/try conformance cannot drift on helper-call resolution or cached return-binding reuse. Progress: the validator now centralizes direct-call snapshot inference in one helper, the query snapshot uses that shared path for resolved-path plus binding metadata, and graph-level coverage now pins query-binding parity against the standalone call-binding snapshot on a non-query helper call.
- ✓ Graph query-type fallback follow-up: reuse shared direct-call binding metadata as a fallback query type text for non-query helper calls so graph query-call and local initializer conformance do not silently drop plain helper types. Progress: the validator now falls back to binding-derived type text when the shared query helper has a resolved call plus binding but no dedicated query type text, the graph-backed query-call and local initializer snapshots now surface plain helper call types like `id(1i32)`, and graph-level coverage now pins that fallback against the standalone call-binding snapshot.
- ✓ Graph temporary-receiver result follow-up: reuse the shared non-collection receiver-path helper when graph query/result snapshots infer method-call `Result` metadata so temporary helper receivers keep their resolved value/error decomposition instead of collapsing to the factory callee path. Progress: method-call `Result` inference now routes non-collection receivers through the shared receiver-path resolver, graph-backed local/query snapshots now preserve `Result<i32, MyError>` decomposition for temporary receivers like `makeWidget().read()`, and graph-level coverage now pins that metadata against the standalone query and call snapshot surfaces.
- ✓ Graph local-Result.ok follow-up: normalize graph-solved local initializer analysis through `Result.ok(...)` wrappers so conformance can assert wrapped helper metadata after local flow instead of only the envelope constructor surface. Progress: graph local-auto capture now unwraps unary `Result.ok(...)` initializers for the shared call/query analysis path, the local-binding snapshot now preserves wrapped helper metadata like `makeValue()` under `Result.ok(...)`, and graph-level coverage now pins that local-flow metadata against the standalone query and call snapshots.
- ✓ Graph on_error follow-up: expose graph-fed definition `on_error` context through a semantics testing snapshot so conformance can assert the handler metadata consumed by `try` for both explicit `Result` returns and graph-inferred `int` returns. Progress: the validator now exports deterministic `on_error` snapshots with handler path/error/bound-arg metadata plus any explicit `Result` return decomposition, the semantics testing API exposes that snapshot beside the other graph surfaces, and graph-level coverage now pins both `Result<int, ContainerError>` and graph-inferred `i32` `on_error` contexts directly.
- ✓ Graph validation-context follow-up: move `Result`/`on_error` state into the shared definition validation context so graph-backed inference and snapshot helpers reuse the same `currentValidationContext_` as primary definition validation. Progress: build setup now refreshes cached definition validation contexts after parameters/imports are ready, that shared context now carries parsed `Result` and `on_error` metadata, and graph-level local-binding coverage now pins `Result.ok(...)` inference under both explicit `Result` returns and `on_error`-driven `int` returns.
- ✓ Graph validation-context snapshot follow-up: expose shared definition validation-context metadata through a semantics testing snapshot and route graph-backed `on_error` snapshot capture through that cached context so conformance can assert effect/compute/unsafe state without reparsing transforms. Progress: the validator now exports deterministic definition-context snapshots with sorted active effects plus cached compute/unsafe/`Result`/`on_error` metadata, the public semantics testing API exposes that snapshot on the shared graph-prepared runner, and the `on_error` snapshot now reads the cached definition validation context instead of reparsing transforms.
- ✓ Graph try-context follow-up: expose cached enclosing return-kind metadata through the graph-backed `on_error` and validation-context snapshots so conformance can assert the full shared `try` context for graph-inferred `int` handlers instead of joining separate snapshot surfaces by hand. Progress: the validator now threads cached `returnKinds_` into both `on_error` and validation-context snapshots, the public semantics testing API exports that return-kind text beside the existing handler/context metadata, and graph-level coverage now pins both inferred-`i32` and explicit-`Result` `on_error` contexts directly.
- ✓ Graph try-value follow-up: expose graph-fed `try` expression value metadata through a semantics testing snapshot so conformance can assert shared `try` consumers directly instead of only through downstream locals or joined query/on_error snapshots. Progress: the validator now exports deterministic `try` snapshots with operand resolved paths plus unwrapped value/error metadata and the enclosing cached return-kind/`on_error` context, the public semantics testing API exposes that snapshot on the shared graph-prepared runner, and graph-level coverage now pins both inferred-`i32` and explicit-`Result` `try` consumers directly.

**Types & Semantics (March 22, 2026)**
- ✓ Publish and maintain a single doc-visible type ownership matrix (`core` vs `hybrid` vs `stdlib-owned`) across the design docs and TODO roadmap so future de-builtinization slices delete compiler special cases instead of introducing more path-name routing. Completed: `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now carries the canonical matrix and this roadmap note points back to it.
- ✓ Remove the remaining `Maybe(value)` name-based constructor rewrite so `Maybe<T>` is now documented and validated as an explicit stdlib-owned optional surface over `Maybe<T>()`, `some<T>(value)`, and `none<T>()`.
- ✓ Extend the stdlib-authored `File<Mode>` facade to explicit single-value numeric text helpers (`/File/write<Mode, T>(value)` / `/File/write_line<Mode, T>(value)`) so imported `/std/file/*` covers a minimal `.prime`-authored text-write slice without reintroducing compiler-owned routing.
- ✓ Extend the stdlib-authored `File<Mode>` facade from explicit single-value numeric text helpers to single-value string writes once file-write lowering can carry string-valued helper parameters without falling back to builtin-only entry points.
- ✓ Split the remaining multi-value `File<Mode>` facade work into an explicit prerequisite before changing the public helper surface: helper families may now reuse the same public path when their exact parameter counts differ, and template monomorphization rewrites those overloads onto stable internal paths before later validation so stdlib-authored `write(...)` / `write_line(...)` can grow past single-value wrappers without reintroducing compiler-owned routing.
- ✓ After that prerequisite lands, extend the stdlib-authored `File<Mode>` facade from single-value helpers to a zero-to-five-value heterogenous `write(...)` and `write_line(...)` overload family without reintroducing compiler-owned routing. Completed: `/std/file/*` now exposes multi-value `.prime` wrappers for both slash-call and imported method syntax across the common zero-to-five-value arities while still lowering through the intrinsic file builtin path.
- ✓ Extend the stdlib-authored `File<Mode>` facade to explicit slash-call `/File/close<Mode>(...)` once helper lowering can safely disarm the original handle binding instead of only a copied receiver.
- ✓ Route imported `file.close()` receiver syntax through the stdlib-authored `/File/close<Mode>(...)` helper once method-call helper selection can bind that templated wrapper without falling back to builtin-only routing.
- ✓ Extend the stdlib-authored `Buffer<T>` facade from slash-call `Buffer/allocate<T>(count)` to the final preferred constructor-shaped allocation surface once canonical and experimental gfx packages settle whether that should be `Buffer<T>(count)` or another type-owned form without reintroducing compiler-owned public routing. Completed: imported canonical and experimental `Buffer<T>(count)` expressions now rewrite onto the matching stdlib allocation helpers while labeled raw `Buffer<T>([token] ..., [elementCount] ...)` construction stays direct struct initialization.
- ✓ Extend the stdlib-authored `Buffer<T>` facade to compute-only storage access helpers once generic `.prime` wrappers can express `buffer_load(...)` / `buffer_store(...)` without falling back to compiler-owned public entry points.
- ✓ Ownership/drop resize/remove substrate slice: implement removed-element destruction plus survivor compaction/swap semantics for non-trivial vector elements once the pointer-backed uninitialized-slot and move/copy substrate is ready. Completed: experimental `Vector<T>` now stores `Pointer<uninitialized<T>>` slots in `.prime`, explicitly `drop(...)`s removed/cleared elements, compacts `remove_at(...)` survivors with `take(...)` / `init(...)`, and swaps `remove_swap(...)` survivors through that same ownership-preserving path instead of rejecting non-trivial element types behind builtin vector gates.
- ✓ Experimental stdlib `vector` variadic constructor slice: replace `vectorSingle|Pair|Triple|...` helper ladders in `/std/collections/experimental_vector/*` with one variadic `.prime` constructor built on trailing `[args<T>]` parameters. Completed: `/std/collections/experimental_vector/vector<T>(values...)` now materializes pointer-backed `Vector<T>` values directly from arg packs, and the fixed-arity compatibility helpers forward to that variadic constructor without depending on builtin vector ownership-validation probes for non-trivial element types.
- ✓ Experimental stdlib `vector` per-slot ownership/drop substrate slice: implement true element destruction, overwrite cleanup, and safe buffer lifetime for pointer-backed experimental vector storage once uninitialized slot and move/copy substrate work is available. Completed: experimental `Vector<T>` now owns `Pointer<uninitialized<T>>` backing storage through `.prime` `Move`/`Destroy` hooks, explicitly drops removed/cleared elements, and moves survivors between slots/buffers through `take(...)` / `init(...)` so non-trivial element lifetimes no longer depend on compiler-owned vector helper gates.
- ✓ Experimental stdlib `map` variadic constructor slice: replace `mapSingle|Double|Triple|...` helper ladders in `/std/collections/experimental_map/*` with `entry(key, value)` plus one variadic `.prime` `map(entries...)` constructor built on trailing `[args<Entry<K, V>>]` parameters. Completed: `/std/collections/experimental_map/*` now exposes public `Entry<K, V>`, `entry(key, value)`, and `map<K, V>(entries...)` helpers, and the fixed-arity compatibility constructors forward to that variadic constructor so experimental map construction no longer needs duplicated helper ladders.
- ✓ Experimental stdlib `map` ownership/drop substrate slice: implement key/value destruction, overwrite cleanup, and safe storage lifetime for `.prime` `Map<K, V>` once the vector per-slot ownership/drop and non-trivial reallocation substrate is ready, so experimental map storage no longer hides blocked ownership work inside the helper/runtime parity item above. Completed: experimental `Map<K, V>` now stores keys/payloads in parallel experimental `Vector<K>` / `Vector<V>` records, inherits non-trivial growth through that pointer-backed `take(...)` / `init(...)` vector path, and overwrites existing values by explicitly `drop(...)`ing then `init(...)`ing the payload slot so shared semantics + C++/VM/native coverage now pins overwrite cleanup plus scope-exit destruction for non-trivial map values.
- ✓ Replace wrapper-backed canonical `/std/collections/vector/*` helpers with the real experimental `.prime` vector implementation once vector parity and ownership substrate are ready. Completed: canonical `/std/collections/vector/*` plus wrapper-layer `/std/collections/vector*` helper calls now rewrite onto `/std/collections/experimental_vector/*` for experimental `Vector<T>` value receivers, including count/capacity/access and mutating growth/remove flows, and shared semantics + C++/VM/native coverage now pins those explicit helper plus method-call routes while preserving the existing import requirement for canonical helper names.
- ✓ Canonical `vector` positional-constructor migration slice: route positional canonical `/std/collections/vector/vector<T>(...)` calls through the real experimental `.prime` `Vector<T>` variadic constructor while preserving current import-gated canonical helper routing on inferred `auto`/temporary receivers. Completed: imported positional `/std/collections/vector/vector<T>(...)` now prefers a real canonical variadic wrapper that forwards to `/std/collections/experimental_vector/vector<T>(...)`, semantics/monomorph/lowering return-kind inference now recognizes that experimental `Vector<T>` return surface for auto bindings and temporary helper/method receivers, and shared semantics + C++/VM/native coverage now pins the positional canonical constructor path on `auto` bindings plus direct temporary helper/method calls.
