- ✓ Canonical `vector` named-constructor migration slice: route named-argument canonical `/std/collections/vector/vector(...)` calls through real fixed-arity canonical overloads that forward to the experimental `.prime` `Vector<T>` implementation for inferred and temporary receivers. Completed: imported named-argument canonical constructor calls now resolve through same-path `/std/collections/vector/vector` overloads instead of helper-path rewrites, those overloads forward to `/std/collections/experimental_vector/*`, and shared semantics + C++/VM/native coverage now pins named-argument `auto` bindings, temporary helper/method receivers, and explicit builtin `vector<T>` mismatch rejects on that direct canonical path.
- ✓ Canonical `vector` namespaced declaration-surface migration slice: move the canonical value-receiver `/std/collections/vector/*` declarations onto the real experimental `.prime` `Vector<T>` implementation. Completed: the imported canonical `/std/collections/vector/count|capacity|at|at_unsafe|push|pop|reserve|clear|remove_at|remove_swap` wrappers now declare `Vector<T>` receivers directly instead of builtin `vector<T>`, and shared semantics + C++/VM/native coverage now pins explicit `Vector<T>` bindings on that canonical namespaced helper surface.
- ✓ Canonical `vector` wrapper helper receiver-surface slice: route imported `vectorCount`/`vectorCapacity`/`vectorAt*`/`vectorPush`/`vectorPop`/`vectorReserve`/`vectorClear`/`vectorRemove*` helpers through one real wrapper definition surface that accepts explicit experimental `Vector<T>` receivers while preserving builtin `vector<T>` behavior. Completed: `stdlib/std/collections/collections.prime` now forwards those wrapper helpers through the canonical `/std/collections/vector/*` path with inferred receivers instead of hard-coding builtin `vector<T>` parameter types, and shared semantics + C++/VM/native coverage now pins explicit `Vector<T>` bindings plus mismatch rejects on the wrapper helper surface.
- ✓ Canonical `vector` wrapper constructor explicit experimental-destination slice: route imported `vectorNew`/`vectorSingle`/`vectorPair`/`vectorTriple|...` helper constructors onto the real experimental `.prime` `Vector<T>` implementation whenever an explicit experimental destination already pins that target type. Completed: template monomorphization now rewrites those wrapper constructor aliases onto `/std/collections/experimental_vector/*` for explicit `[Vector<T>]` bindings, explicit `return<Vector<T>>` definitions, explicit `Vector<T>` parameters/struct fields, and explicit assignment/init targets, and shared semantics + C++/VM/native coverage now pins direct plus helper-wrapped constructor flows together with mismatch rejects.
- ✓ Canonical `vector` wrapper constructor inferred `auto`/`return<auto>` slice: extend the same wrapper-constructor cutover beyond explicit experimental destinations to inferred `[auto]` locals and `return<auto>` flows without breaking builtin `vector<T>` compatibility on the wrapper layer. Completed: template monomorph binding and fallback type inference now treat imported `vectorNew`/`vectorSingle`/`vectorPair`/`vectorTriple|...` aliases as experimental `Vector<T>` constructors on inferred `[auto]` locals and `return<auto>` definitions when `/std/collections/experimental_vector/*` is available, and shared semantics + C++/VM/native coverage now pins direct and helper-wrapped auto inference plus template-conflict diagnostics.
- ✓ Canonical `vector` wrapper constructor direct receiver slice: extend the same wrapper-constructor cutover to direct helper-call and method-call receiver expressions without breaking builtin `vector<T>` compatibility on the wrapper layer. Completed: canonical `/std/collections/vector/*` plus wrapper-layer `vector*` helper receivers and direct method-call receivers built from imported `vectorNew`/`vectorSingle`/`vectorPair`/`vectorTriple|...` constructor aliases now rewrite those nested constructor expressions onto `/std/collections/experimental_vector/*` before helper/method resolution, and shared semantics + C++/VM/native coverage now pins direct plus helper-wrapped receiver flows together with template-conflict diagnostics.
- ✓ Remove the remaining no-import builtin fallback for canonical `/std/collections/vector/*` mutator helper spellings once the imported stdlib wrapper path is stable. Completed: canonical `/std/collections/vector/push(...)`, `/std/collections/vector/pop(...)`, `/std/collections/vector/reserve(...)`, `/std/collections/vector/clear(...)`, `/std/collections/vector/remove_at(...)`, and `/std/collections/vector/remove_swap(...)` now require either imported stdlib wrappers or explicit helper definitions in direct-call form, explicit canonical method forms `values./std/collections/vector/push|pop|reserve|clear|remove_at|remove_swap(...)` now deterministically reject with same-path `unknown method` diagnostics when no imported or declared canonical helper exists, imported canonical wrappers accept ordinary named arguments in statement position, and shared semantics + C++ coverage pins the direct-call and method-form reject contracts.

**Maintainability (March 22, 2026)**
- ✓ Split `primec_lib` into smaller subsystem libraries once the semantics and lowering interfaces are explicit. Completed: the build graph now separates shared utilities, frontend passes, and backend/runtime code into explicit subsystem targets; `primec` links the narrower `primec_ir_lib` + `primec_backend_registry_lib` pair directly; doctest binaries link `primec_ir_lib`, `primec_frontend_lib`, or that same narrower backend pair directly; and `primec_lib` remains only as a compatibility umbrella target.
- ✓ Split `primec_backend_lib` further into narrower IR/emitter/runtime subsystem libraries once the remaining transitive dependencies are reduced. Completed: backend-facing implementation now stays on explicit `primec_ir_lib`, `primec_backend_registry_lib`, `primec_backend_emitters_lib`, and `primec_runtime_lib` targets; `primec` and `PrimeStruct_backend_tests` link the narrower IR + registry pair directly; and `primec_backend_lib` remains only as a compatibility umbrella target.
- ✓ Reduce the remaining transitive coupling between `primec_ir_lib` and `primec_codegen_lib` so backend registry/pipeline glue depends on narrower interfaces instead of broad codegen targets. Completed: backend registry glue now links the dedicated `primec_backend_emitters_lib` implementation target instead of the broad `primec_codegen_lib` compatibility umbrella, while `primec_codegen_lib` remains available only as the compatibility umbrella over that narrower emitter implementation target.
- ✓ Continue shrinking backend-registry dependencies so compile-pipeline callers can depend on registry interfaces without pulling unrelated emitter/runtime implementation libraries. Completed: shared VM backend diagnostics now live on the IR side, `primevm` links `primec_ir_lib` + `primec_runtime_lib` directly instead of depending on the backend registry/codegen path, compile-pipeline-driven semantics suites run from a dedicated `primec_ir_lib`-linked doctest binary, and `primec_backend_registry_lib` now keeps emitter/runtime implementation targets private so callers only depend on the registry interface on their direct link surface.
- ✓ Refactor `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorPasses.cpp` (`2737` lines) below the `700`-line target. Completed: extracted effects/capabilities resolution helpers into `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorPassesEffects.cpp`, definition/execution intra-body diagnostic collection helpers into `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorPassesDiagnostics.cpp`, moved definition validation orchestration into `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorPassesDefinitions.cpp`, moved effect-free summary/expression/statement validation into `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorPassesEffectFree.cpp`, moved execution validation orchestration into `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorPassesExecutions.cpp`, moved omitted-initializer plus zero-arg-constructor validation into `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorPassesOmittedInitializers.cpp`, moved uninitialized-storage definite-state validation into `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorPassesUninitialized.cpp`, and moved struct-layout validation into `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorPassesStructLayouts.cpp`, leaving the original file focused on the small remaining orchestration/lookup helpers.
- ✓ Refactor `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorStatement.cpp` (`1515` lines) below the `700`-line target. Completed: extracted vector helper statement validation into `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorStatementVectorHelpers.cpp`, extracted container drop/relocation helper validation into `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorStatementContainerHelpers.cpp`, extracted string/printability statement helpers into `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorStatementPrintability.cpp`, extracted return-statement validation into `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorStatementReturns.cpp`, extracted loop/conditional statement validation into `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorStatementControlFlow.cpp`, extracted binding initialization and borrow-validation statement checks into `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorStatementBindings.cpp`, extracted path-space/compute/buffer builtin statement validation into `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorStatementBuiltins.cpp`, and extracted body-argument target remapping plus block validation into `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorStatementBodyArguments.cpp`, leaving the original file focused on top-level statement orchestration and builtin entry handling.
- ✓ Refactor `src/Vm.cpp` (`2950` lines) below the `700`-line target. Completed: extracted shared heap allocation and indirect-address helpers into `src/VmHeapHelpers.cpp`, extracted source-breakpoint resolution plus mapped stack-trace formatting helpers into `src/VmDebugHelpers.cpp`, extracted debug-session setup/start methods into `src/VmDebugSessionSetup.cpp`, extracted debug-session continue/breakpoint/pause/hook/snapshot methods into `src/VmDebugSession.cpp`, extracted debug-session instruction stepping into `src/VmDebugSessionInstruction.cpp`, extracted shared print/file I/O opcode handlers into `src/VmIoHelpers.cpp`, and moved the main VM execution loop into `src/VmExecution.cpp`, leaving `src/Vm.cpp` as the small public wrapper surface.
- ✓ Refactor `src/VmExecution.cpp` (`875` lines) below the `700`-line target. Completed: extracted arithmetic/compare/convert execution handling into `src/VmExecutionNumeric.cpp`, leaving `src/VmExecution.cpp` focused on stack/local/heap setup, control flow, calls/returns, I/O dispatch, and string helpers.
- ✓ Extract source-breakpoint resolution and mapped stack-trace formatting helpers from `Vm.cpp` into dedicated helper unit(s). Completed: `resolveSourceBreakpoints(...)` and `VmDebugSession::appendMappedStackTrace(...)` now live in `src/VmDebugHelpers.cpp`, leaving `Vm.cpp` focused on execution and session control flow.
- ✓ Extract shared file/print I/O opcode helpers from `Vm.cpp` into dedicated helper unit(s). Completed: print and file opcode families now route through shared `vm_detail::handlePrintOpcode(...)` and `vm_detail::handleFileOpcode(...)` helpers in `src/VmIoHelpers.cpp`, so `Vm::execute(...)` and `VmDebugSession::stepInstruction(...)` share one implementation for those behaviors.
- ✓ Refactor `src/wasm_emitter/WasmEmitter.cpp` (`2306` lines) below the `700`-line target. Completed: extracted Wasm module section encoding helpers into `src/wasm_emitter/WasmEmitterSections.cpp`, extracted WASI runtime detection/context setup plus data-segment staging into `src/wasm_emitter/WasmEmitterRuntimeContext.cpp`, extracted the `emitWasi*` helper cluster into `src/wasm_emitter/WasmEmitterWasiHelpers.cpp`, extracted structured control-flow lowering into `src/wasm_emitter/WasmEmitterControlFlow.cpp`, extracted local-layout plus function-body assembly into `src/wasm_emitter/WasmEmitterFunctionBodies.cpp`, extracted module assembly plus export-name/return-kind helpers into `src/wasm_emitter/WasmEmitterModule.cpp`, extracted print/file/argv opcode lowering into `src/wasm_emitter/WasmEmitterIo.cpp`, and shared internal Wasm type/layout structs through `src/wasm_emitter/WasmEmitterInternal.h`, leaving the original file focused on generic opcode lowering glue.

**Architecture follow-up (type-resolution dependency graph / SCC solver) (March 22, 2026)**
- ✓ Add a `DiagnosticSink` abstraction shared by compile pipeline, semantics, lowering, and both CLIs. Progress: `DiagnosticSink`/`DiagnosticSinkReport` now back shared parser/semantic/lowering diagnostics, `IrPreparationFailure` and both CLI entrypoints consume the same report shape, and compile-pipeline/semantics adapters no longer translate between bespoke diagnostic record structs.
- ✓ Extract per-definition `ValidationContext` objects from `SemanticsValidator` and remove shared mutable traversal fields. Progress: `SemanticsValidator` now caches base validation contexts per definition and execution, scopes a single `currentValidationContext_` through validation, and no longer carries parallel mutable traversal fields for effects, moves, borrow ends, or definition metadata.
- ✓ Add a short staged rollout note that names the implementation checkpoints (builder/dump, return-kind pilot, parity, template/local expansion, query and CT-eval follow-ups, legacy removal) and the rollback gates between them. Progress: the Architecture follow-up section now spells out the ordered builder/dump → return-kind pilot → parity/default flip → template/local expansion → query/CT-eval → legacy removal stages and the rollback gate that must hold between each stage.
- ✓ Add a short design note in `docs/PrimeStruct.md` defining the graph model and invariants: node kinds (definition return-kind, call-site type constraints, local `auto` constraints), edge kinds (dependency vs requirement), legal-cycle policy (SCC fixed-point) vs illegal-cycle policy (deterministic diagnostic), and deterministic ordering guarantees. Progress: `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now defines the planned internal graph boundary, node and edge classes, SCC-vs-illegal-cycle policy, and the stable ordering guarantees that later builder/dump/solver slices must preserve.
- ✓ Introduce a new semantics-internal `TypeResolutionGraph` data model and builder (no behavior change): build nodes/edges from canonical AST after semantic transforms/template monomorphization, with stable node IDs and stable edge insertion order. Progress: semantics now has an internal `TypeResolutionGraph` with definition-return, call-constraint, and local-auto nodes plus dependency/requirement edges, and the testing helper path builds it from the canonical post-transform/post-monomorphization AST with deterministic node IDs and edge order.
- ✓ Add a deterministic graph dump hook for tests/tooling (`--dump-stage=type-graph` or equivalent) and snapshot tests that lock graph shape/order for representative programs (simple call chain, mutual recursion, namespace imports, templates). Progress: the compile pipeline and both CLIs now expose deterministic `type-graph`/`type_graph` dumps through the shared formatter, and snapshot-style tests lock representative simple-call, mutual-recursion, namespace-import, and template-specialization graph shape and order.
- ✓ Implement reusable SCC utilities (Tarjan/Kosaraju) in a standalone unit with unit tests for acyclic, single-cycle, multi-cycle, and disconnected graphs. Progress: semantics now has a standalone Tarjan-based SCC utility with deterministic component/member ordering, a stable testing helper API, and focused unit coverage for acyclic, single-cycle, multi-cycle, and disconnected graphs.
- ✓ Implement a condensation-DAG builder on top of the SCC utilities, with deterministic ordering tests. Progress: semantics now has a standalone condensation-DAG utility layered on the SCC helper, with deduplicated inter-component edges, deterministic topological traversal order, a stable testing snapshot API, and focused ordering tests for collapsed cycles and disconnected roots.
- ✓ Land a first vertical solver slice for definition return-kind inference only: run SCC fixed-point over return constraints, preserve current diagnostics, and gate the new path behind a temporary opt-in flag (`--type-resolver=graph`). Progress: the validator now accepts `--type-resolver=graph`, orders return-kind inference through the type-resolution graph plus SCC/condensation traversal, and keeps the current recursive-annotation, unresolved, and conflicting-return diagnostics stable while the graph path remains opt-in.
- ✓ Add parity harness tests that run legacy vs graph return-kind inference on the same corpus and assert identical diagnostics/IR for supported cases; include explicit expected-diff tests where legacy behavior is intentionally corrected. Progress: IR-pipeline parity helpers now compare compile-pipeline diagnostics plus serialized VM IR across `legacy` and `graph` resolver modes for a shared corpus, and the expected-diff coverage now locks grounded mutual recursion as a deliberate graph-only fixed-point success while legacy still reports the explicit-annotation diagnostic.
- ✓ Flip return-kind inference default to graph mode once parity is stable; keep a temporary fallback flag (`--type-resolver=legacy`) for rollback during stabilization. Progress: shared `Options` and `Semantics` entrypoints now default to the graph resolver, semantics test helpers follow the same default path, and explicit `--type-resolver=legacy` / `typeResolver=\"legacy\"` overrides remain available as the temporary rollback path with focused default-vs-legacy regressions.
- ✓ Upgrade graph-mode return-kind fallback to deterministic illegal-cycle diagnostics that name unresolved SCC members and lock the intentional diff versus legacy. Progress: unresolved graph SCCs now fail with stable cycle-member messages/spans instead of the old per-definition fallback, and parity coverage now snapshots the graph-only recursive-cycle diagnostic as an explicit expected diff from legacy.
- ✓ Land the first post-stability graph expansion slice for local `auto` inference on direct call initializers, keeping deterministic diagnostics and the temporary `--type-resolver=legacy` rollback path. Progress: graph mode now records resolved direct-call `local_auto` sites from the type-resolution graph and reuses those binding types during validation, while legacy remains available as an explicit rollback path and parity coverage now includes struct/collection direct-call auto bindings.
- ✓ Extend graph local `auto` solving beyond direct call initializers to block/control-flow-valued initializers, keeping deterministic diagnostics and the temporary `--type-resolver=legacy` rollback path. Progress: graph mode now walks definition bodies in deterministic source order, captures supported block/`if`/`match` local-`auto` sites alongside the existing direct-call slice, and reuses those binding types during validation while explicit `--type-resolver=legacy` remains the rollback path.
- ✓ Extend graph local `auto` solving to omitted-envelope inference, keeping deterministic diagnostics and the temporary `--type-resolver=legacy` rollback path. Progress: graph mode now reuses graph-collected omitted-envelope bindings for struct-field resolution via owner-scoped lookup keys, so omitted field envelopes follow the same deterministic graph cache as local `auto` sites while explicit `--type-resolver=legacy` remains the rollback path.
- ✓ Land the first graph-backed inference-query cleanup slice: replace the remaining ad hoc inferred-envelope query call sites with a shared graph-derived lookup helper in graph mode, while preserving diagnostics and the temporary `--type-resolver=legacy` rollback path. Progress: shared `inferDefinitionReturnBinding(...)` queries now run under the callee definition context so graph-mode local/omitted inference caches are visible during query-time return binding reconstruction, and the remaining inline query walkers in `SemanticsValidator.cpp`/`SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp` now delegate to that shared helper instead of rebuilding callee locals separately.
- ✓ Extract a shared graph-backed type-text query helper for call-site result/map receiver probing so `SemanticsValidator.cpp` and `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp` stop carrying parallel direct map-constructor and envelope-type walkers, while preserving diagnostics and the temporary `--type-resolver=legacy` rollback path. Progress: `SemanticsValidatorInferCollections.cpp` now owns shared graph-aware query-time type-text inference for envelope-wrapped/direct-map/direct-call probes, `resolveResultTypeForExpr(...)` consumes that helper for map/result receiver queries, and `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp` now calls the shared collection-query helpers with explicit validation context instead of keeping a second copy of the same walkers.
- ✓ Extract the same shared graph-backed type-text query helper into `SemanticsValidatorInfer.cpp` so inference-time collection/map probing stops carrying the remaining parallel direct map-constructor and envelope-type walkers before any broader CT-eval/query expansion, while preserving diagnostics and the temporary `--type-resolver=legacy` rollback path. Progress: the inference-side collection/map probing block in `SemanticsValidatorInfer.cpp` now delegates to the shared `resolveCallCollectionTypePath(...)` and `resolveCallCollectionTemplateArgs(...)` helpers instead of carrying its own direct-map constructor and type-text walkers, and parity coverage now includes helper-returned map value inference through inferred `at(...)` result kinds.
- ✓ Land the first CT-eval/query interaction slice for the graph resolver: route compile-time evaluation collection/result/map receiver probing through the shared graph-backed query helpers instead of legacy-only inference islands, while preserving deterministic diagnostics and the temporary `--type-resolver=legacy` rollback path. Progress: the compile-time struct/query probe path in `inferStructReturnPath(...)` now delegates collection/map receiver probing to the shared graph-backed helper stack instead of keeping a legacy-only direct-map/type-text island, and parity coverage now includes helper-returned map value inference through that compile-time probe path.
- ✓ Extract the remaining CT-eval/query receiver-classifier closures from `inferStructReturnPath(...)` into the shared builtin-collection dispatch/resolver path so args-pack/vector/string/map receiver probing stops depending on a separate local classification island, while preserving deterministic diagnostics and the temporary `--type-resolver=legacy` rollback path. Progress: `SemanticsValidatorInferCollections.cpp` now builds the shared builtin receiver-classifier bundle for args-pack/array/vector/string/map probing, `inferStructReturnPath(...)` consumes that shared bundle instead of carrying a second local classifier island, and parity coverage now locks nested string helper inference through args-pack, vector, and map receivers.
- ✓ Extract the earlier builtin collection receiver-classifier island from `inferExprReturnKind(...)` into the same shared resolver bundle so return-kind inference and CT-eval/query probing consume one args-pack/vector/string/map classification path before any broader legacy-removal slice. Progress: `inferExprReturnKind(...)` now binds args-pack/array/vector/string/map receiver classifiers from `makeBuiltinCollectionDispatchResolvers(...)` instead of carrying a second local island, and parity coverage now locks auto return-kind inference through shared args-pack/vector/map string-helper receivers.
- ✓ Extract the remaining indexed args-pack / wrapped-pointee collection plumbing shared by `inferExprReturnKind(...)` and `inferStructReturnPath(...)` into the same resolver stack so `soa_vector` and `Buffer` probing stop depending on a second local helper layer before broader legacy removal. Progress: the shared builtin receiver bundle now exports indexed-args-pack / dereferenced / wrapped-pointee resolver hooks plus shared `soa_vector` and `Buffer` target resolvers, `inferExprReturnKind(...)` binds those shared hooks instead of keeping a second local helper layer, and parity coverage now locks borrowed `soa_vector` auto return inference through `to_aos(dereference(values[0i32]))`.
- ✓ Extract the same indexed args-pack / wrapped-pointee builtin receiver plumbing into `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp` so validation-time collection, `soa_vector`, and related helper probing stop carrying a parallel resolver island before broader legacy removal. Progress: `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp` now binds indexed / dereferenced / wrapped args-pack receiver resolution from `makeBuiltinCollectionDispatchResolvers(...)`, keeps only Expr-specific field-binding and inferred-return fallbacks, and validation coverage now locks borrowed indexed `soa_vector` probing through `to_aos(dereference(values[0i32]))`.
- ✓ Extract the remaining Expr-specific collection/map/string receiver fallbacks in `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp` onto shared resolver adapters so validation no longer carries parallel field-binding-aware wrappers before broader legacy removal. Progress: `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp` now passes field-binding and inferred-call hooks into `makeBuiltinCollectionDispatchResolvers(...)`, consumes the shared array/vector/`soa_vector`/string/map receiver resolvers directly, and validation coverage now locks field-bound map access through `holder.values.at(1i32).count()`.
- ✓ Extract the remaining experimental-map receiver routing helpers in `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp` onto shared map-receiver adapters so canonical/experimental helper redirection stops depending on local field-binding and inferred-return walkers before broader legacy removal. Progress: the shared builtin collection resolver bundle now exports experimental-map receiver and value-map classifiers, `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp` consumes those adapters for canonical/experimental helper redirection instead of keeping local field-binding and inferred-return walkers, and validation coverage now locks field-bound `Map<string, i32>` method receivers through `holder.values.tryAt(...)` / `.count()` / `.at(...)`.
- ✓ Consolidate the remaining experimental-map helper path canonicalization and rewrite tables in `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp` into shared helper metadata so map helper alias/canonical routing stops depending on local hard-coded path matchers before broader legacy removal. Progress: shared experimental-map helper metadata now owns helper-name/path canonicalization for canonical, alias, wrapper, and experimental helper paths, `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp` calls those shared helpers instead of keeping a local hard-coded table, and validation coverage now locks field-bound wrapper helper calls such as `/std/collections/mapCount(holder.values)`.
- ✓ Replace the remaining local experimental-map wrapper/compatibility heuristics in `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp` (for example `shouldBuiltinValidateCurrentMapWrapperHelper(...)` and the map compatibility path helpers) with shared helper metadata so map helper fallback rules stop depending on duplicated helper-name conditionals before broader legacy removal. Progress: the shared experimental-map helper descriptor table now drives wrapper-availability checks plus direct/method compatibility-path parsing, `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp` calls those shared helpers instead of keeping local helper-name conditionals, and validation coverage now locks removed-path diagnostics on field-bound experimental-map receivers for both `/map/count(...)` and `./std/collections/map/count()`.
- ✓ Extract the remaining removed collection-helper compatibility parsing in `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp` (notably `explicitRemovedCollectionMethodPath(...)` and the unnamespaced map-count fallback logic) into shared collection-helper metadata so legacy removed-helper diagnostics stop depending on local string parsing before broader legacy removal. Progress: shared removed-collection helper metadata now owns vector/map compatibility helper whitelists plus explicit removed-path parsing, `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp` calls those shared helpers for explicit removed method routing, bare `count(...)` map fallback, and body-argument preservation, and validation coverage now locks field-bound bare `count(holder.values)` plus removed-path diagnostics on field-bound compatibility calls.
- ✓ Extract the remaining bare/body-argument removed map helper parsing in `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp` (for example `resolveBareMapCallBodyArgumentTarget(...)` and the remaining direct `count`/`at`/`at_unsafe` helper-name branches) onto shared collection-helper metadata so body-argument remapping stops depending on local helper-name parsing before broader legacy removal. Progress: shared removed-collection metadata now resolves bare and wrapped map body-argument helpers through one helper in `SemanticsValidatorInferCollections.cpp`, `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp` calls that shared helper instead of keeping local `count`/`at`/`at_unsafe` parsing lambdas, and validation coverage now locks field-bound bare `at(holder.values, ...) { ... }` remapping.
- ✓ Extract the remaining vector-like body-argument compatibility remapping in `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp` (currently the `preferVectorStdlibHelperPath(...)` path rewrite around block arguments and the remaining bare mutator/access helper branches) into shared collection-helper metadata so vector body-argument remapping stops depending on local path rewriting before broader legacy removal. Progress: `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp` now calls the shared `preferVectorStdlibHelperPath(...)`, `getVectorStatementHelperName(...)`, and `getDirectVectorHelperCompatibilityPath(...)` helpers instead of keeping local vector mutator/access compatibility tables, and validation coverage now locks array-alias statement diagnostics plus `/vector/push(...) { ... }` block-argument rejection through the shared helper metadata path.

**Types & Semantics (March 18, 2026)**
- ✓ Variadic arg-pack IR-backed experimental stdlib `Map<K, V>` value-pack count slice: explicit struct locals materialized from indexed `[args<Map<K, V>>]` access now preserve full struct-slot copies through IR/VM/native lowering, and callable-definition `[args<Map<K, V>>]` locals retain struct slot counts for downstream lowering, so canonical `/std/collections/map/count(...)` works across direct, pure-spread, and mixed forwarding of experimental stdlib map value packs.
- ✓ Variadic arg-pack pointer-backed uninitialized scalar-storage slice: `[args<Pointer<uninitialized<i32>>>]` parameters now validate in semantics, preserve uninitialized-storage metadata through variadic matching and indexed `at|at_unsafe|[]` dereference resolution, and execute indexed `init(...)` plus `take(dereference(...))` across direct, pure-spread, and mixed forwarding on IR/VM/native/C++ emitter paths, while `args<Reference<uninitialized<i32>>>` remains a follow-up.
- ✓ Variadic arg-pack borrowed uninitialized scalar-storage slice: `[args<Reference<uninitialized<i32>>>]` parameters now validate in semantics, preserve uninitialized-storage metadata through variadic matching and indexed `at|at_unsafe|[]` dereference resolution, and execute indexed `init(...)` plus `take(dereference(...))` across direct, pure-spread, and mixed forwarding on IR/VM/native/C++ emitter paths.
- ✓ Ownership/drop pointer-backed uninitialized storage slice: top-level `Pointer<uninitialized<T>>` and `Reference<uninitialized<T>>` wrappers now carry uninitialized-storage metadata through semantics and IR lowering, so pointer/reference-backed `init(dereference(...), value)`, `drop(dereference(...))`, and `take(dereference(...))` work for supported payload kinds.
- ✓ Remove the remaining no-import builtin fallback for canonical `/std/collections/vector/clear(...)`, `/std/collections/vector/remove_at(...)`, and `/std/collections/vector/remove_swap(...)` helper spellings once the imported stdlib wrapper path is stable. Progress: explicit canonical clear/remove_at/remove_swap statement calls now require imported stdlib wrappers or explicit helper definitions instead of falling through to builtin mutator emission, and semantics/C++/VM/native coverage pins deterministic `unknown call target: /std/collections/vector/clear|remove_at|remove_swap` failures when neither helper path exists.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned bare `vector` count/capacity behavior from semantics once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: bare `count(values)` and `capacity(values)` vector calls now rewrite to imported canonical `/std/collections/vector/count|capacity` helpers when available, still honor explicit `/vector/count|capacity` compatibility definitions, and otherwise fail deterministically with `unknown call target: /std/collections/vector/count|capacity` across semantics/C++/VM/native coverage.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned bare `vector` access behavior from semantics once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: bare `at(values, index)` and `at_unsafe(values, index)` vector calls now rewrite to imported canonical `/std/collections/vector/at|at_unsafe` helpers when available, still honor explicit `/vector/at|at_unsafe` compatibility definitions, and otherwise fail deterministically with `unknown call target: /std/collections/vector/at|at_unsafe` across semantics/C++/VM/native coverage.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned bare `vector` mutator behavior from semantics once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: bare `push(values, ...)`, `pop(values)`, `reserve(values, ...)`, `clear(values)`, `remove_at(values, ...)`, and `remove_swap(values, ...)` vector statements now rewrite to imported canonical `/std/collections/vector/*` helpers when available, still honor explicit `/vector/*` compatibility definitions, and otherwise fail deterministically with `unknown call target: /std/collections/vector/push|pop|reserve|clear|remove_at|remove_swap` across semantics/C++/VM/native coverage.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned explicit `/vector/*` compatibility helper routing from semantics once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: unresolved direct `/vector/count|capacity|at|at_unsafe|push|pop|reserve|clear|remove_at|remove_swap` calls now fail deterministically with `unknown call target: /vector/...` instead of falling back into builtin semantics, and explicit canonical `/std/collections/vector/*` calls no longer route through `/vector/*` compatibility definitions during semantics validation or return-kind inference.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` direct compatibility-call return-kind fallback from IR lowering once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: direct `/vector/count|capacity|at|at_unsafe` compatibility spellings now stop at IR setup return-kind resolution instead of inheriting canonical `/std/collections/vector/*` return metadata, and explicit canonical `/std/collections/vector/at|at_unsafe` probes no longer inherit `/vector/*` alias return metadata.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit access-helper receiver-kind synthesis from IR lowering once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: explicit `/vector/at(...)`, `/vector/at_unsafe(...)`, `/std/collections/vector/at(...)`, and `/std/collections/vector/at_unsafe(...)` receiver probes now stop before builtin primitive receiver-kind synthesis when no real same-path helper definition exists, so downstream method lookup no longer inherits `i32`/string-derived fallback types from those helper spellings while real same-path helper definitions still resolve through their declared return metadata.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit helper builtin method-sugar routing from IR lowering once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: explicit slash-method `/vector/count|capacity|at|at_unsafe|push|pop|reserve|clear|remove_at|remove_swap` and canonical `/std/collections/vector/*` spellings now stay on deterministic same-path `unknown method` diagnostics instead of re-entering builtin method fallback, direct helper probes for those same spellings now bypass the builtin count/capacity/access/mutator return-kind sugar even when helper definitions exist, and explicit slash-method vector access spellings no longer synthesize builtin element kinds through method-return fallback.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` statement helper emission fallback from IR lowering once canonical stdlib vector parity is proven. Progress: explicit `/vector/push|pop|reserve|clear|remove_at|remove_swap(...)` and canonical `/std/collections/vector/*` statement spellings now stop at ordinary direct-definition resolution instead of rewriting through the IR statement helper fallback, so lowering leaves those spellings unresolved unless a real same-path helper definition exists.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` native tail-dispatch builtin helper emission for explicit count/capacity/access helper spellings from IR lowering once canonical stdlib vector parity is proven. Progress: explicit `/vector/count|capacity|at|at_unsafe(...)` and canonical `/std/collections/vector/*` count/capacity/access spellings now stop at native tail-dispatch entry instead of emitting builtin vector IR, so the native dispatcher leaves those helper names unresolved for later same-path definition handling.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` non-tail explicit count/capacity/access helper fallback from IR lowering once canonical stdlib vector parity is proven. Progress: explicit `/vector/count|capacity|at|at_unsafe(...)` and canonical `/std/collections/vector/*` count/capacity/access spellings now skip the inline-call non-method helper fallback, so IR call dispatch either resolves same-path definitions directly or leaves those spellings `NotHandled` instead of rewriting them into builtin-style method helper probes.
- ✓ Remove the remaining compiler-owned `vector` helper emission paths from IR lowering beyond non-tail explicit count/capacity/access helper fallback, including canonical mutator helper fallback paths. Progress: canonical `/std/collections/vector/push|pop|reserve|clear|remove_at|remove_swap(...)` helper spellings now skip the inline-call non-method helper fallback too, so the remaining IR-lowering call paths either resolve real same-path helper definitions directly or leave those canonical mutator spellings `NotHandled` instead of rewriting them into builtin-style method probes.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` bare `capacity()` method fallback behavior from native/vm backends once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: native/vm lowering no longer clears the method-resolution failure for bare `values.capacity()` and wrapper-temporary `.capacity()` vector calls when no real helper exists, so those paths now require imported canonical or explicit helper definitions and otherwise fail deterministically with `unknown method: /vector/capacity`.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` bare `count()` method fallback behavior from native/vm backends once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: native/vm lowering no longer clears the method-resolution failure for bare `values.count()` and vector literal or wrapper-temporary `.count()` calls when no real helper exists, so those paths now require imported canonical or explicit helper definitions and otherwise fail deterministically with `unknown method: /vector/count`.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` bare-call `count/capacity` fallback behavior from native/vm backends once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: native/vm lowering no longer rewrites bare `count(values)` and `capacity(values)` vector calls through backend-owned method fallback or direct builtin IR emission, so those paths now require imported canonical helpers and otherwise fail deterministically with `unknown call target: /std/collections/vector/count|capacity`.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` bare access method fallback behavior from native/vm backends once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: native/vm lowering no longer clears unresolved `values.at(...)` and `values.at_unsafe(...)` vector method calls or synthesizes builtin access return kinds for those bare vector method forms when no real helper exists, so local, literal, and wrapper-temporary vector access methods now require imported canonical or explicit helper definitions and otherwise fail deterministically with `unknown method: /vector/at|at_unsafe`.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` bare mutator method fallback behavior from native/vm backends once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: native/vm lowering no longer clears unresolved `values.push(...)`, `values.pop()`, `values.reserve(...)`, `values.clear()`, `values.remove_at(...)`, and `values.remove_swap(...)` vector method calls when no real helper exists, so bare vector mutator methods now require imported canonical or explicit helper definitions and otherwise fail deterministically with `unknown method: /vector/push|pop|reserve|clear|remove_at|remove_swap`.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` bare `capacity()` method helper-path synthesis from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: `resolveMethodCallPath(...)` no longer synthesizes bare `values.capacity()` vector helper paths unless a real `/vector/capacity` or `/std/collections/vector/capacity` definition/metadata path exists, so unresolved C++ emitter helper-resolution probes stop before backend-owned `capacity()` method routing.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` bare `capacity()` method builtin emission fallback from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: unresolved bare `values.capacity()` and wrapper-temporary `.capacity()` calls now emit a deterministic deleted-helper stub instead of lowering through `ps_vector_capacity(...)`, so the C++ emitter no longer silently keeps compiler-owned builtin capacity-method execution when no real helper resolves.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` bare `count()` method helper-path synthesis from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: `resolveMethodCallPath(...)` no longer synthesizes bare `values.count()` vector helper paths unless a real `/vector/count` or `/std/collections/vector/count` definition/metadata path exists, so unresolved C++ emitter helper-resolution probes stop before backend-owned `count()` method routing.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` bare `count()` method builtin emission fallback from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: unresolved bare `values.count()` and wrapper-temporary `.count()` calls now emit a deterministic deleted-helper stub instead of lowering through the builtin count emit path, so the C++ emitter no longer silently keeps compiler-owned builtin count-method execution when no real helper resolves.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` bare access method helper-path synthesis from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: `resolveMethodCallPath(...)` no longer infers builtin element receiver types for bare `values.at(...)` and `values.at_unsafe(...)` vector methods when no same-path helper metadata exists; those bare methods now prefer real `/vector/at*` metadata first, otherwise canonical `/std/collections/vector/at*`, and otherwise stay unresolved.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` bare access method builtin emission fallback from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: unresolved bare `values.at(...)` and wrapper-temporary `.at_unsafe(...)` vector methods now emit deterministic deleted-helper stubs instead of lowering through builtin `ps_vector_at(...)` or `ps_array_at_unsafe(...)` execution, so the C++ emitter no longer silently keeps compiler-owned vector access-method execution when no real helper resolves.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` bare access call builtin emission fallback from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: unresolved bare `at(wrapVector(), ...)` and `at_unsafe(wrapVector(), ...)` vector calls now emit deterministic deleted-helper stubs instead of lowering through builtin access execution, so wrapper-returned vector calls no longer silently keep compiler-owned bare-call access behavior when no real helper resolves.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` bare `count(...)` call builtin emission fallback from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: unresolved bare `count(wrapVector())` calls now emit a deterministic deleted-helper stub instead of lowering through builtin array-count execution, so wrapper-returned vector count calls no longer silently keep compiler-owned bare-call count behavior when no real helper resolves.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` bare `capacity(...)` call builtin emission fallback from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: unresolved bare `capacity(wrapVector())` calls now emit a deterministic deleted-helper stub instead of lowering through builtin vector-capacity execution, so wrapper-returned vector capacity calls no longer silently keep compiler-owned bare-call capacity behavior when no real helper resolves.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` remaining bare mutator fallback behavior from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: unresolved bare `push/pop/reserve/clear/remove_at/remove_swap` vector statements now emit deterministic deleted-helper stubs instead of lowering through builtin mutator execution, so the C++ emitter no longer silently keeps compiler-owned bare mutator behavior when no real helper resolves.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit direct-call access struct-return receiver metadata fallback from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: explicit `/vector/at*` and `/std/collections/vector/at*` direct calls now prefer real same-path or canonical struct-return helper metadata over builtin element-type synthesis when used as receivers, so direct field/method chains follow actual helper return structs instead of forced vector-element routing.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit direct-call access helper return-kind receiver metadata fallback from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: explicit `/vector/at*` and `/std/collections/vector/at*` direct-call receivers now honor real helper return kinds like `string` and `i32` before vector-element fallback, so downstream `count()` and other receiver typing follows helper metadata whenever a real helper definition exists.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` no-helper explicit direct-call access `count(...)` receiver fallback from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: `count(/vector/at(...))` and matching explicit access receiver uses without real helper metadata now lower to a deterministic deleted helper stub instead of inheriting compiler-owned receiver typing from the access call result.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` no-helper explicit `/vector/at*` direct-call method receiver fallback from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: wrapper-returned `/vector/at(...).tag()` and matching alias direct-call method chains without helper metadata no longer borrow builtin vector element receiver typing, while helper-backed canonical return metadata still drives direct-call method chains.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` no-helper explicit `/std/collections/vector/at*` direct-call method receiver fallback from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: wrapper-returned `/std/collections/vector/at(...).tag()` and matching canonical direct-call method chains without helper metadata no longer borrow builtin vector element receiver typing, while helper-backed canonical return metadata still drives those direct-call method chains.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` remaining no-helper explicit direct-call access receiver metadata fallback from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: `resolveMethodCallPath(...)` no longer synthesizes primitive receiver types for no-helper explicit `/vector/at*` and `/std/collections/vector/at*` direct calls, so the C++ emitter helper layer now leaves those method receivers unresolved unless real same-path or canonical helper metadata exists.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit slash-method access helper receiver metadata fallback from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: helper-backed `values./vector/at*...` and `values./std/collections/vector/at*...` method chains now honor same-path or canonical helper return metadata before builtin element typing, so slash-method receiver method resolution follows real helper return structs and return kinds instead of element-type fallback.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` no-helper explicit slash-method access receiver fallback from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: no-helper `values./vector/at*...` and `values./std/collections/vector/at*...` chains on real vector receivers now stop before builtin element-type inference, so explicit slash-method access receivers stay unresolved unless helper metadata exists.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit call-form mutator builtin emission fallback from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: explicit `/vector/push...` and `/std/collections/vector/push...` statement calls without real helpers now lower to deterministic deleted-helper stubs instead of falling through to builtin `ps_vector_*` execution, including lambda statement emission.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit method-form mutator builtin emission fallback from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: explicit `values./vector/push...` and `values./std/collections/vector/clear...` statement forms without real helpers now lower to deterministic deleted-helper stubs instead of falling through to builtin `ps_vector_*` execution, including lambda statement emission.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit direct-call access count-receiver builtin emission fallback from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: no-helper `count(/vector/at*...)` and canonical explicit access count receivers on real vector values now lower through deleted access-helper stubs instead of nesting builtin `ps_vector_at*` emission inside the deleted receiver helper.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit direct-call access builtin emission fallback from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: no-helper explicit `/vector/at*` and `/std/collections/vector/at*` direct calls on real vector values now lower through deleted access-helper stubs instead of falling through to compiler-owned access emission.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit direct-call count/capacity builtin emission fallback from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: no-helper explicit `/vector/count|capacity(...)` and canonical `/std/collections/vector/count|capacity(...)` calls on real vector values now lower through deleted count/capacity helper stubs instead of falling through to compiler-owned helper routing.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit direct-call count/capacity compatibility helper routing from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: explicit `/vector/count|capacity(...)` calls now require same-path alias helpers in the emitter; canonical-only `/std/collections/vector/count|capacity(...)` definitions no longer satisfy alias direct-call receiver metadata or expression emission.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit canonical slash-method count/capacity builtin emission fallback from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: no-helper `values./std/collections/vector/count()` and `.capacity()` on real vector receivers now lower to the existing deleted method-helper stubs instead of falling through to builtin `ps_array_count` or `ps_vector_capacity` expression emission.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit alias slash-method count/capacity builtin emission fallback from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: no-helper `values./vector/count()` and `.capacity()` on real vector receivers now lower to the existing deleted method-helper stubs instead of falling through past vector builtin guards, while same-path alias helpers still work.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit canonical slash-method access builtin emission fallback from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: no-helper `values./std/collections/vector/at(...)` and `.at_unsafe(...)` on real vector receivers now lower to the existing deleted access-method helper stubs instead of falling through to builtin access expression emission.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit alias slash-method access builtin emission fallback from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: no-helper `values./vector/at(...)` and `.at_unsafe(...)` on real vector receivers now lower to the existing deleted access-method helper stubs instead of falling through to builtin access expression emission, while same-path alias helpers still work.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit slash-method access count-receiver builtin emission fallback from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: no-helper `count(values./vector/at(...))` and canonical slash-method access receivers now lower through the deleted access-helper stubs instead of dropping out of the explicit receiver path, while helper-backed slash-method access count forwarding still works.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit slash-method count/capacity compatibility helper routing from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: explicit `values./vector/count()` and `values./std/collections/vector/capacity()` now require same-path helpers in the emitter helper layer, so opposite-path slash-method helpers no longer satisfy method resolution or expression emission.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit slash-method access compatibility helper routing from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: explicit `values./vector/at(...)` and `values./std/collections/vector/at_unsafe(...)` now require same-path helpers in the emitter helper layer, so opposite-path slash-method access helpers no longer satisfy receiver metadata or method-chain resolution.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit slash-method access builtin emission fallback on non-vector receivers from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: explicit slash-method `vector/at(...)` and `vector/at_unsafe(...)` on string receivers now lower to deleted vector-helper stubs instead of falling through to `ps_string_at` or `ps_string_at_unsafe`.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit direct-call access builtin emission fallback on non-vector receivers from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: explicit direct-call `vector/at(...)` and `vector/at_unsafe(...)` on string receivers now lower to deleted vector-helper stubs instead of falling through to `ps_string_at` or `ps_string_at_unsafe`.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit canonical slash-method count builtin emission fallback on non-vector receivers from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: explicit canonical slash-method `count()` on string receivers now lowers to deleted vector-helper stubs unless a real same-path helper exists, instead of falling through to `ps_string_count`.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit canonical direct-call count builtin emission fallback on non-vector receivers from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: explicit canonical direct-call `count(...)` on string receivers now lowers to deleted vector-helper stubs unless a real same-path helper exists, instead of falling through to `ps_string_count`.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit canonical direct-call capacity builtin emission fallback on non-vector receivers from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: explicit canonical direct-call `capacity(...)` on map receivers now lowers to deleted vector-helper stubs unless a real same-path helper exists, instead of falling through to vector-target builtin fallback.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit canonical slash-method capacity builtin emission fallback on non-vector receivers from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: explicit canonical slash-method `capacity()` on map receivers now lowers to deleted vector-helper stubs unless a real same-path helper exists, instead of falling through to vector-target builtin fallback.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit canonical `clear/remove_at/remove_swap` alias-bridge mutator classification from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: canonical `clear/remove_at/remove_swap` statements now classify directly as vector mutators in the emitter helper layer without depending on `/vector/*` alias presence, so deleted-helper fallback stays available for those canonical spellings on its own.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit same-order call-form mutator compatibility helper routing from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: explicit `/vector/push(...)` and `/std/collections/vector/push(...)` statement calls now require same-path helpers in the body/lambda emitters when the receiver stays in the leading position, so opposite-path helper definitions no longer satisfy those explicit call-form mutators and they fall back to deleted helper stubs instead.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit same-order method-form mutator compatibility helper routing from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: explicit `values./vector/push(...)` and `values./std/collections/vector/push(...)` method statements now require same-path helpers in the body/lambda emitters, so opposite-path helper definitions no longer satisfy those explicit method-form mutators and they fall back to deleted helper stubs instead.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit reordered call-form mutator compatibility helper routing from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: explicit `/vector/push(3i32, values)` and `/std/collections/vector/push(3i32, values)` statement calls now require same-path helpers in the body/lambda emitters even when receiver probing finds the vector argument in a reordered position, so opposite-path helper definitions no longer satisfy those reordered explicit mutator calls and they fall back to deleted helper stubs instead.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit canonical direct-call `count(...)` map-target validation fallback once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: no-helper `/std/collections/vector/count(wrapMap())` now reports `unknown call target: /std/collections/vector/count` instead of re-entering builtin vector-target validation, while real same-path canonical helpers on map receivers still compile.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit canonical direct-call `capacity(...)` map-target validation fallback once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: no-helper `/std/collections/vector/capacity(wrapMap())` and `/std/collections/vector/capacity(values)` on map receivers now report `unknown call target: /std/collections/vector/capacity` instead of re-entering builtin vector-target validation, while real same-path canonical helpers on map receivers still compile.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit alias direct-call `capacity(...)` map-target validation fallback once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: no-helper `/vector/capacity(wrapMap())` and `/vector/capacity(values)` on map receivers now report `unknown call target: /vector/capacity` instead of re-entering builtin vector-target validation, while real same-path alias helpers on map receivers still compile.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit alias direct-call `count(...)` map-target validation fallback once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: no-helper `/vector/count(wrapMap())` and `/vector/count(values)` on map receivers now report `unknown call target: /vector/count` instead of re-entering builtin vector-target validation, while real same-path alias helpers on map receivers still compile.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit alias direct-call `count(...)` builtin emission fallback on string receivers from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: no-helper `/vector/count(wrapText())` now lowers to the deleted count-helper stub instead of falling through to `ps_string_count(...)`, while real same-path alias helpers on string receivers still compile.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit alias slash-method `count()` builtin emission fallback on string receivers from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: no-helper `wrapText()./vector/count()` now lowers to the deleted count-helper stub instead of falling through to string-count execution, while real same-path alias helpers on string receivers still compile.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit alias slash-method `count()` builtin emission fallback on map receivers from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: no-helper `wrapMap()./vector/count()` now lowers to the deleted count-helper stub instead of falling through to map-count execution, while real same-path alias helpers on map receivers still compile.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit alias slash-method `capacity()` builtin emission fallback on map receivers from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: no-helper `wrapMap()./vector/capacity()` now lowers to the deleted capacity-helper stub instead of falling through to vector-capacity execution, while real same-path alias helpers on map receivers still compile.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit alias direct-call `count(...)` builtin emission fallback on array receivers from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: no-helper `/vector/count(wrapArray())` now lowers to the deleted count-helper stub instead of falling through to `ps_array_count(...)`, while real same-path alias helpers on array receivers still compile.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit alias slash-method `count()` builtin emission fallback on array receivers from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: no-helper `wrapArray()./vector/count()` now lowers to the deleted count-helper stub instead of falling through to `ps_array_count(...)`, while real same-path alias helpers on array receivers still compile.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit alias direct-call `capacity(...)` array-target validation fallback once canonical stdlib `vector` parity is proven. Progress: no-helper `/vector/capacity(wrapArray())` and `/vector/capacity(values)` on array receivers now report `unknown call target: /vector/capacity` instead of re-entering vector-target validation, while real same-path alias helpers on array receivers still compile.
- ✓ Remove compiler-owned `vector` explicit canonical slash-method `count()` builtin emission fallback on map and array receivers from the C++ emitter once canonical stdlib vector parity is proven. Progress: no-helper `wrapMap()./std/collections/vector/count()` and `wrapArray()./std/collections/vector/count()` now lower to deleted vector-helper stubs instead of falling through to builtin `map`/`array` count execution, while real same-path canonical helpers on those receivers still compile.
- ✓ Add stdlib matrix types (`Mat2`, `Mat3`, `Mat4`) with constructors and component accessors. Progress: stdlib now ships `Mat2`, `Mat3`, and `Mat4` as nominal math structs with public scalar `mRC` fields, so positional/named struct constructors and direct component access work under `import /std/math/*`.
- ✓ Add stdlib `Quat` with constructors, component accessors, and normalization helpers. Progress: stdlib now ships `Quat` as a nominal math struct with public scalar `x/y/z/w` fields plus `toNormalized()` and `normalize()` helpers, so constructor, field-access, and normalization flows work under `import /std/math/*`.
- ✓ Implement matrix/quaternion `plus` shape validation with deterministic diagnostics. Progress: semantics now accepts `plus` only when both operands resolve to the same stdlib matrix/quaternion type (`Mat2`, `Mat3`, `Mat4`, or `Quat`) and emits a stable mismatch diagnostic otherwise.
- ✓ Implement matrix/quaternion `minus` shape validation with deterministic diagnostics. Progress: semantics now accepts `minus` only when both operands resolve to the same stdlib matrix/quaternion type (`Mat2`, `Mat3`, `Mat4`, or `Quat`) and emits a stable mismatch diagnostic otherwise.
- ✓ Implement matrix/quaternion `multiply` allowlist validation with deterministic diagnostics. Progress: semantics now accepts only the documented `multiply` combinations that mention `Mat*`/`Quat` (scalar scaling, `Mat*Vec*`, matching `Mat*Mat*`, `Quat*Quat`, and `Quat*Vec3`) and emits a stable mismatch diagnostic otherwise.
- ✓ Implement matrix/quaternion `divide` composite-by-scalar-only validation with deterministic diagnostics. Progress: semantics now accepts only `Mat* / scalar` and `Quat / scalar` in the matrix/quaternion family and emits a stable mismatch diagnostic otherwise.
- ✓ Implement `quat_to_mat3`. Progress: stdlib now ships `/std/math/quat_to_mat3`, which normalizes the input quaternion and returns the corresponding `Mat3` rotation matrix under `import /std/math/*`.
- ✓ Implement `quat_to_mat4`. Progress: stdlib now ships `/std/math/quat_to_mat4`, which reuses `quat_to_mat3` for the rotation basis and expands it into a homogeneous `Mat4` under `import /std/math/*`.
- ✓ Implement `mat3_to_quat`. Progress: stdlib now ships `/std/math/mat3_to_quat`, which converts a `Mat3` rotation basis into a normalized `Quat` using a deterministic branch order under `import /std/math/*`.
- ✓ Reject implicit matrix/quaternion family conversions with deterministic diagnostics. Progress: semantics now rejects implicit `Mat*`/`Quat` family conversions at binding initialization, return sites, and helper-call arguments with a stable diagnostic that points users to explicit constructor/helper flows instead.
- ✓ Add VM support-matrix conformance tests for matrix/quaternion behavior, including accept/reject cases and deterministic diagnostics. Progress: VM compile-run coverage now locks the current accepted nominal/helper surface (`Mat*`, `Quat`, `quat_to_mat3`, `quat_to_mat4`, `mat3_to_quat`) and deterministic reject diagnostics for matrix-shape mismatch and implicit matrix/quaternion family conversions.
- ✓ Add native support-matrix conformance tests for matrix/quaternion behavior, including accept/reject cases and deterministic diagnostics. Progress: native compile-run coverage now locks the current accepted nominal/helper surface (`Mat*`, `Quat`, `quat_to_mat3`, `quat_to_mat4`, `mat3_to_quat`) and deterministic reject diagnostics for matrix-shape mismatch and implicit matrix/quaternion family conversions.
- ✓ Add Wasm support-matrix conformance tests for matrix/quaternion behavior, including accept/reject cases and deterministic diagnostics. Progress: Wasm smoke coverage now locks the current accepted nominal/helper surface (`Mat*`, `Quat`, `quat_to_mat3`, `quat_to_mat4`, `mat3_to_quat`) and deterministic reject diagnostics for matrix-shape mismatch and implicit matrix/quaternion family conversions.
- ✓ Add C++ support-matrix conformance tests for matrix/quaternion behavior, including accept/reject cases and deterministic diagnostics. Progress: C++ emitter compile-run coverage now locks the current accepted nominal/helper surface (`Mat*`, `Quat`, `quat_to_mat3`, `quat_to_mat4`, `mat3_to_quat`) and deterministic reject diagnostics for matrix-shape mismatch and implicit matrix/quaternion family conversions.
- ✓ Add GLSL support-matrix conformance tests for matrix/quaternion behavior and deterministic unsupported/reject diagnostics where features are unavailable. Progress: GLSL compile coverage now locks the current accepted scalar + vector + matrix + quaternion subset, and it keeps deterministic reject coverage for invalid quaternion operator shapes.
- ✓ Extend math conformance suite with quaternion reference tests, including Hamilton product and quaternion-vector rotation. Progress: VM/native/Wasm/C++ conformance now locks exact runtime reference outputs for `Quat * Quat` Hamilton products and `Quat * Vec3` rotation while keeping the existing deterministic reject coverage around the matrix/quaternion operator surface.
- ✓ Extend math conformance suite with matrix/vector composition-order references and an explicit tolerance policy. Progress: VM/native/Wasm/C++ conformance now locks `Mat3 * Vec3` plus `Mat3 * Mat3` composition-order references under the documented column-vector convention, and the float matrix/quaternion reference suites now use an explicit absolute `1e-4` tolerance policy.
- ✓ Lower remaining runtime matrix arithmetic paths (`plus`, `minus`, scalar scaling, divide`) for VM/native/Wasm/C++ and add conformance coverage. Progress: VM/native/Wasm/C++ now lower component-wise `Mat2`/`Mat3`/`Mat4` `plus` + `minus`, scalar-left/right matrix scaling, and matrix-by-scalar `divide`, with representative runtime conformance references locked under the existing absolute `1e-4` tolerance policy.
- ✓ Lower remaining runtime quaternion arithmetic paths (`plus`, `minus`, scalar scaling, divide`) for VM/native/Wasm/C++ and add conformance coverage. Progress: VM/native/Wasm/C++ now lower component-wise `Quat` `plus` + `minus`, scalar-left/right quaternion scaling, and quaternion-by-scalar `divide`, with representative runtime conformance references locked under the existing absolute `1e-4` tolerance policy.
- ✓ Implement GLSL lowering for nominal `Mat2`/`Mat3`/`Mat4` values, `mRC` field access, and the matrix-only operator subset (`plus`, `minus`, scalar scale/divide, matching matrix-matrix multiply) in GLSL, replacing those unsupported-type diagnostics with conformance coverage.
- ✓ Implement GLSL lowering for `Vec2`/`Vec3`/`Vec4` values plus matrix-vector interop, with conformance coverage.
- ✓ Implement GLSL lowering for documented vector arithmetic (`plus`, `minus`, scalar scale/divide`) with conformance coverage.
- ✓ Implement GLSL lowering for `Quat` nominal values and quaternion conversion/operator helpers, with conformance coverage.

**Maintainability (March 18, 2026)**
- ✓ Move duplicated compile-pipeline and IR-preparation error handling out of `main.cpp` and `primevm_main.cpp` into a shared driver layer. Progress: shared CLI driver helpers now own transform-list output plus compile-pipeline / IR-preparation diagnostic mapping and emission, and both entrypoints route those failures through `primec::CliDriver` instead of duplicating local switch trees.
- ✓ Introduce `primec_support_lib`, `primec_frontend_lib`, and `primec_backend_lib`, keeping `primec_lib` as the compatibility umbrella target for executables and tests.
- ✓ Introduce `primec_ir_lib`, `primec_codegen_lib`, and `primec_runtime_lib`, keeping `primec_backend_lib` as the compatibility umbrella target for backend-facing links.
- ✓ Introduce `primec_backend_registry_lib` for `IrBackends.cpp`, and route `primec_backend_lib` through that narrower registry target instead of linking `primec_codegen_lib` and `primec_runtime_lib` directly.
- ✓ Move shared VM backend diagnostics/normalization into IR-side helpers, and relink `primevm` to `primec_ir_lib` + `primec_runtime_lib` instead of the broader `primec_lib` umbrella.
- ✓ Move compile-pipeline-driven semantics suites onto a dedicated `PrimeStruct_semantics_tests` target linked to `primec_ir_lib` instead of `primec_lib`.
- ✓ Carve additional frontend/IR-only doctest suites out of `PrimeStruct_tests` so parser/text-filter coverage can link frontend/IR targets without the backend registry umbrella.
- ✓ Move text-filter doctest suites onto a dedicated `PrimeStruct_text_filter_tests` target linked to `primec_frontend_lib` instead of `primec_lib`.
- ✓ Move parser/template doctest suites onto a dedicated `PrimeStruct_parser_tests` target linked to `primec_frontend_lib` instead of `primec_lib`.
- ✓ Add CI checks for include-layer violations and direct `tests -> src/` internal header dependencies.
- ✓ Define a stable internal testing API so unit tests stop including `src/` headers directly.
- ✓ Expose parser and text-filter helper declarations via `include/primec/testing/` headers and switch the related unit tests away from direct `src/` includes.
- ✓ Remove the unused private `src/emitter/EmitterHelpers.h` dependency from `test_compile_run.cpp`.
- ✓ Expose semantics validation helper declarations via `include/primec/testing/SemanticsValidationHelpers.h` and switch `test_ir_pipeline.cpp` away from direct `src/semantics` includes.
- ✓ Add stable testing facades for `test_ir_pipeline.cpp` emitter helper access so it can drop its remaining `src/emitter` header dependencies.
- ✓ Add stable testing facades for `test_ir_pipeline.cpp` IR-lowerer helper access so it can drop its remaining `src/ir_lowerer` header dependencies.
- ✓ Extract lambda-expression validation from `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp` into dedicated helper unit `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprLambda.cpp`.
- ✓ Extract numeric and matrix/quaternion expression validation from `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp` into a dedicated helper unit `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprNumeric.cpp`.
- ✓ Extract control-flow/block expression validation from `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp` into dedicated helper units.
- ✓ Extract builtin `if(...)` expression validation from `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp` into dedicated helper unit `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprControlFlow.cpp`.
- ✓ Extract builtin `block { ... }` expression validation from `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp` into dedicated helper unit `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprBlock.cpp`.
- ✓ Extract match/if/block return-kind inference from `SemanticsValidatorInfer.cpp` into dedicated helper unit `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorInferControlFlow.cpp`.
- ✓ Extract collection and call-target return-kind inference from `SemanticsValidatorInfer.cpp` into dedicated helper unit(s).
- ✓ Extract collection type/template call-target inference helpers from `SemanticsValidatorInfer.cpp` into dedicated helper unit `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorInferCollections.cpp`.
- ✓ Extract builtin collection access/count/capacity return-kind dispatch from `SemanticsValidatorInfer.cpp` into dedicated helper unit(s).
- ✓ Extract builtin collection method/access return-kind dispatch from `SemanticsValidatorInfer.cpp` into dedicated helper unit `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorInferCollectionDispatch.cpp`.
- ✓ Extract the shared builtin collection count/capacity resolver from `SemanticsValidatorInfer.cpp` into dedicated helper unit `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorInferCollectionCountCapacity.cpp`.
- ✓ Move the remaining direct builtin collection count/capacity branches in `SemanticsValidatorInfer.cpp` onto dedicated helper dispatch paths.
- ✓ Extract definition/statement return-kind inference helpers from `SemanticsValidatorInfer.cpp` into dedicated helper unit `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorInferDefinition.cpp`.
- ✓ Extract templated fallback type-inference helpers from `TemplateMonomorph.cpp` into `src/semantics/TemplateMonomorphFallbackTypeInference.h`.
- ✓ Extract method-call target and binding-type inference helpers from `TemplateMonomorph.cpp` into dedicated helper unit(s).
- ✓ Extract method-call target resolution helpers from `TemplateMonomorph.cpp` into `src/semantics/TemplateMonomorphMethodTargets.h`.
- ✓ Extract binding-type inference helpers from `TemplateMonomorph.cpp` into dedicated helper unit(s).
- ✓ Extract block-bodied binding inference from `inferBindingTypeForMonomorph(...)` into `src/semantics/TemplateMonomorphBindingBlockInference.h`.
- ✓ Extract call/return-based binding-type inference from `inferBindingTypeForMonomorph(...)` into `src/semantics/TemplateMonomorphBindingCallInference.h`.
- ✓ Extract effects/capabilities resolution helpers from `SemanticsValidatorPasses.cpp` into `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorPassesEffects.cpp`.
