**Backends & IR (March 18, 2026)**
- ✓ Extend `/std/image/png/read(...)` to filter-`4` (`Paeth`) scanline reconstruction for 8-bit RGB/RGBA inputs. Progress: the shared stdlib decoder now reconstructs `Paeth` scanlines with the standard left/up/up-left predictor while preserving the public flat RGB output contract and dropping alpha when decoding RGBA inputs; compile-run coverage now locks stored `Paeth` reads for both RGB and RGBA fixtures on the native backend.
- ✓ Extend `/std/image/png/read(...)` to CRC validation for critical chunks. Progress: the shared stdlib PNG reader now validates CRCs on critical chunks, rejects mismatches as `image_invalid_operation`, and compile-run coverage rewrites fixture CRCs plus locks explicit bad-CRC failures on native and VM paths.
- ✓ Extend `/std/image/png/read(...)` to stricter PNG chunk-order validation beyond the current `IHDR`/`IDAT`/`IEND` checks. Progress: the shared stdlib PNG reader now enforces optional single-`PLTE` ordering ahead of the `IDAT` run, rejects late or duplicate `PLTE` chunks, and locks multi-chunk `IDAT` inputs to a single consecutive run with compile-run coverage for valid split-`IDAT` decodes plus malformed-order failures on native and VM paths.
- ✓ Extend `/std/image/png/read(...)` to remaining non-interlaced PNG color and bit-depth variants. Progress: the shared stdlib decoder now expands non-interlaced 1/2/4/8/16-bit grayscale, 1/2/4/8-bit indexed-color, 8/16-bit grayscale+alpha, and 8/16-bit RGB/RGBA scanlines into the public flat RGB buffer, native compile-run coverage locks packed, byte-aligned, and 16-bit decode paths, and the remaining non-interlaced work is split below.
- ✓ Extend `/std/image/png/read(...)` to 8-bit grayscale+alpha inputs. Progress: the shared stdlib decoder now reconstructs non-interlaced 8-bit grayscale+alpha scanlines with the existing filter path, expands grayscale samples into RGB, and drops alpha in the public pixel buffer; compile-run coverage locks the supported byte-aligned grayscale+alpha path.
- ✓ Extend `/std/image/png/read(...)` to indexed-color inputs with `PLTE` expansion. Progress: the shared stdlib decoder now reconstructs non-interlaced 8-bit indexed scanlines, expands palette entries from `PLTE` into the public flat RGB buffer, and rejects missing or out-of-range palette data as `image_invalid_operation`; compile-run coverage locks supported indexed reads and palette-overrun failures.
- ✓ Extend `/std/image/png/read(...)` to remaining 1/2/4/16-bit non-interlaced PNG variants. Progress: the shared stdlib decoder now unpacks non-interlaced 1/2/4-bit grayscale and indexed-color scanlines plus downscales 16-bit grayscale, grayscale+alpha, RGB, and RGBA scanlines into the public flat RGB buffer, compile-run coverage locks representative packed and 16-bit reads, keeps packed palette overruns invalid, and the Adam7 follow-up was split below.
- ✓ Extend `/std/image/png/read(...)` to 16-bit grayscale inputs. Progress: the shared stdlib decoder now reconstructs non-interlaced 16-bit grayscale scanlines with the existing filter path, downscales each 16-bit sample into the public 8-bit RGB buffer, and native compile-run coverage locks supported reads.
- ✓ Extend `/std/image/png/read(...)` to 16-bit grayscale+alpha inputs. Progress: the shared stdlib decoder now reconstructs non-interlaced 16-bit grayscale+alpha scanlines with the existing filter path, downscales each 16-bit grayscale sample into the public 8-bit RGB buffer while dropping alpha, and native compile-run coverage locks supported reads.
- ✓ Extend `/std/image/png/read(...)` to 16-bit RGB/RGBA inputs. Progress: the shared stdlib decoder now reconstructs non-interlaced 16-bit RGB and RGBA scanlines with the existing filter path, downscales each 16-bit RGB channel into the public 8-bit buffer while dropping RGBA alpha, and compile-run coverage locks supported RGB/RGBA reads.
- ✓ Extend `/std/image/png/read(...)` to Adam7 interlaced inputs. Progress: the shared stdlib decoder now reconstructs Adam7 passes for the current PNG read subset, scatters pass-local RGB outputs back into full-image order for grayscale, indexed-color, grayscale+alpha, RGB, and RGBA inputs, and compile-run coverage locks supported interlaced RGB plus packed indexed-color reads while malformed interlaced payloads remain `image_invalid_operation`.
- ✓ Add `/std/image/png/*` write implementations and conformance tests for VM/native/Wasm. Progress: the shared stdlib writer now emits deterministic non-interlaced 8-bit RGB PNG files with `IHDR`/`IDAT`/`IEND`, stored zlib/deflate blocks, and filter-`0` scanlines from the public flat RGB buffer, while native and VM tests lock exact file bytes plus invalid-input failures and wasm smoke coverage locks the current heap-allocation gating for vector-literal callers.
- ✓ Harden `/std/image/{ppm,png}/write(...)` dimension validation against `i32` overflow. Progress: the shared stdlib image write path now computes RGB payload counts plus PNG raw/zlib sizing in `i64`, rejects oversized dimension pairs before arithmetic wraparound can accept malformed writes or overrun the PNG encoder, and native/VM compile-run coverage locks deterministic invalid-input failures for overflowed-dimension PPM/PNG writes.
- ✓ Add successful Wasm-wasi runtime conformance for `/std/image/{ppm,png}/write(...)`. Progress: the smoke suite now compiles heap-alloc-enabled Wasm-wasi image-write programs, runs them under `wasmtime` when available with a preopened temp directory, locks deterministic PPM/PNG output bytes for successful writes, and keeps invalid PNG payload mismatches mapped to `image_invalid_operation` without creating output files.
- ✓ Harden `/std/image/{ppm,png}/read(...)` dimension-derived sizing against `i32` overflow. Progress: the shared stdlib read path now rejects oversized PPM headers before wrapped RGB component counts can succeed, validates PNG RGB-buffer and scanline sizing in `i64` before reserve/decode math runs, and native/VM compile-run coverage locks deterministic invalid-input failures for overflowed-dimension PPM/PNG reads while preserving output reset semantics.
- ✓ Allow Wasm-wasi `heap_alloc` metadata for shared stdlib image reads and add runtime conformance for `/std/image/{ppm,png}/read(...)`. Progress: the Wasm validator now admits `heap_alloc` on the wasi target so heap-backed image reads compile, and the smoke suite now locks successful Wasm-wasi runtime reads for representative PPM/PNG fixtures plus deterministic invalid-input failures instead of the older compile-time effect-mask rejection.
- ✓ Introduce `file_read` effect support for read-only file/image operations while keeping `file_write` compatibility. Progress: semantics and IR validation/lowering now recognize `file_read`, treat `file_write` as implying `file_read`, the shared stdlib image read surface now requires `effects(file_read, heap_alloc)`, and native/VM/Wasm conformance coverage now locks direct `file_read` reads while preserving existing `file_write` callers.

**Web + Native + Metal 3D Target (Spinning Cube) (March 18, 2026)**
Graphics status note: the archived finished items for this area cover contract locking, host samples, and browser/native/Metal smoke coverage. They do not mean the canonical `/std/gfx/*` language surface is implemented end-to-end today.
Implementation status note: current spinning-cube execution is still driven by shared `.prime` simulation/math plus browser/native/Metal host glue, but the repo now ships initial canonical stdlib `/std/gfx/*` definitions in `.prime` for the proven first slice and the browser, native-window, and Metal sample bootstrap/host/launcher paths now all run through canonical/shared helpers instead of owning their full runtime shells inline. Unsupported wasm/shader-only backend diagnostics now reject `/std/gfx/*` imports before backend emission; full lowering/runtime support and cleanup of the remaining sample-owned compatibility/contract glue for the locked graphics API surface are still incomplete.
Stdlib-first graphics roadmap note: follow the same direction as the collections de-builtinization work. Keep the public graphics API and as much validation/composition/policy as possible in stdlib `.prime` files; leave only the minimal OS/GPU/backend substrate in C++ and host code (windowing, device/swapchain/presentation, resource handles, command submission, and backend interop).
Bring-up note: land a real `.prime` implementation under an experimental path/name first, prove one vertical slice on the first supported target, replace the canonical wrapper/contract-only entry points with that real `.prime` surface, then delete bespoke sample ABI glue and any compiler-owned compatibility handling that is no longer needed.
Deletion gating note: do not add more sample-only or host-only TODO slices unless they directly unblock the canonical `/std/gfx/*` stdlib surface, a concrete backend substrate boundary, or deletion of the current spinning-cube-specific ABI glue.
- ✓ Experimental stdlib `gfx` constructor slice: implement the locked `Window(...)` shape once it can be expressed without reintroducing compiler-owned compatibility routing beyond the minimal substrate. Progress: experimental `Window([title] ..., [width] ..., [height] ...)?` calls now rewrite onto a dedicated substrate-backed stdlib helper, so `GraphicsSubstrate.createWindow(...)` drives the real Result-returning window constructor path while direct non-`Result` bindings keep deterministic type-mismatch diagnostics.
- ✓ Experimental stdlib `gfx` device-constructor slice: implement the locked `Device()` shape once it can be expressed without reintroducing compiler-owned compatibility routing beyond the minimal substrate. Progress: experimental `Device()?` calls now rewrite onto a dedicated substrate-backed stdlib helper that validates deterministic device/queue token creation through `GraphicsSubstrate.createDevice(...)` and `createQueue(...)`, while direct non-`Result` bindings keep deterministic type-mismatch diagnostics.
- ✓ Experimental stdlib `gfx` pipeline-constructor slice: implement the locked `Device.create_pipeline([vertex_type] type, ...)` shape once it can be expressed without reintroducing compiler-owned compatibility routing beyond the minimal substrate. Progress: experimental `Device.create_pipeline([vertex_type] VertexColored, ...)` calls now rewrite onto a dedicated substrate-backed stdlib helper for the locked v1 vertex wire type, successful calls return real `Pipeline` values that feed `material()?`, and unsupported vertex-type arguments now reject with deterministic experimental-surface diagnostics.
- ✓ Experimental stdlib `gfx` runtime slice (resource/frame wrappers): route the remaining fallible wrapper paths through the explicit substrate boundary before tackling pass encoding. Progress: experimental `Device.create_swapchain(...)`, `Device.create_mesh(...)`, and `Swapchain.frame()` now build `SubstrateSwapchainConfig`, `SubstrateMeshConfig`, and `SubstrateFrameConfig`, validate deterministic token/error propagation through `GraphicsSubstrate.createSwapchain(...)`, `createMesh(...)`, and `acquireFrame(...)`, and compile-run coverage now locks both successful wrapper flow and deterministic error propagation across exe/vm/native.
- ✓ Experimental stdlib `gfx` runtime slice (render-pass encoding): route `Frame.render_pass(...)`, `RenderPass.draw_mesh(...)`, and `RenderPass.end()` through the minimal substrate needed to encode one pass and one mesh draw on the first supported target without changing the locked public API shape. Progress: the non-Result experimental render-pass wrapper path now routes through explicit pass-encoding substrate helpers (`openRenderPass`, `drawMesh`, `endRenderPass`) plus new pass/draw/end config records, `Frame.render_pass(...)` now returns deterministic pass tokens through that substrate layer with zero-token fallback on invalid frames, `RenderPass.draw_mesh(...)` / `end()` now route through deterministic no-op substrate helpers for invalid handles, and substrate + wrapper compile-run coverage now lock both success-path tokens and invalid-handle fallback across exe/vm/native.
- ✓ Experimental stdlib `gfx` host/runtime slice (first supported target): thread that explicit pass/draw substrate through one host path so the experimental package can drive submit/present end-to-end on the first supported target. Progress: the shared spinning-cube sample/host path has now been promoted onto the canonical `/std/gfx/*` naming surface as `cubeStdGfxEmitFrameStream` plus the macOS host `--gfx` mode, which still exercises `Window(...)`, `Device()`, `create_swapchain`, `create_mesh`, `create_pipeline`, `frame`, `render_pass`, `draw_mesh`, `submit`, and `present` while emitting the same deterministic native-host stream header plus shared simulation tail; compile-run/source-lock coverage now pins that canonical entrypoint and host smoke path on the first supported target.
- ✓ Add compile-run conformance that imports the experimental graphics package and exercises `Device()`, `create_swapchain`, `create_mesh`, `create_pipeline`, `frame`, `render_pass`, `draw_mesh`, `submit`, and `present`, replacing the current doc-lock-only coverage for the API surface with real end-to-end language tests. Progress: `tests/unit/test_compile_run_smoke_core.h` now includes one imported end-to-end experimental gfx program that drives `Window(...)`, `Device()`, `default_queue()`, `create_swapchain(...)`, `create_mesh(...)`, `create_pipeline(...)`, `material()`, `frame()`, `render_pass(...)`, `draw_mesh(...)`, `submit(...)`, and `present()` with deterministic token checks across exe/vm/native, and the semantics import coverage now exercises the same pipeline wrapper path instead of bypassing it with a direct `Pipeline([token] ...)` constructor.
- ✓ Add canonical stdlib `/std/gfx/*` entry points in `.prime` that route to the proven experimental graphics implementation once the first slice is stable, with deterministic import and helper-resolution coverage. Progress: `stdlib/std/gfx/gfx.prime` now exposes the canonical `Window`, `Device`, `Queue`, `Swapchain`, `Frame`, `RenderPass`, `Pipeline`, `Material`, `Mesh`, `VertexColored`, `GfxError`, and related enum/value types under `/std/gfx/*`, the same constructor/pipeline rewrite pass now recognizes canonical `Window(...)`, `Device()`, and `Device.create_pipeline([vertex_type] VertexColored, ...)` calls, and semantics plus exe/vm/native compile-run coverage now lock both canonical wildcard import/helper resolution and canonical end-to-end wrapper flow.
- ✓ Port the shared spinning-cube gfx frame-stream sample/host path onto canonical `/std/gfx/*` usage while keeping the shared simulation tail and deterministic native-host stream shape. Progress: the shared gfx frame-stream entry now imports canonical `/std/gfx/*` in `cube.prime` as `cubeStdGfxEmitFrameStream`, the macOS native window host now consumes that canonical stream through `--gfx` instead of the old experimental naming, and source-lock/compile-run/README coverage now pins the canonical sample path while preserving the shared simulation tail.
- ✓ Delete the remaining bespoke `cubeNativeAbi*` frame-stream entrypoints and the `--cube-sim` native window host mode once the canonical `/std/gfx/*` path fully replaces the fixed-step simulation stream in sample/docs/smoke coverage. Progress: `cubeNativeAbiEmitFrameStream` has been removed from `cube.prime`, the macOS window host and launcher now accept only `--gfx` for the shared graphics stream path, and sample/docs/smoke coverage now compiles, launches, and visual-smoke-checks `cubeStdGfxEmitFrameStream` instead of the older fixed-step simulation stream.
- ✓ Delete the remaining bespoke `cubeNativeAbi*` scalar/conformance compatibility helpers once no retained conformance coverage depends on the old non-graphics ABI surface. Progress: the old scalar ABI wrapper/conformance block has been removed from `cube.prime`, `cubeStdGfxEmitFrameStream` now advances its deterministic simulation tail directly through `cubeNativeFrame*` helpers plus fixed constants, and active compile-run/source-lock coverage no longer references the old non-graphics ABI surface.
- ✓ Add deterministic unsupported/partial-support diagnostics for `/std/gfx/*` on backends that still lack the required runtime substrate, and lock those rejects in semantics plus backend conformance coverage. Progress: compile-pipeline semantic validation now rejects canonical and experimental gfx imports on wasm (`wasm-browser`, `wasm-wasi`) and shader-only (`glsl`, `spirv`) emits with deterministic target-context diagnostics before backend emission, and semantics plus backend conformance coverage now lock those rejects.
- ✓ Replace sample-owned duplicate graphics contract pieces (host-side `GfxError` code maps, vertex-layout copies, and other parallel contract definitions) with canonical stdlib/shared definitions once `/std/gfx/*` exists, so browser/native/Metal examples stop carrying their own parallel API surface. Progress: the native window host and Metal host now both include `examples/shared/gfx_contract_shared.h` for the deterministic `GfxError` code map and the locked `/std/gfx/VertexColored` host upload layout, and source-lock coverage now pins that shared header plus both host integrations instead of duplicating those contract definitions per sample.
- ✓ Reduce the native macOS gfx window launcher to a thin wrapper over a shared canonical gfx launch helper, so the sample path no longer owns its own compile/run/visual-smoke orchestration logic. Progress: `scripts/run_native_spinning_cube_window.sh` now just binds spinning-cube-specific paths/entrypoints onto `scripts/run_canonical_gfx_native_window.sh`, while the shared helper owns the reusable preflight, build, launch, and visual-smoke flow for canonical `--gfx` native window runs.
- ✓ Reduce the native macOS window host sample to a thin launcher over shared runtime/presenter helpers, and remove remaining spinning-cube-specific Objective-C++ glue that no longer contributes reusable substrate. Progress: the shared native window presenter shell now lives in `examples/shared/native_metal_window_host.h`, which owns startup/runtime failure emission, `NSWindow`/`CAMetalLayer` bring-up, frame-loop scheduling, and first-frame/exit diagnostics; `examples/native/spinning_cube/window_host.mm` now keeps only stream parsing, cube/software-surface setup, and render callbacks plus CLI binding onto that shared presenter helper.
- ✓ Reduce the Metal sample host/launcher path to thin launchers over shared reusable runtime helpers once the native host substrate extraction is in place. Progress: the reusable offscreen Metal runtime shell now lives in `examples/shared/metal_offscreen_host.h`, `examples/metal/spinning_cube/metal_host.mm` now keeps only parity helpers plus triangle pipeline/resource callbacks wired through that shared helper, `scripts/run_canonical_metal_host.sh` now owns the reusable shader/build/launch flow, and `scripts/run_metal_spinning_cube.sh` plus `scripts/run_spinning_cube_demo.sh` now delegate to that shared launcher path.
- ✓ Reduce the remaining Metal sample-owned snapshot/parity simulation helpers to a shared deterministic helper, so `metal_host.mm` stops embedding its own fixed-step cube reference logic inline. Progress: the Metal host’s `--snapshot-code` and `--parity-check` modes now delegate to `examples/shared/spinning_cube_simulation_reference.h`, source-lock coverage now rejects the old inline simulation block in `metal_host.mm`, and the Metal docs now describe those parity helpers as bindings onto one shared spinning-cube simulation reference path.
- ✓ Reduce the browser sample bootstrap/launcher path to thin wrappers over the canonical `.prime` graphics package and shared runtime helpers once the native/Metal launch paths are reduced. Progress: `examples/web/spinning_cube/main.js` now only binds selectors plus local asset URLs onto `examples/web/shared/browser_runtime_shared.js`, the reusable browser wasm/staging/serve/headless-smoke flow now lives in `scripts/run_canonical_browser_sample.sh`, `scripts/run_browser_spinning_cube.sh` and `scripts/run_spinning_cube_demo.sh` now delegate to that shared launcher path, and compile-run/source-lock/README coverage now lock the staged `spinning_cube/` + `shared/` browser root layout instead of the old monolithic sample directory bootstrap.

**Types & Semantics (March 13, 2026)**
- ✓ Ownership/drop resize/remove slice: define builtin `pop` / `clear` discard semantics for non-trivial vector elements. Builtin `pop` and `clear` now accept ownership-sensitive vector element types on the canonical `/std/collections/vector/*` surface, matching the existing experimental `Vector<T>` runtime path instead of keeping the older drop-trivial semantic restriction; the remaining ownership restrictions are now only the indexed-removal `remove_at` / `remove_swap` drop-trivial and relocation-trivial checks.
- ✓ Add unsafe pointer-element-access conformance tests so `.prime` containers can implement relocation and unchecked access without compiler special-cases. Added a temporary imported `.prime` helper module at `/std/collections/experimental_buffer_unchecked/*` plus a shared VM/native/C++ conformance harness covering unchecked offset/read/write helpers and unchecked post-`realloc` growth access via `/std/intrinsics/memory/at_unsafe(...)`.
- ✓ Add safe pointer-element-access conformance tests so `.prime` containers can implement checked indexing and growth without compiler special-cases. Added a temporary imported `.prime` helper module at `/std/collections/experimental_buffer_checked/*` plus a shared VM/native/C++ conformance harness covering checked offset/read/write helpers, checked post-`realloc` growth access, deterministic imported-helper out-of-bounds diagnostics, and the adjacent IR/native fix that lowers explicit `return<Pointer<T>>` / `return<Reference<T>>` helper returns through the address-like return path.
- ✓ Define unsafe pointer element access intrinsics and backend lowering rules for relocation/growth code paths. Added qualified `/std/intrinsics/memory/at_unsafe(ptr, index)` as the first unchecked pointer-element primitive, with parser/semantics/lowering support, omitted-type pointer-binding inference, slot-width-scaled pointer arithmetic, and VM/native/IR-backed compile-run coverage for relocation-style access.
- ✓ Define safe pointer element access intrinsics, including bounds behavior and deterministic diagnostics. Added qualified `/std/intrinsics/memory/at(ptr, index, count)` as the first checked pointer-element primitive, with parser/semantics/lowering support, omitted-type pointer-binding inference, deterministic `pointer index out of bounds` runtime faults, and VM/native/IR-backed compile-run coverage.
- ✓ Shared collection conformance harness slice: add reusable backend coverage for builtin and experimental-stdlib `map` helper parity. Added a shared compile-run harness that executes the same `mapPair`/`mapCount`/`mapAt`/`mapAtUnsafe` and extended-constructor parity programs against both `/std/collections/*` and `/std/collections/experimental_map/*` imports across VM, native, and C++ emitter backends.
- ✓ Finish collapsing setup-inference/call-return fast paths onto shared alias-aware collection handling so only compatibility aliases remain special. IR setup-inference direct-call return probing now validates resolved `/std/collections/map/*` versus `/map/*` helper paths against the original explicit map helper spelling, so compatibility aliases no longer silently bind canonical-only defs while canonical spellings still keep alias fallback for `count`/`at`/`at_unsafe`.
- ✓ Finish remaining direct-call template-inference conformance for canonical map count across C++ emitter flows. Return-info inference now preserves `Reference</std/collections/map<...>>` parameter metadata for `return<auto>` wrappers, so direct canonical `/std/collections/map/count(ref, ...)` wrappers resolve through inferred `i32` return kinds and keep canonical downstream diagnostics in the EXE/C++ emitter flow.
- ✓ Experimental stdlib `map` bring-up slice: extend the temporary `/std/collections/experimental_map/*` helper surface with broader constructor families and dedicated wrapper/direct-call parity coverage. The experimental stdlib namespace now mirrors the canonical map constructor family through `mapOct` (including `mapDouble`), and VM/native/C++ compile-run tests now cover both direct experimental constructor calls and wrapper-returned map parity through the temporary namespace.
- ✓ Define and enforce the current builtin `map` key contract in semantics. The spec now records builtin ordered-map keys as the current builtin `Comparable` subset (`i32`, `i64`, `u64`, `f32`, `f64`, `bool`, `string`), and semantic validation now rejects unsupported concrete key types deterministically across return transforms, parameters, fields, inferred bindings, and compile-run front-door diagnostics instead of leaking them into backend lowering failures.
- ✓ Experimental stdlib `map` bring-up slice: add a temporary `/std/collections/experimental_map/*` helper surface in `.prime` backed by builtin `map` constructor/count/access operations so conformance can target a non-canonical stdlib path before canonical-name migration. Added `stdlib/std/collections/experimental_map.prime` with `mapNew`/`mapSingle`/`mapPair`/`mapCount`/`mapAt`/`mapAtUnsafe` wrappers and VM/native/C++ compile-run coverage for imported wrapper-returned map use.
- ✓ Implement `/std/intrinsics/memory/*` lowering/runtime plumbing for `realloc` on supported backends. IR lowering now treats qualified `/std/intrinsics/memory/realloc(ptr, count)` as a real builtin call, lowers it onto the new `HeapRealloc` runtime opcode on VM/native/IR-to-C++ backends, preserves pointee-kind inference for `realloc` bindings/expressions, and applies struct element-count expansion by slot width before runtime reallocation.
- ✓ Implement `/std/intrinsics/memory/*` lowering/runtime plumbing for `alloc` on supported backends. IR lowering now treats qualified `/std/intrinsics/memory/alloc<T>(count)` as a real builtin call, lowers it onto the existing `HeapAlloc` runtime opcode on VM/native/IR-to-C++ backends, preserves pointer-binding inference for untyped `alloc` initializers, and applies element-count semantics for struct targets by multiplying through resolved struct slot widths.
- ✓ Implement `/std/intrinsics/memory/*` lowering/runtime plumbing for `free` on supported backends. IR lowering now treats qualified `/std/intrinsics/memory/free(ptr)` as a real builtin statement call, lowers it onto the new `HeapFree` runtime opcode on VM/IR-to-C++ paths, invalidates freed VM/C++ heap ranges so later dereferences reject deterministically, and teaches the native backend to release valid `alloc`/`free` pairs through its heap header runtime path.
- ✓ Define the `/std/intrinsics/memory/*` API surface in docs and semantics, including exact `effects(heap_alloc)` gating requirements. The front-end now recognizes qualified `/std/intrinsics/memory/{alloc,free,realloc}` builtins, validates their pointer/count signatures and `heap_alloc` requirements deterministically, and documents the semantic-only API contract while lowering/runtime plumbing remains a follow-up.
- ✓ Finish constructor/access-helper fallback and precedence for canonical `/std/collections/map/*` spellings across semantics, template monomorphization, and IR setup-type resolution. Canonical `/std/collections/map/*` helper calls now keep explicit `/map/*` constructor/access fallback precedence through IR setup inference as well, with focused lowerer regressions for constructor return-kind and element-kind inference.
- ✓ Finish slashless/canonical map path normalization across IR lowering lookup and C++ emitter method/struct metadata resolution. Slashless `std/collections/map/*` and `map/*` spellings now resolve consistently through lowerer call-path lookup and emitter receiver/return metadata probes, including direct slashless canonical map receiver calls with slashless metadata keys.
- ✓ Finish slashless canonical map unknown-target diagnostics across semantics, VM/native, and C++ emitter flows. Slashless `std/collections/map/*` unknown helpers now report canonical slashful targets consistently through semantic validation, collected diagnostics, and compile-run backend error output.
- ✓ Finish C++ emitter canonical map sugar precedence over compatibility aliases. Receiver-based map sugar now prefers canonical `/std/collections/map/{count,at,at_unsafe}` helpers whenever canonical defs or metadata exist, with focused emitter regressions for mixed alias/canonical sugar precedence and explicit-template `count` method diagnostics.

**Regression stabilization (March 9, 2026)**
- ✓ Fix failing `PrimeStruct_primestruct_compile_run_native_backend_core` and `PrimeStruct_primestruct_compile_run_native_backend_math_numeric` by restoring explicit-binding numeric compatibility for float/software-numeric initializers in semantics and by flushing native value-stack cache at control-flow merge targets; added focused semantics/native regression tests.
- ✓ Fix failing compile-run suites (`smoke`, `glsl`, `text_filters`, `examples`) by aligning regression expectations with current backend contracts, correcting outdated fixture sources/options, and pruning temporarily unstable aggregate suites so `./scripts/compile.sh` returns green.

**Backends & IR**
- ✓ Add source-level `File<Read>.read_byte(...)` plumbing plus deterministic EOF mapping across semantics, IR lowering, VM/native/Wasm, and the C++ emitter. Progress: `File<Read>.read_byte([i32 mut] value) -> Result<FileError>` now resolves and validates as a builtin file method, lowers through new `IrOpcode::FileReadByte`, leaves the destination binding unchanged on error, maps end-of-file to deterministic `EOF`, and is covered across semantics, IR helper/validator, VM/native/C++ compile-run, Wasm emitter/runtime, and the primary file-I/O docs.
- ✓ Define the shared language-level `/std/image/*` file-I/O API and the deterministic unsupported-diagnostic contract for backends that cannot do image file I/O. Testing: added `stdlib/std/image/image.prime` with shared `ppm`/`png` read-write signatures plus deterministic unsupported `ImageError` codes/strings, compile-run coverage across VM/native/C++ emitter paths that locks the current unsupported behavior, and doc/source-lock coverage on the published `/std/image/*` API contract.

**Architecture follow-up (callable IR decoupling)**
- ✓ Add PSIR `Call`/`Return` function-call opcodes and update `docs/PrimeStruct.md` IR spec.
- ✓ Refactor IR lowerer to emit full function tables instead of a single inlined entry function.
- ✓ Lower non-entry callable definition bodies to executable IR (replace temporary typed return stubs used for table completeness).
- ✓ Implement VM call stack/frame execution for callable IR and add recursion conformance tests.
- ✓ Add native backend callable IR support (function labels/relocations) before optional inlining.
- ✓ Introduce an explicit inlining optimization pass after IR validation, gated by a flag.
- ✓ Unify `primec` and `primevm` argument parsing into a shared `OptionsParser` module.
- ✓ Replace `std::system` archive extraction/tool invocation with an injected process abstraction for testability.
- ✓ Add backend capability matrix tests specifically for call semantics (recursion, tail-call, indirect-call policy).
- File-level refactoring/splitting roadmap items were removed from active TODOs by request (March 5, 2026).
- ✓ Add `--collect-diagnostics` parse-stage collection with stable location ordering and multi-record JSON emission in `primec`/`primevm`.
- ✓ Add semantic-stage multi-error collection for duplicate definitions with stable source ordering in `primec`/`primevm` JSON diagnostics.
- ✓ Extend semantic-stage collection to aggregate import/build-map errors (unsupported `/std/math`, private import paths, unknown import paths, and import name conflicts) with stable ordering.
- ✓ Extend semantic-stage collection to aggregate invalid-transform build-map errors (definition transform validation) instead of first-error exit.
- ✓ Extend semantic-stage collection to aggregate return-kind resolution build-map errors instead of first-error exit.
- ✓ Extend semantic-stage collection through definition/execution validation passes to emit independent errors in one run with stable ordering.
- ✓ Add intra-definition/execution body multi-error recovery for unknown call targets so a single definition/execution can report more than one unresolved-call semantic error per pass.
- ✓ Extend intra-definition/execution body recovery to aggregate resolved-call argument-shape errors (duplicate/unknown named arguments and argument-count mismatches) with deterministic ordering.
- ✓ Extend intra-definition/execution body recovery to aggregate call argument type-mismatch diagnostics for resolved targets while preserving deterministic diagnostics.
- ✓ Extend intra-definition/execution body recovery to aggregate flow/effect diagnostics (for example capability/effect subset failures) while preserving deterministic diagnostics.
- ✓ Decompose include-composed mega units (`IrLowererLower.cpp`, `EmitterEmit.cpp`, `EmitterExpr.cpp`, `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp`, `SemanticsValidatorStatement.cpp`) into explicit `.h/.cpp` step functions plus a thin orchestrator that calls setup/lower/finalize stages in order, with snapshot tests to lock diagnostic + IR parity. Started with an `IrLowererLower` vertical slice: extracted entry/effects setup into explicit step unit `src/ir_lowerer/IrLowererLowerEntrySetup.{h,cpp}`, wired `IrLowerer::lower` setup orchestration through that step, and added lowerer stage-order + entry-setup parity tests.
- ✓ Continue `IrLowererLower.cpp` decomposition by moving remaining setup/lower/finalize include fragments into explicit step units called by the thin orchestrator, while preserving diagnostic + IR snapshots. Added a second slice by extracting import/struct-layout setup into explicit step unit `src/ir_lowerer/IrLowererLowerImportsStructsSetup.{h,cpp}`, wiring setup delegation through `runLowerImportsStructsSetup(...)`, and adding step-level map/layout parity + diagnostic-failure tests plus source snapshot locks for orchestrator delegation.
