**Architecture Review Follow-Up (March 6, 2026)**
- ✓ Add an `IrBackend` interface and route all emit modes through `IrModule`. Progress: introduced shared `IrBackend` abstraction + registry for IR-capable emit modes (`vm`, `native`, `ir`, `wasm`, `glsl-ir`, `spirv-ir`, `cpp-ir`, `exe-ir`) and routed `primec` through one lower/validate/inline/emit IR path; added standalone `IrToGlslEmitter` and `IrToCppEmitter` vertical slices for stack-based `i32` arithmetic/comparisons and jump control flow over `IrModule`, and widened `IrToGlslEmitter` parity with `f32` literal/arithmetic/comparison opcode emission (`PushF32`, `Add/Sub/Mul/Div/NegF32`, `Cmp*F32`) plus `PushI64` literal emission for `i32`-range values with deterministic out-of-range diagnostics, narrowed signed/unsigned i64 compare/arithmetic emission (`CmpEq/Ne/Lt/Le/Gt/GeI64`, `CmpLt/Le/Gt/GeU64`, `Add/Sub/Mul/Div/NegI64`, `DivU64`) and `ReturnI64` support for that subset, narrowed integer-to-f32 conversion parity (`ConvertI64ToF32`, `ConvertU64ToF32`, `ConvertF32ToI64`, `ConvertF32ToU64`), `ConvertF32ToI32` and `ConvertI32ToF32` conversion emission, narrowed `ConvertF32ToF64`/`ConvertF64ToF32` passthrough conversion emission, narrowed `ConvertI32ToF64`/`ConvertF64ToI32` conversion emission through deterministic f32 payload lowering, narrowed `ConvertI64ToF64`/`ConvertU64ToF64` conversion emission through deterministic f32 payload lowering, narrowed `ConvertF64ToI64`/`ConvertF64ToU64` conversion emission through deterministic f32 payload lowering, narrowed `PushF64`/`ReturnF64` emission through deterministic f32 payload lowering, narrowed `AddF64`, `SubF64`, `MulF64`, `DivF64`, and `NegF64` arithmetic emission through deterministic f32 payload lowering, narrowed `CmpEqF64`, `CmpNeF64`, `CmpLtF64`, `CmpLeF64`, `CmpGtF64`, and `CmpGeF64` compare emission through deterministic f32 payload lowering, and `ReturnF32` support, including deterministic `ConvertF32ToI32` NaN/overflow clamp emission, plus multi-function `Call`/`CallVoid` dispatch over shared stack state, `PushArgc` emission mapped to deterministic zero (`argc` unavailable in shader entry), deterministic print/argv no-op emission (`PrintString`, `PrintI32`, `PrintI64`, `PrintU64`, `PrintStringDynamic`, `PrintArgv`, `PrintArgvUnsafe`) with `PrintString` string-index validation, `FileOpenRead`/`FileOpenWrite`/`FileOpenAppend` deterministic invalid-handle emission, `FileClose`/`FileFlush`/`FileWriteI32`/`FileWriteI64`/`FileWriteU64`/`FileWriteString`/`FileWriteByte`/`FileWriteNewline` deterministic success-code emission, deterministic `AddressOfLocal` local-slot offset emission, deterministic `LoadIndirect` aligned/bounded local-slot emission with zero fallback for invalid addresses, deterministic `StoreIndirect` aligned/bounded local-slot store with value passthrough fallback for invalid addresses, and `LoadStringByte` string-table byte emission with deterministic out-of-range zero fallback for shader entry semantics, so simple `f32` local, arithmetic, `convert<int>(f32)`, `convert<f32>(i32)`, `return<f32>`, helper-call, and entry-arg-count shaders stay on the IR path; added `--emit=glsl-ir`/`--emit=spirv-ir` and `--emit=cpp-ir`/`--emit=exe-ir` CLI modes that lower through `IrModule` and emit via the IR emitters; default `--emit=glsl` now routes directly through the registered `glsl-ir` backend with no legacy fallback, while `--emit=spirv` now routes directly through the registered `spirv-ir` backend with no legacy fallback; non-emit IR failures (lower/validate/inline/output-write) now surface immediately instead of silently falling back; default `--emit=cpp`/`--emit=exe` now route directly through registered `cpp-ir`/`exe-ir` backends; extended `IrToCppEmitter` callable coverage to handle direct `Call`/`CallVoid` dispatch across multi-function IR modules with dedicated `cpp-ir`/`exe-ir` compile-run tests; routed `glsl-ir`, `spirv-ir`, `cpp-ir`, and `exe-ir` through `IrBackend` registry implementations in `primec` and removed their dedicated `main.cpp` special branches so they now use the shared lower/validate/inline/emit backend path; default `glsl` and `spirv` now both route through their registered `*-ir` backends in the shared backend execution path with no legacy fallback; widened `IrToCppEmitter` float parity by adding `f32` arithmetic/comparison/conversion/return opcode emission plus `f64` comparison/arithmetic/negation/return opcode emission (`PushF64`, `Cmp*F64`, `Add/Sub/Mul/Div/NegF64`, `ReturnF64`), conversion slices (`ConvertI32/I64/U64ToF64`, `ConvertF64ToI32/I64/U64`, `ConvertF32ToF64`, `ConvertF64ToF32`), pointer opcode emission (`AddressOfLocal`, `LoadIndirect`, `StoreIndirect`), full file-I/O open parity (`FileOpenRead`, `FileOpenWrite`, `FileOpenAppend`) plus write/flush/close emission (`FileWrite*`, `FileFlush`, `FileClose`), and `LoadStringByte` string-index emission so default `cpp`/`exe` stays on the IR path (including file-read IR-first compile-run coverage). This item is now complete.
- ✓ Implement an `IrToGlslEmitter` vertical slice for a numeric/control-flow subset.
- ✓ Implement an `IrToCppEmitter` vertical slice for a VM/native parity subset. Progress: added stack-machine `i32` + `i64` arithmetic/comparison/control-flow coverage (`PushI32`/`PushI64`, local load/store, `i32`/`i64`/`u64` compares, jump/jump-if-zero, `ReturnI32`/`ReturnI64`/`ReturnVoid`), plus print/argv surface opcodes (`PrintI32`/`PrintI64`/`PrintU64`/`PrintString`/`PrintStringDynamic`, `PushArgc`, `PrintArgv`, `PrintArgvUnsafe`) with deterministic diagnostics for invalid entry/local/jump/string-index/argv-index and unsupported opcodes; added direct callable opcode coverage (`Call`/`CallVoid`) across multi-function IR modules with deterministic call-target diagnostics; expanded float parity with `f32` arithmetic/comparison/conversion and `ReturnF32` emission plus `f64` comparison/arithmetic/negation and `ReturnF64` support (`PushF64`, `Cmp*F64`, `Add/Sub/Mul/Div/NegF64`, `ReturnF64`) and conversion support (`ConvertI32/I64/U64ToF64`, `ConvertF64ToI32/I64/U64`, `ConvertF32ToF64`, `ConvertF64ToF32`) with helper generation; added pointer opcode emission (`AddressOfLocal`, `LoadIndirect`, `StoreIndirect`) with deterministic runtime alignment/range diagnostics, full file-I/O open parity (`FileOpenRead`, `FileOpenWrite`, `FileOpenAppend`) plus write/flush/close emission (`FileWrite*`, `FileFlush`, `FileClose`) with deterministic error packing/write behavior, and `LoadStringByte` emission with deterministic runtime bounds diagnostics, plus backend + compile-run coverage for these paths; routed `cpp-ir`/`exe-ir` emission through registered `IrBackend` implementations and added backend-registry coverage for deterministic kind order + `cpp-ir` source emission, in addition to compile-run coverage for `i64`, argv-print, dynamic-string print, callable dispatch, string indexing, pointer indirect paths, file-write flows, and file-read IR-first flows. Added malformed-module hardening for parity with GLSL IR emission: `IrToCppEmitter` now rejects empty non-entry function bodies with deterministic index diagnostics, with dedicated emitter and backend regression coverage. Added deterministic clamped `ConvertF32ToI32`/`ConvertF64ToI32` helper emission (NaN -> 0, overflow -> i32 min/max) with emitter/backend and compile-run source-lock coverage. Added deterministic clamped `ConvertF32ToU64`/`ConvertF64ToU64` helper emission (NaN/non-positive -> 0, overflow -> u64 max) with emitter/backend and compile-run source-lock coverage. Added deterministic clamped `ConvertF32ToI64`/`ConvertF64ToI64` helper emission (NaN -> 0, overflow -> i64 min/max) with emitter/backend and compile-run source-lock coverage, plus `exe-ir`/`exe` runtime checks for both edge clamps (NaN, +Inf, -Inf) and in-range truncation semantics on f32/f64 convert-to-i64 paths, and direct `cpp-ir`/`cpp` source-lock checks for `ConvertF32ToI64` and `ConvertF64ToI64` helper wiring, including standalone direct conversion snippets for both helper paths and helper-elision coverage when i64 float conversion opcodes are absent, including backend `cpp-ir` helper-elision coverage when those opcodes are absent and include-presence/elision checks for `<cmath>`/`<limits>` in those backend cases, plus matching backend include-presence/elision checks for the u64 clamp-helper paths. Added `exe-ir`/`exe` runtime checks for in-range f32/f64 convert-to-u64 truncation semantics so the IR-first C++ path now locks unsigned truncation behavior alongside the existing signed coverage, and added direct emitter/backend helper-elision checks that i32 clamp helpers/includes are omitted when convert-to-i32 opcodes are absent. Added direct emitter/backend source-lock coverage for unsigned `Cmp*U64` emission and canonicalized compare-result literals to `1u`/`0u` for deterministic C++ output parity, plus file-I/O helper/include elision coverage when no file opcodes are present and dedicated append-open (`FileOpenAppend`) source-lock coverage in both emitter and `cpp-ir` backend paths, plus dedicated write-open (`FileOpenWrite`) source-lock coverage for deterministic `O_WRONLY | O_CREAT | O_TRUNC` flag emission in both paths, dedicated `FileWriteU64` source-lock coverage for the unsigned-to-string write path in both emitter and backend tests, dedicated signed `FileWriteI32`/`FileWriteI64` source-lock coverage for stack pop/sign-cast/to-string write paths in both emitter and backend tests, dedicated byte/newline `FileWriteByte`/`FileWriteNewline` source-lock coverage in both emitter and backend tests, dedicated `FileWriteString` source-lock coverage for handle unpack + string-table length write paths in both emitter and backend tests, dedicated `FileWriteString` out-of-range string-index diagnostics in both emitter and backend tests, and dedicated out-of-range file-open string-index diagnostics in both emitter and backend tests, plus dedicated out-of-range call-target diagnostics in cpp-ir backend tests, and dedicated out-of-range jump-target diagnostics in cpp-ir backend tests, plus dedicated out-of-range unconditional jump-target diagnostics in cpp-ir backend tests, plus dedicated unsupported-opcode diagnostics in cpp-ir backend tests, and dedicated out-of-range print-string index diagnostics in cpp-ir backend tests, plus dedicated out-of-range string-byte-load index diagnostics in cpp-ir backend tests, and dedicated invalid-entry-index diagnostics in cpp-ir backend tests. Added deterministic runtime stack-underflow guards for `Dup`/`Pop` (with source-lock coverage) to match VM fault behavior. This vertical slice is now complete; follow-up items track additional parity hardening work.
- ✓ Extend `IrToCppEmitter` runtime stack-underflow guards from `Dup`/`Pop` to remaining stack-consuming opcodes (`Add*`/`Sub*`/`Mul*`/`Div*`, print/file helpers, and return paths) for deterministic VM fault parity. Added deterministic guard emission + VM-parity diagnostics for integer/float arithmetic (`add`/`sub`/`mul`/`div` + `float op`), print stack consumers, file helper stack consumers, and non-void return opcodes, with dedicated `cpp-ir` source-lock regression coverage.
- ✓ Add differential tests comparing AST emitters vs IR emitters on a shared corpus. Added compile-run differential corpus coverage that locks `cpp`/`exe` parity against `cpp-ir`/`exe-ir` (source equality + runtime exit/stdout/stderr parity) and `glsl` parity against `glsl-ir` on shared IR-supported snippets.
- ✓ Add `IrValidationTarget::Glsl` and move GLSL legality checks from the AST emitter into IR validation. Added explicit GLSL target validation in `validateIrModule(...)`, routed `glsl-ir`/`spirv-ir` backends through that target, and added validation + compile-run regressions for GLSL i64 literal range and local-slot limits with validation-stage diagnostics.
- ✓ Refactor `SemanticsValidator` into immutable shared analysis state plus per-definition validation contexts. Deduplicated against the shipped validator-context refactor work (explicit definition/execution validation contexts via `ValidationContextScope`) and extended regression coverage with `on_error` cross-definition context-isolation semantics.
- ✓ Replace include-composed `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp`/`SemanticsValidatorStatement.cpp` fragments with explicit compile units. Completed by extracting `SemanticsValidatorStatementReturns.h` into explicit compile unit `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorStatementReturns.cpp`, flattening `SemanticsValidatorStatement.cpp` by removing include-composed `SemanticsValidatorStatementHelpers.h`/`SemanticsValidatorStatementBindings.h`/`SemanticsValidatorStatementControlFlow.h` fragments into one explicit compile unit implementation and deleting those fragment headers, and flattening `SemanticsValidatorExpr.cpp` by removing include-composed `SemanticsValidatorExprPredicates.h`/`SemanticsValidatorExprValidation.h` fragments into one explicit compile unit implementation and deleting those fragment headers; added source-delegation + behavior regression coverage (including `for` condition binding bool/non-bool and lambda capture-all known/unknown cases).
- ✓ Replace `ProcessRunner` string shell commands with argv-based process execution APIs. Updated `ProcessRunner` to an argv-based interface, switched `ExternalTooling` and import-archive extraction to argv invocations (no shell command strings), and added deterministic tooling/import resolver regression coverage for argv argument-shape dispatch.
- ✓ Convert the transform registry from static arrays to a registration API with phase metadata. `TransformRegistry` now exposes `registerTransform(...)` / `contains(...)` / `list(...)`, default transform registration is built through that API, and phase checks (`isTextTransformName`/`isSemanticTransformName`) now resolve directly from registered metadata.
- ✓ Add an ADR documenting backend boundary policy: all codegen consumes IR only. Added `docs/adr/0001-backend-ir-boundary.md` (accepted decision + guardrails) and linked it from `docs/PrimeStruct.md` compilation model notes.

**Architecture follow-up (IR-only backend boundary hardening)**
- ✓ Route production `--emit=cpp` and `--emit=exe` through `IrBackend` (`cpp-ir`/`exe-ir`) in `primec`, removing AST-direct emission from the default production path while preserving current output defaults. Completed by adding shared emit-kind alias resolution (`cpp -> cpp-ir`, `exe -> exe-ir`, plus existing `glsl`/`spirv` aliases) in `primec::resolveIrBackendEmitKind(...)`, routing `main` backend lookup through that helper, removing the AST-direct `cpp`/`exe` emitter branch, and tightening compile-run parity coverage so `cpp`/`exe` and `cpp-ir`/`exe-ir` now assert identical emitted C++ source plus runtime stdout/stderr for the shared corpus while keeping output path defaults and the existing software-numeric guardrail.
- ✓ Extract shared IR preparation/execution orchestration (`lower -> ir-validate -> optional ir-inline -> revalidate -> emit`) into one helper used by both `primec` and `primevm` to remove duplicated backend flow logic. Added shared helper `prepareIrModule(...)` in `primec_lib` (`include/primec/IrPreparation.h`, `src/IrPreparation.cpp`) that centralizes lowering + target validation + optional inlining + revalidation stage handling, wired `primec` backend execution flow and `primevm` runtime path through this helper, and added helper behavior + source-lock regressions to ensure both entrypoints use the shared path without inline duplicated IR prep logic.
- ✓ Expand backend parity coverage to gate the migration: add focused regressions that compare `cpp` vs `cpp-ir` and `exe` vs `exe-ir` for source output shape, runtime behavior, and diagnostics (JSON + text). Completed by locking source + runtime parity on a shared corpus (`tests/unit/test_compile_run_vm_outputs.h`) and adding focused diagnostic parity checks for both semantic-stage and lowering-stage failures across both emit-mode pairs, with equality assertions for text diagnostics and `--emit-diagnostics` JSON output.
- ✓ Add a guardrail test that fails if any production `primec --emit` mode bypasses `IrBackend` or reintroduces AST-direct production emission. Completed by centralizing the production emit-kind allowlist/usage text in `primec::EmitKind` (`listPrimecEmitKinds`, `isPrimecEmitKind`, `primecEmitKindsUsage`), routing `OptionsParser` + `primec` usage output through that shared list, and adding `tests/unit/test_ir_pipeline_backends.h` coverage that asserts every production emit kind resolves through `resolveIrBackendEmitKind(...)` to a registered `IrBackend` while existing source-lock checks continue to reject AST-direct `main.cpp` emission branches.
- ✓ Remove the legacy AST-direct production branch after parity passes are stable, then update `docs/PrimeStruct.md` and ADR follow-up notes to record the unified backend path as complete. Completed by deleting `main.cpp`'s production-only pre-backend software-numeric branch for `cpp`/`exe` (so production aliases now follow the same `IrBackend` lowering/validation path as their `*-ir` forms), adding regression coverage that rejects reintroduction of `isProductionCppOrExeEmitKind(...)`/`scanSoftwareNumericTypes(...)` source branches, extending `cpp`/`exe` vs `cpp-ir`/`exe-ir` diagnostics parity coverage with a software-numeric lowering failure case, and updating `docs/PrimeStruct.md` plus ADR `0001` follow-up status notes to record the unified IR-only backend path as complete.

**Reflection Codegen Roadmap (March 6, 2026)**
- ✓ Add reflection syntax/validation for struct definitions: `[reflect]` plus `[generate(...)]` with deterministic diagnostics for unsupported targets/generators. Implemented parser+semantics coverage for reflection transforms, added struct-only target checks (definitions and execution rejection), and added deterministic diagnostics for `generate(...)` shape/allowlist issues (requires `[reflect]`, duplicate/unsupported generator names).
- ✓ Define baseline reflection API scope for v1: compile-time-only metadata queries with no runtime reflection objects/tables. Documented v1 scope + reserved query names in `docs/PrimeStruct.md`, and added deterministic semantics diagnostics that reserve `meta.*`/`/meta/*` metadata queries as compile-time-only (not yet implemented) while explicitly rejecting runtime reflection object/table paths.
- ✓ Implement core reflection primitives: `meta.type_name<T>`, `meta.type_kind<T>`, `meta.is_struct<T>`, `meta.field_count<T>`, `meta.field_name<T>(i)`, `meta.field_type<T>(i)`, and `meta.field_visibility<T>(i)`. Implemented compile-time evaluation for all listed primitives with deterministic diagnostics for invalid call shape, non-struct targets, non-constant indices, and out-of-range field indices.
- ✓ Complete remaining core reflection field-metadata primitives: `meta.field_name<T>(i)`, `meta.field_type<T>(i)`, and `meta.field_visibility<T>(i)` compile-time evaluation.
- ✓ Implement transform reflection predicate for generators: `meta.has_transform<T>(name)` compile-time evaluation (method/path forms) with deterministic argument-shape diagnostics.
- ✓ Implement trait-conformance reflection predicates used by generated helper gating: `meta.has_trait<T>(TraitName)` and `meta.has_trait<T, Elem>(Indexable)` now evaluate at compile time with deterministic trait-name/template-arity diagnostics.
- ✓ Add deterministic reflection diagnostics for invalid targets/indices (non-reflect types, non-constant indices, out-of-range field index, and unsupported metadata queries). Field metadata queries now reject non-reflect struct targets deterministically, existing index diagnostics remain deterministic, and unknown `meta.*`/`/meta/*` query names now report explicit unsupported-query errors.
- ✓ Guarantee reflection builtins are compile-time eliminated before IR emission; add regression tests that lock zero runtime reflection opcodes/state. IR lowering now rejects non-eliminated reserved `/meta/*` query paths before emission, and IR pipeline coverage locks that lowered reflection-query programs emit no runtime call-based reflection state.
- ✓ Implement v1 generated helpers: `Equal`, `NotEqual`, `Default`, `IsDefault`, `Clone`, and `DebugPrint`.
- ✓ Implement v1 generated helper slice: `Equal` now emits `/Type/Equal([Type] left, [Type] right) -> bool` for `[reflect generate(Equal)]` structs, performs deterministic field-wise equality checks (returns `true` for zero-field structs), and rejects path collisions deterministically when `/Type/Equal` already exists.
- ✓ Implement v1 generated helper slice: `NotEqual` now emits `/Type/NotEqual([Type] left, [Type] right) -> bool` for `[reflect generate(NotEqual)]` structs, performs deterministic field-wise inequality checks (returns `false` for zero-field or static-only structs), and rejects path collisions deterministically when `/Type/NotEqual` already exists.
- ✓ Implement v1 generated helper slice: `Default` now emits `/Type/Default() -> Type` for `[reflect generate(Default)]` structs, returns `Type()` deterministically (including static-only structs), and rejects path collisions deterministically when `/Type/Default` already exists.
- ✓ Implement v1 generated helper slice: `IsDefault` now emits `/Type/IsDefault([Type] value) -> bool` for `[reflect generate(IsDefault)]` structs, compares non-static fields against a synthesized `Type()` instance (returns `true` for zero-field or static-only structs), and rejects path collisions deterministically when `/Type/IsDefault` already exists.
- ✓ Implement v1 generated helper slice: `Clone` now emits `/Type/Clone([Type] value) -> Type` for `[reflect generate(Clone)]` structs, rebuilds instances deterministically from non-static fields (`Type()` for static-only structs), and rejects path collisions deterministically when `/Type/Clone` already exists.
- ✓ Implement v1 generated helper slice: `DebugPrint` now emits `/Type/DebugPrint([Type] value) -> void` for `[reflect generate(DebugPrint)]` structs, prints deterministic line-based struct/field-name output (`/Type {}`, or `/Type {` + one line per non-static field + `}`), and rejects path collisions deterministically when `/Type/DebugPrint` already exists.
- ✓ Define deterministic helper naming + visibility contract for generated helpers (`/Type/Helper` paths, collision/override rules, and import behavior). Generated helpers now use explicit `/Type/Helper` paths with deterministic collision diagnostics (`generated reflection helper already exists: /Type/Helper`), are emitted as `[public]`, and are therefore importable via `import /Type/*` helper-name aliases.
- ✓ Add v1 conformance coverage for reflection codegen (parse, semantics, canonical AST/IR snapshots, and backend compile-run parity). Added parser coverage for full v1 `generate(...)` helper lists, semantics coverage that locks canonical generated-helper emission order/visibility, compile-run dump coverage that locks canonical `ast-semantic` and `ir` helper ordering, and vm/exe/native runtime parity coverage that exercises all v1 generated helpers together.
- ✓ Implement v1.1 generated helpers: `Compare` (lexicographic for sortable fields), `Hash64`, `Clear`, and `CopyFrom`.
- ✓ Implement v1.1 generated helper slice: `Compare` now emits `/Type/Compare([Type] left, [Type] right) -> i32` for `[reflect generate(Compare)]` structs, performs deterministic lexicographic non-static field comparison (`left < right -> -1`, `left > right -> 1`, equality -> `0`), returns `0` for zero-field/static-only structs, and rejects path collisions deterministically when `/Type/Compare` already exists.
- ✓ Implement v1.1 generated helper slice: `Hash64` now emits `/Type/Hash64([Type] value) -> u64` for `[reflect generate(Hash64)]` structs, folds non-static fields in deterministic source order via `convert<u64>(field)` with stable FNV-style multiply/add constants, returns the deterministic seed literal for zero-field/static-only structs, and rejects path collisions deterministically when `/Type/Hash64` already exists.
- ✓ Implement v1.1 generated helper slice: `Clear` now emits `/Type/Clear([Type mut] value) -> void` for `[reflect generate(Clear)]` structs, resets non-static fields in deterministic source order by assigning from a synthesized default instance (`Type()`), emits a no-op helper for zero-field/static-only structs, and rejects path collisions deterministically when `/Type/Clear` already exists.
- ✓ Implement v1.1 generated helper slice: `CopyFrom` now emits `/Type/CopyFrom([Type mut] value, [Type] other) -> void` for `[reflect generate(CopyFrom)]` structs, copies non-static fields in deterministic source order via field-wise assignment from `other` into `value`, emits a no-op helper for zero-field/static-only structs, and rejects path collisions deterministically when `/Type/CopyFrom` already exists.
- ✓ Add deterministic field-eligibility diagnostics for generated helpers (for example unsupported field envelopes in `Compare`/`Hash64`). Reflection helper rewriting now validates field envelopes before emitting generated `Compare`/`Hash64` helpers and reports deterministic helper-scoped diagnostics (for example `generated reflection helper /Type/Compare does not support field envelope: <field> (<type>)`) in source-field order.
- ✓ Implement v2 generated helpers: `Validate` with explicit error contract and generated field-check scaffolding hooks. Reflection helper rewriting now emits `/Type/Validate([Type] value) -> Result<FileError>` plus deterministic `/Type/ValidateField_<field>([Type] value) -> bool` hooks for non-static fields in source order; `Validate` short-circuits on the first failing hook with deterministic `1`-based field error codes and otherwise returns `Result.ok()`, while zero-field/static-only structs emit no field hooks and return `Result.ok()` directly.
- ✓ Implement v2 generated helpers: `Serialize`/`Deserialize` with a stable format version tag and deterministic field order. Reflection helper rewriting now emits `/Type/Serialize([Type] value) -> array<u64>` with deterministic source-order payload slots prefixed by stable format tag `1u64`, plus `/Type/Deserialize([Type mut] value, [array<u64>] payload) -> Result<FileError>` with deterministic size/version guards (`1` = payload-size mismatch, `2` = format-version mismatch) and source-order `convert<fieldType>(at(payload, index))` decoding before `Result.ok()` success.
- ✓ Defer `ToString` generation until dynamic string construction/runtime support is finalized; keep `DebugPrint` as the v1 debug surface. Reflection transform validation now reports a dedicated deterministic defer diagnostic for `generate(ToString)` (`reflection generator ToString is deferred on /Type ... use DebugPrint`) instead of the generic unsupported-generator error, and coverage locks semantics + compile-run diagnostic behavior.

**Pipeline & CLI**
- ✓ Implement semantic transform phase and registry (`--semantic-transforms`, `--no-semantic-transforms`) and the `text(...)` / `semantic(...)` grouping syntax in transform lists.
- ✓ Make per-envelope transform lists drive text/semantic transforms (today text filters are global and ignore `[operators]`, `[collections]`, etc).
- ✓ Add auto-injected transform rules and path filters (`/std/math/*`, recurse flags) described in docs; currently only a global text filter list exists.
- ✓ Support text transforms appending additional text transforms to the same node (self-expansion in the transform pipeline).
- ✓ Align CLI naming and behavior with docs: support `--text-transforms`, `--no-text-transforms`, and true auto-deduction in `--transform-list` (currently `--text-filters` only).
- ✓ Apply `single_type_to_return` via semantic transforms or per-definition markers (it is currently toggled via the text filter list).
- ✓ Document `--emit=ir` (PSIR bytecode output) in the docs or remove it from the CLI.
- ✓ Enforce canonical-only parsing when `--no-transforms` is active (disable parser sugar like `if(...) {}` / `value[index]` and require explicit return transforms); currently still accepts surface forms.
- ✓ Replace `include` with unified `import` in the parser (support `import<...>` for source expansion and `import /path/*` for namespace exposure; parser rejects `include` directives, include resolver + text filter pipeline accept `import<...>`).
- ✓ Rework include resolver into an import resolver (expand `import<...>` before transforms/inference; `import<...>` now expands, including versioned entries).
- ✓ Implement import resolver version-selection rules (1/2-part “latest matching” vs 3-part exact), `_`-prefixed privacy rejection, and duplicate import handling + tests.
- ✓ Update CLI flags/help: deprecate `--include-path` in favor of `--import-path` (or document the alias).
- ✓ Add regression coverage for legacy `include<...>` compatibility alias handling in the resolver/text-filter pipeline and compile-run flows (added resolver, text-filter, and compile-run tests that lock alias behavior).
- ✓ Add CLI regression coverage for legacy `--include-path` alias handling in both `primec` and `primevm` (including `--include-path=<dir>` form) via compile-run import expansion coverage.
- ✓ Add versioned regression coverage for legacy `include<...>` alias handling (`version=...`) in resolver and compile-run flows (added resolver and compile-run tests for version-selector alias behavior).
- ✓ Add version-first regression coverage for legacy `include<...>` alias handling (`include<version=\"...\", ...>`) in resolver and compile-run flows (added resolver and compile-run tests for version-first alias ordering).
- ✓ Add text-filter regression coverage for versioned legacy `include<...>` alias directives (including `version`-first ordering) so directive payload strings are never rewritten (added pipeline passthrough tests for both ordered forms).
- ✓ Add text-filter regression coverage for single-quoted versioned legacy `include<...>` alias directives (including `version`-first ordering) so quote-style aliases stay rewrite-safe (added pipeline passthrough tests for both single-quoted ordered forms).
- ✓ Remove legacy `include<...>` compatibility aliases from resolver/text-filter parsing so only `import<...>` is accepted (resolver now rejects `include<...>` with a migration diagnostic; legacy compatibility tests converted to rejection diagnostics).
- ✓ Remove legacy `--include-path` CLI aliases from `primec` and `primevm` so `--import-path`/`-I` are the only supported import-root flags (removed alias parsing branches from both CLIs and converted alias regression coverage to argument-error diagnostics).
- ✓ Rename import pipeline internals away from `Include*` naming (`IncludeResolver`, `expandIncludes`, `includePaths`) to `Import*` naming and update call sites (migrated to `ImportResolver`, `expandImports`, and `importPaths` across headers/sources/tests, and renamed resolver header/source paths accordingly).
- ✓ Rename import-stage diagnostics/enums away from `Include*` (`CompilePipelineErrorStage::Include`, `DiagnosticCode::IncludeError`) while preserving existing diagnostic code stability guarantees (renamed to import-focused enum values and added diagnostics-code regression coverage that locks `PSC1001` stability).
- ✓ Rename include-focused test files/suites to import-focused names and keep coverage parity for import expansion, versions, privacy, and ordering (renamed resolver and compile-run suite/file names to `import*` across CMake + doctest registrations without dropping coverage cases).
- ✓ Update docs/changelog notes to mark the compatibility break: `include<...>` and `--include-path` removed, `import<...>` and `--import-path` required (added compatibility notes to README/design/spec docs and recorded the break in `docs/changelog.md`).
- ✓ Extract a shared compile pipeline abstraction for `primec` and `primevm` so parse/import/transform/semantics steps run through one codepath.
- ✓ Make import/archive expansion ordering deterministic across platforms (normalize path ordering before expansion and keep duplicate handling stable).
- ✓ Add `--list-transforms` CLI output (transform name, phase, aliases, and availability) for faster transform discovery.
- ✓ Add a post-semantics AST dump stage (`--dump-stage ast-semantic`, alias `ast_semantic`) to inspect canonicalized AST after rewrites/inference.
- ✓ Update `primec`/`primevm` usage text to prefer `--text-transforms` and `--import-path`/`-I` (usage strings now advertise modern flags only; compile-run regression coverage locks the help text for both CLIs).
- ✓ Remove legacy `--text-filters` CLI aliases from `primec` and `primevm` so `--text-transforms` is the only text-transform flag (removed alias parsing branches from both CLIs, migrated alias-based positive tests to `--text-transforms`, and added compile-run diagnostics coverage that locks alias rejection in both `primec` and `primevm`).
- ✓ Add CLI regression coverage for bare legacy `--text-filters` rejection in `primec` and `primevm` (existing alias rejection coverage now locks both bare `--text-filters` and `--text-filters=...` unknown-option diagnostics).
- ✓ Add `--emit-diagnostics` regression coverage for removed `--text-filters` in `primec` and `primevm` (compile-run diagnostics tests now lock `PSC0001` JSON argument payloads for `--text-filters=...` and ensure no plain usage text leaks in diagnostics mode).
- ✓ Add `--emit-diagnostics` regression coverage for bare removed `--text-filters` in `primec` and `primevm` (compile-run diagnostics tests now lock `PSC0001` JSON argument payloads for bare `--text-filters` and ensure no plain usage text leaks in diagnostics mode).
- ✓ Add `--emit-diagnostics` regression coverage for removed `--include-path` in `primec` and `primevm` (compile-run diagnostics tests now lock `PSC0001` JSON argument payloads for both bare `--include-path` and `--include-path=...` and ensure no plain usage text leaks in diagnostics mode).
- ✓ Add `--emit-diagnostics` regression coverage for legacy `include<...>` import rejection in `primec` and `primevm` (compile-run diagnostics tests now lock `PSC1001` JSON payloads, including `stage: import`, and ensure structured diagnostics mode avoids plain `Import error:` text).

**Syntax & Surface Features**
- ✓ Treat semicolons as optional separators everywhere the SyntaxSpec allows them (top-level/bodies, transform/template/param/arg lists, import lists); currently hard errors.

**Backends & IR (March 16, 2026)**
- ✓ Add `/std/image/ppm/*` read implementations and conformance tests for VM/native/Wasm on top of `File<Read>.read_byte(...)`. Progress: `/std/image/ppm/read(...)` now parses ASCII `P3` inputs through shared stdlib byte-reading helpers built on `File<Read>.read_byte(...)`, successful reads materialize `width`/`height` plus flat RGB `vector<i32>` payloads, missing/malformed/unsupported inputs deterministically return `image_invalid_operation`, and compile-run coverage now locks that behavior across VM/native/Wasm plus the shared source-lock/docs expectations.
- ✓ Add `/std/image/ppm/*` write implementations and conformance tests for VM/native/Wasm. Progress: `/std/image/ppm/write(...)` now emits deterministic ASCII `P3` payloads through the shared stdlib `File<Write>` path, validates positive dimensions plus exact RGB byte counts/component ranges before opening the destination, maps file-open/write failures to `image_invalid_operation`, and compile-run coverage now locks successful writes plus invalid-input rejection across VM/native/Wasm alongside the shared docs/source-lock contract checks.
- ✓ Extend `/std/image/ppm/read(...)` beyond ASCII `P3` inputs to binary `P6` and broader format edge cases once the initial parser path is stabilized. Progress: the shared stdlib reader now dispatches between ASCII `P3` and binary `P6`, enforces positive dimensions plus `maxValue == 255`, requires whitespace before `P6` raster bytes, clears partially read outputs on truncated payloads, and compile-run coverage now locks successful `P6` reads plus deterministic failure/reset behavior across VM/native/Wasm alongside the shared docs/source-lock contract checks.
- ✓ Add `/std/image/png/read(...)` container parsing and conformance tests for VM/native/Wasm. Progress: the shared stdlib reader now opens PNG files, validates the PNG signature plus chunk framing, enforces an `IHDR`-first container with positive dimensions and spec-legal `IHDR` fields, requires at least one `IDAT` before `IEND`, maps malformed/truncated/missing files to `image_invalid_operation`, and returns `image_read_unsupported` for structurally valid PNG containers while compressed scanline decode is still pending; compile-run coverage now locks that behavior across VM/native/Wasm alongside the shared docs/source-lock contract checks.
- ✓ Add `/std/image/png/read(...)` IDAT decompression and scanline reconstruction for 8-bit RGB/RGBA PNGs in VM/native/Wasm. Progress: the shared stdlib reader now inflates zlib streams that use stored/no-compression deflate blocks, validates Adler-32 trailers, reconstructs filter-`0` scanlines for non-interlaced 8-bit RGB/RGBA PNGs, and materializes the public flat RGB buffer (dropping alpha from RGBA inputs) across VM/native/Wasm; malformed zlib/scanline payloads return `image_invalid_operation`, while still-valid PNGs that need unsupported Huffman deflate paths or other decode paths deterministically return `image_read_unsupported`.
- ✓ Extend `/std/image/png/read(...)` to filter-`1` (`Sub`) scanline reconstruction for stored/no-compression 8-bit RGB/RGBA PNGs in VM/native/Wasm. Progress: the shared stdlib reader now reconstructs both filter-`0` and filter-`1` (`Sub`) scanlines for non-interlaced 8-bit RGB/RGBA PNGs whose `IDAT` payload uses stored/no-compression deflate blocks, continues materializing the public flat RGB buffer while dropping alpha from RGBA inputs, and compile-run coverage now locks left-byte reconstruction across VM/native/Wasm; malformed scanline payloads still return `image_invalid_operation`, while still-valid PNGs that need unsupported Huffman deflate paths or remaining PNG filters deterministically return `image_read_unsupported`.
- ✓ Extend `/std/image/png/read(...)` to fixed-Huffman literal deflate blocks for 8-bit RGB/RGBA inputs in VM/native/Wasm. Progress: the shared stdlib reader now dispatches between stored and fixed deflate block types, decodes fixed-Huffman literal/end-of-block streams in non-interlaced 8-bit RGB/RGBA PNGs, keeps filter-`0` and filter-`1` (`Sub`) scanline reconstruction working on top of that compressed path, and compile-run coverage now locks successful fixed-Huffman reads plus deterministic rejection of dynamic-Huffman PNGs; malformed compressed payloads still return `image_invalid_operation`, while still-valid PNGs that need dynamic-Huffman tables or broader decode paths deterministically return `image_read_unsupported`.
- ✓ Extend `/std/image/png/read(...)` to fixed-Huffman length/distance backreferences for 8-bit RGB/RGBA inputs. Progress: the shared stdlib reader now decodes fixed-Huffman length/distance backreferences with correct overlapping copy semantics, which extends the compressed PNG path beyond literal-only streams for non-interlaced 8-bit RGB/RGBA inputs; compile-run coverage now locks repeated-pixel PNG reads across VM/native/Wasm using real fixed-Huffman backreference payloads, while malformed fixed-Huffman matches still return `image_invalid_operation` and dynamic-Huffman blocks remain `image_read_unsupported`.
- ✓ Extend `/std/image/png/read(...)` to dynamic-Huffman deflate blocks for 8-bit RGB/RGBA inputs in VM/native/Wasm. Progress: the shared stdlib reader now parses dynamic-Huffman headers/code-length tables, decodes both literal-only and length/distance backreference dynamic deflate blocks through the shared Huffman path, and compile-run coverage now locks successful dynamic-Huffman RGB/RGBA reads across VM/native/Wasm while preserving deterministic invalid-operation behavior for malformed matches.
- ✓ Extend `/std/image/png/read(...)` to filter-`2` (`Up`) scanline reconstruction for 8-bit RGB/RGBA inputs in VM/native/Wasm. Progress: the shared stdlib reader now reconstructs `Up` scanlines against the previous decoded row for non-interlaced 8-bit RGB/RGBA PNGs, while keeping the existing flat RGB output contract and alpha-drop behavior; compile-run coverage now locks successful stored `Up`-filter reads across VM/native/Wasm, and the remaining unsupported-filter coverage now points at `Average` instead of `Up`.
- ✓ Extend `/std/image/png/read(...)` to filter-`3` (`Average`) scanline reconstruction for 8-bit RGB/RGBA inputs. Progress: the shared stdlib reader now reconstructs `Average` scanlines using the standard floor-average predictor over the left and prior-row bytes for non-interlaced 8-bit RGB/RGBA PNGs, while preserving the flat RGB output contract and RGBA alpha-drop behavior; compile-run coverage now locks successful stored `Average`-filter reads across VM/native/Wasm, and the remaining unsupported-filter coverage now points at `Paeth`.
- ✓ Add an experimental `.prime` graphics package (`/std/gfx/experimental/*`) that exposes the first real graphics surface for the locked v1 path (`Window`, `Device`, `Queue`, `Swapchain`, `Frame`, `RenderPass`, `Mesh`, `Pipeline`, `Material`, `ShaderLibrary`, `VertexColored`, `GfxError`, plus the required enum/descriptor/value types) and proves the API shape can be expressed without sample-specific host ABI helpers, with deterministic import coverage. Progress: `stdlib/std/gfx/experimental.prime` now provides the initial importable type/value surface in `.prime`, and shared semantics plus C++/VM/native smoke coverage lock the wildcard import path, core resource/value structs, `VertexColored`, and deterministic error-factory construction.
- ✓ Experimental stdlib `gfx` helper-resolution slice (phase 1): imported type-namespace helpers like `GfxError.why(err)` now resolve directly from imported stdlib definitions instead of failing with synthetic receiver argument-count mismatches. Progress: semantics coverage now locks generic `Type.helper(...)` receiver dropping, and VM smoke covers `/std/gfx/experimental/GfxError.why(...)` through imported stdlib definitions.
- ✓ Experimental stdlib `gfx` helper-resolution slice (phase 2a): imported static value access for `/std/gfx/experimental/*` now works directly from stdlib definitions, so locked enum/static shapes like `ColorFormat.Bgra8Unorm`, `DepthFormat.Depth32F`, `PresentMode.Fifo`, and `ShaderLibrary.CubeBasic` compile and run through vm/exe/native coverage instead of requiring constructor-only fallback patterns.
- ✓ Experimental stdlib `gfx` helper-resolution slice (phase 2b): exe/native parity now holds for string-returning imported type-namespace helpers like `GfxError.why(...)`. Progress: the IR lowerer now canonicalizes `Type.helper(...)` calls with type receivers onto the direct helper path before inline emission, so local and imported string-returning helpers run through exe/vm/native coverage instead of diverging on the native path.
- ✓ Define the minimal backend substrate/intrinsic boundary for graphics on the first supported target, keeping C++ limited to irreducible runtime responsibilities (window creation, device/swapchain/resource handles, command submission, and presentation) instead of owning API-shape semantics. Progress: `/std/gfx/experimental/*` now exposes explicit `.prime` `Substrate*Config` records plus token-returning `GraphicsSubstrate.create*` / `acquireFrame` / `beginRenderPass` / `submitFrame` / `presentFrame` helpers, and the typed `Window` / `Device` / `Queue` / `Swapchain` / `Mesh` / `Pipeline` / `Frame` / `RenderPass` wrappers are assembled in `.prime` on top of that minimal substrate; shared semantics plus exe/vm/native smoke now lock the importable substrate boundary.
- ✓ Experimental stdlib `gfx` semantics slice (phase 1): importable method-form wrappers now cover the locked window/frame lifecycle surface that is already expressible in `.prime` (`Window.is_open`, `Window.poll_events`, `Window.aspect_ratio`, `Device.default_queue`, `Device.create_swapchain`, `Device.create_mesh`, `Swapchain.frame`, `Pipeline.material`, `Frame.render_pass`, `Frame.submit`, `Frame.present`, `RenderPass.draw_mesh`, `RenderPass.end`), and semantics coverage now locks those imports plus deterministic rejects for constructor-like `Window([title] ...)` and source-level `Device([profile] ...)` forms on the current experimental type surface.
- ✓ Experimental stdlib `gfx` semantics slice (phase 2a): imported Result-carrying method wrappers now reject bare explicit non-`Result` struct bindings during semantics instead of deferring those failures to IR lowering; coverage locks the deterministic `binding initializer type mismatch` reject for cases like `[Swapchain] swapchain{device.create_swapchain(...)}` without `?`.
- ✓ Experimental stdlib `gfx` semantics slice (phase 2b1): the remaining currently-unimplemented constructor/type-valued entry points on the experimental path now fail with deterministic contract-aware diagnostics instead of degrading into accidental zero-value struct construction or generic unknown-method/try errors. Progress: bare `Device()` now rejects with an explicit experimental-surface diagnostic, `Device.create_pipeline([vertex_type] type, ...)` now rejects with an explicit unimplemented-entry-point diagnostic, and `Window([title] ...)` remains a deterministic named-argument reject on the current type surface.
- ✓ Treat commas as optional separators everywhere the SyntaxSpec treats them as whitespace (statement lists and binding initializers, not just argument/template lists).
- ✓ Allow trailing separators (comma/semicolon) in lists where the SyntaxSpec permits them; parser rejects trailing commas and semicolons entirely.
- ✓ Allow whitespace-separated import source entries (grammar allows separators to be optional; import lists currently require commas).
- ✓ Remove `include` syntax and unify `import` to cover source expansion (`import<...>`) plus namespace exposure (`import /path/*`; `import<...>` now expands like includes; `include` rejected by parser and resolver).
- ✓ Enforce reserved keywords `if`, `else`, `loop`, `while`, `for` in identifiers and slash paths (parser/import resolver currently allow them).
- ✓ Enforce reserved keyword `auto` in identifiers and slash paths (parser/import resolver currently allow it).
- ✓ Allow omitting empty parameter lists on definitions (`Foo { ... }` → `Foo() { ... }`) at all language levels (executions still require `()`).
- ✓ Implement control-flow sugar for `loop`, `while`, and `for` (only `repeat(count) { ... }` is implemented today).
- ✓ Implement operator sugar for `++` / `--` (`increment` / `decrement` are documented but not rewritten by the text filter).
- ✓ Support comma digit separators in numeric literals (lexer currently splits `1,000i32` into multiple tokens).
- ✓ Support nested definitions inside definition bodies (nested definitions receive their own transforms).
- ✓ Support lambdas inside definition bodies (spec references lambdas; parser only allows bindings/expressions).
- ✓ Allow execution-style transforms inside bodies and argument lists (SyntaxSpec allows `[effects(io_out)] log()` anywhere a form is allowed; parser treats leading `[...]` as a binding and rejects `()`).
- ✓ Support brace constructor forms in value positions (`Type{ ... }` in arguments/returns).
- ✓ Implement definition transform tail sugar (`name(...) [transforms] { ... }`) and normalize it to canonical prefix form (`[transforms] name(...) { ... }`); reject `name[]() { ... }` to avoid indexing-style ambiguity.
- ✓ Allow single-branch surface `if(cond) { ... }` in statement position and desugar to canonical `if(cond, then() { ... }, else() { })`; diagnose single-branch `if` in value/form positions.

**Types & Semantics**
- ✓ Implement software numeric envelopes (`integer`, `decimal`, `complex`) or document their explicit rejection in the syntax spec.
- ✓ Software numeric compatibility follow-up: call-site argument validation now accepts core numeric values (`i32`/`i64`/`u64`/`f32`/`f64`/`bool`) for `integer`/`decimal`/`complex` parameters using deterministic widening rules, while non-numeric arguments still emit deterministic parameter type-mismatch diagnostics.
- ✓ Remove `int`/`float` aliases; require explicit widths (`i32`, `i64`, `u64`, `f32`, `f64`).
- ✓ Add template monomorphization for user-defined definitions (templates are parsed but only builtins/collections use them today).
- ✓ Complete struct lowering (layout metadata, alignment enforcement, `Create`/`Destroy` semantics, and backend emission).
- ✓ Implement `copy`/`public`/`private`/`static` binding semantics and metadata (currently validated but unused).
- ✓ Enforce definition export visibility: definitions are private by default and only `[public]` definitions are importable.
- ✓ Implement `[profile(...)]` transform parsing and validation (per-definition backend profile gating).
- ✓ Add type system conformance tests (positive typing, negative typing, inference resolution, unresolved `auto`).
- ✓ Define/implement struct helper semantics: helpers inside struct bodies with implicit `this`, `[static]` helpers with no `this`, and method-call sugar for non-static helpers.
- ✓ Allow omitted initializer for local bindings of struct types when zero-arg construction is provably effect-free; emit diagnostics otherwise.
- ✓ Define and implement the "no outside effects" proof for zero-arg construction (effects/capabilities mask, write restrictions, transitive checks).
- ✓ Formalize zero-arg constructor rules (field initializers vs `Create()` + execution order) and align effect checks.
- ✓ Prove `Create()` initializes all fields for zero-arg construction when field initializers are missing.
- ✓ Add explicit trait constraints (EoP-style) defined by required named functions (e.g., `plus`, `multiply`, `count`).
- ✓ Define initial built-in traits (`Additive`, `Multiplicative`, `Comparable`, `Indexable`) and wire validation to type checking.
- ✓ Define enum transform desugaring to a struct (value field + static bindings) and document auto-increment/underlying type rules.
- ✓ Define enum value access surface (static field access via type name) and add tests.
- ✓ Implement `match` expressions (syntax, exhaustive type checking, and lowering; surface `match(cond) { ... } else { ... }` sugar parses, but it still aliases `if` for bool conditions with then/else blocks).
- ✓ Define `uninitialized<T>` storage plus `init`/`drop`/`take`/`borrow` helpers (required for `Maybe<T>` and other explicit storage patterns; validates local `uninitialized<T>()` bindings, statement-only `init`/`drop`, `take`/`borrow` expression kinds, inferred init type matching (primitives/arrays/vectors/maps/structs), collection/user-template restrictions, pointer/reference/return-type restrictions, struct-field layout, and definite-init analysis for void definitions and non-void defs that always return; helper calls now accept `this.field` access for uninitialized struct fields, expression-level checks for `if`/`block` value paths are in place; `for` tracking follows runtime order (`init -> condition -> body -> step`) and re-checks condition effects on continued iterations; `loop`/`while`/`repeat` now use multi-iteration fixed-point convergence and `repeat` tracks count-expression helper effects; VM/native lowering supports numeric/bool, struct, string, and numeric/bool array/vector/map storage).
- ✓ Improve `uninitialized<T>` definite-state precision for bounded `loop`/`repeat` counts (literal `0`/`1` and `repeat(true/false)` now run exact iteration-state simulation so known bounded loops avoid conservative `Unknown` diagnostics).
- ✓ Define `Maybe<T>` (optional value) using uninitialized storage + tag; add docs and tests (stdlib shape + `some`/`none` + helper surface added; direct `Maybe(value)` sugar was later removed so present values stay explicit; VM/native backend lowering now supports numeric/bool/struct/string `Maybe<T>`).
Borrow-checker status: core non-lexical lifetime rules, no-escape validation, and unsafe aliasing behavior are implemented; remaining edge-case work is tracked as concrete items below.
- ✓ Implement non-lexical lifetime (last-use) borrow-end analysis for `Reference<T>` so borrows can end before lexical block exit.
- ✓ Enforce `Reference<T>` no-escape rules (allow direct parameter-reference returns only; reject local/derived escapes).
- ✓ Add `[unsafe]` aliasing escape-hatch semantics for references and enforce safe/unsafe boundary escape diagnostics.
- ✓ Align `[unsafe]` pointer-to-reference conversion behavior with docs.
- ✓ Design explicit unsafe pointer-to-reference conversion semantics beyond `location(...)`-based reference construction.
- ✓ Extend pointer-alias borrow-liveness checks across loop iteration boundaries (`for`/`while`/`repeat`/`loop`) with focused regressions.
- ✓ Extend pointer-alias borrow-liveness checks across branch-path merges (`if`/`match`) when alias usage is path-dependent.
- ✓ Extend pointer-alias borrow-liveness checks through lambdas/captures and execution body-argument blocks.
- ✓ Expand alias-chain diagnostic coverage so borrow/escape errors consistently report root and sink context.
- ✓ Investigate the unreachable statement-level `on_error` diagnostic path in `SemanticsValidatorStatementControlFlow` (removed dead statement-level validation branch; reachable diagnostics are covered by execution-transform and block-scope tests).
- ✓ Allow non-primitive `Pointer<T>`/`Reference<T>` targets (or document the primitive-only restriction enforced today).
- ✓ Allow untagged definitions to be used as struct types in bindings (docs say struct tags are optional for instantiation).
- ✓ Treat `if` block envelope names as ignored even if they collide with definitions (branch blocks should not resolve to defs).
- ✓ Align default effects behavior: docs say entry defaults include `io_out`, but code applies no default effects unless `--default-effects` is provided.
- ✓ Implement binding initializer block semantics: `{ ... }` should allow multi-statement blocks and `return(value)` with last-expression value; current parser treats multiple expressions as constructor args (requires explicit type) and forbids `return`.
- ✓ Implement deterministic conversion semantics for `T{value}` (float -> int trunc+clamp, NaN -> 0, +/-Inf -> min/max; integer width sign/zero-extend or truncate) across VM/native/C++ backends.
- ✓ Add conversion edge-case tests (NaN/Inf, out-of-range, narrowing).
- ✓ Add user-defined convert constructors (resolve `convert<T>(u)` to `T.Convert(u)` for non-numeric targets) and tests.
- ✓ Formalize and implement convert-constructor resolution rules (builtin fast-path + signature/ambiguity done; visibility pending definition-level public/private support).
- ✓ Enforce the core type set in semantic validation and backend filters (software numeric rejection now enforced in semantics; still need a full per-backend allowlist for other non-core envelopes).
- ✓ Allow vector binding initializer forms for explicit/inferred mut bindings with user element types: `[vector<T> mut] v{vector<T>()}`, `[mut] v{vector<T>()}`, and omitted explicit shorthand `[vector<T> mut] v{}` (rewritten to zero-arg vector construction).
- ✓ Vector/map migration checklist item: complete vector stdlib-forwarding so constructor/helper surface names (`vector(...)`, `count`, `capacity`, `push`/`pop`/`reserve`/`clear`/`remove_at`/`remove_swap`, `at`/`at_unsafe`) resolve through `/std/collections/*` canonical paths across semantics, IR lowering, VM, native, and C++ emitter flows.
- ✓ Vector/map migration map-forwarding follow-up: IR setup-type method-target resolution now normalizes slashless resolved receiver paths (for example `std/collections/map`) before method lookup, so canonical map helper fallback/diagnostics match slash-prefixed behavior; added focused IR regressions for positive canonical map lookup via resolved receiver path and deterministic unknown-method diagnostics.
- ✓ Vector/map migration map-forwarding follow-up: C++ emitter `resolveMethodCallPath` now normalizes slashless canonical map import-alias receiver paths before struct-receiver metadata lookup, so map helper method-path resolution matches slash-prefixed metadata keys; added focused emitter helper regressions for positive slashless-import resolution and negative no-metadata behavior.
- ✓ Vector/map migration map-forwarding follow-up: C++ emitter method-path receiver-type inference now probes canonical map helper return metadata via import-alias call targets before primitive fallback, so method calls on alias-shaped map helper receivers retain canonical struct-return resolution; added focused emitter helper regression coverage.
- ✓ Vector/map migration map-forwarding follow-up: C++ emitter method-path resolution now canonicalizes slashless map struct-metadata hits to slash-prefixed method targets before emission (`/std/collections/map/...`), with focused emitter helper regression coverage for slashless metadata-key inputs.
- ✓ Vector/map migration map-forwarding follow-up: template monomorph direct-call validation now rejects explicit-template canonical map helper calls that fall back to non-templated `/map/*` compatibility definitions, so `/std/collections/map/map<T, U>(...)` keeps deterministic `template arguments are only supported on templated definitions: /map/map` diagnostics instead of silently dropping template-argument validation; added focused semantics + VM/native compile-run regressions.
- ✓ Vector/map migration map-forwarding follow-up: template monomorph implicit-template helper-path fallback now keeps explicit `/map/*` helper precedence for non-templated compatibility definitions when canonical `/std/collections/map/*` templated helpers are also present, so call-form `/map/count(...)` keeps deterministic compatibility argument-shape diagnostics (`argument count mismatch for /map/count`) instead of silently falling through to canonical templated helper acceptance; added focused semantics + VM/native compile-run precedence/mismatch regressions.
- ✓ Vector/map migration map-forwarding follow-up: template monomorph explicit-template helper-path fallback now also keeps explicit `/map/*` helper precedence when a compatibility helper definition is non-templated, so call/method forms like `/map/count<i32, i32>(...)` and `values.count<i32, i32>(...)` keep deterministic compatibility diagnostics (`template arguments are only supported on templated definitions: /map/count`) instead of silently falling through to canonical `/std/collections/map/*` templated helper acceptance; added focused semantics + VM/native compile-run precedence/mismatch regressions.

- ✓ Collection contract slice: statement-form vector mutator helper classification now resolves call-form receiver method targets before builtin fallback, so user-shadowed call-form mutator statements (`push([values] v, [value] x)` and peers) no longer get misclassified as builtin named-argument rejections; VM/native compile-run coverage now locks named-argument call-form shadow parity.
