- ✓ Variadic arg-pack IR canonical free-builtin wrapped File-handle slice: canonical free-builtin `at([values] values, [index] i).write*()` / `.flush()` plus `at([values] values, [index] i).read_byte(...)` `?` on `[args<Reference<File<Mode>>>]` and `[args<Pointer<File<Mode>>>]` packs now preserves wrapped borrowed/pointer file-handle receiver metadata across direct calls plus pure/mixed spread forwarding.
- ✓ Variadic arg-pack IR status-only Result.error/why slice: indexed `Result.error(...)` / `Result.why(...)` on `[args<Result<Error>>]`, `[args<Reference<Result<Error>>>]`, and `[args<Pointer<Result<Error>>>]` packs now preserve status-only error metadata across direct calls plus pure/mixed spread forwarding, and IR/VM/native coverage exercises direct plus dereferenced borrowed/pointer access on those packs.
- ✓ Variadic arg-pack IR wrapped FileError named-access slice: canonical free-builtin `at([values] values, [index] i).why()` on `[args<Reference<FileError>>]` and `[args<Pointer<FileError>>]` packs now preserves wrapped borrowed/pointer FileError receiver metadata across direct calls plus pure/mixed spread forwarding, and semantics/IR/VM/native coverage exercises that named receiver form while keeping noncanonical labels on the builtin named-argument rejection path.
- ✓ Variadic arg-pack legacy-backend parity slice: concrete value-pack parity and read-only body API coverage are now locked on the C++ emitter path. C++ compile-run coverage includes concrete value-pack `Result<i32, ParseError>` and status-only `Result<ParseError>` flows across direct invocation, pure spread forwarding, and mixed explicit-prefix + spread forwarding, plus direct/pure-spread/mixed compile-run coverage for canonical `map<i32, i32>`, `Buffer<i32>`, `FileError`, and `File<Write>` packs exercising indexed count/why/file-helper access on the legacy backend so stdlib `.prime` variadic helpers no longer depend on constructor ladders or backend-only special cases.
- ✓ Variadic arg-pack legacy-backend borrowed/location slice: borrowed and pointer arg-pack materialization is now locked on the C++ emitter path for the active indexed receiver patterns. C++ emitter variadic pack-location binding resolution tracks `dereference(...)` receiver element types, so `location(dereference(values.at(...)).<reference_field>)` and equivalent indexed-dereference receiver shapes materialize deterministic pointer pack elements instead of falling back to wrapper-address forms, and compile-run coverage locks direct/pure-spread/mixed `Reference<T>` / `Pointer<T>` packs across `FileError`, `File<Write>`, maps, scalar/struct holders, and uninitialized wrappers, including explicit `location(...)` forwarding plus indexed `dereference(...).why()`, file-method, and dereferenced-field receiver access on the legacy backend.
- ✓ Variadic arg-pack legacy-backend reject/diagnostic slice: deterministic rejects for unsupported element kinds and unsupported forwarding/body-API shapes are now locked on the C++ emitter path. Inline-parameter lowering rejects `args<Pointer<string>>` / `args<Reference<string>>` with the explicit `variadic args<T> does not support string pointers or references` diagnostic instead of falling through generic variadic type-mismatch handling, directly rejects non-`location(...)` value forwarding into `args<Reference<T>>` / `args<Pointer<T>>` with dedicated wrapped-pack diagnostics, and helper-level IR validation plus C++ compile coverage assert both reject contracts directly.
- ✓ Add compile-pipeline graph conformance coverage for the current query-shaped canonical helper/method boundary on local `auto` vector receivers. `test_ir_pipeline_type_resolution_parity.cpp` now locks both direct-call and slash-method `/vector/count` query-local-`auto` cases to the present `if branches must return compatible types` compile-pipeline boundary, so the remaining live Group 11 work is narrowed to backend dispatch after that query-shaped resolution path is fixed.
- ✓ Narrow the remaining Group 11 graph-conformance queue after the query-boundary slice landed. The stale umbrella item that mixed backend dispatch with the now-locked compile-pipeline query-shaped helper boundary is archived here, leaving only the genuinely open backend-dispatch follow-up live in `docs/todo.md`.
- ✓ Add backend conformance coverage for the current query-shaped local-`auto` vector helper boundary. The C++/native/VM compile-run suites now lock both direct-call and slash-method `/vector/count` query-local-`auto` cases to the present `if branches must return compatible types` lowering boundary, so the remaining live Group 11 backend item is narrowed to positive dispatch coverage after that query-shaped resolution path is fixed.
- ✓ Document the planned graph performance guardrails and regression-budget contract before implementation work. `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now defines the required single-threaded graph perf baselines, invalidation measurements, sustained micro-plus-end-to-end coverage, and the rule that CT-eval/template/parallel expansions must either fit the current budget or update the documented budget and coverage together.
- ✓ Document the planned graph/CT-eval boundary before implementation work. `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now defines the rule that compile-time evaluation must either consume graph-backed query/binding/result facts directly or go through one explicit tested adapter, plus the coverage and migration constraints that keep CT-eval from growing a second hidden inference/cache model.
- ✓ Document the planned graph invalidation contract before implementation work. `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now defines the edit families, propagation scope, deterministic revisit rules, and coverage expectations for both intra-definition and cross-definition graph invalidation, so the live queue can focus on implementing those invalidation paths rather than discovering the contract piecemeal.
- ✓ Document the planned optional parallel-solve contract before implementation work. `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now defines the entry criteria, dependency-safe work partitions, deterministic merge rules, and parity requirements for parallel graph solving, so the live queue can focus on implementation after the single-threaded path and prerequisites are stable.
- ✓ Document the planned non-template inference migration contract before implementation work. `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now defines the intended thin-slice migration order, completion criteria, and parity expectations for moving remaining non-template inference islands onto graph-backed query/binding state.
- ✓ Document the planned template-inference migration contract before implementation work. `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now defines the staging, completion criteria, and parity expectations for moving explicit and implicit template inference onto the graph-backed path without reintroducing a deferred side-system.
- ✓ Document the planned omitted-envelope and local-`auto` graph-expansion contract before implementation work. `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now defines the staged widening rules, completion criteria, and parity expectations for broadening omitted-envelope and local-`auto` inference beyond the current graph pilot surfaces.
- ✓ Document the planned diagnostic merge-order contract before implementation work. `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now defines how stable semantic-product node identities or sort keys should anchor deterministic diagnostic ordering and future parallel-validation merges, leaving only the implementation work live.
- ✓ Implement stable deterministic diagnostic sort keys for the current lowering-facing semantic diagnostics. `SemanticsValidatorDiagnostics.cpp` now owns shared definition-order and type-resolution-node-order helpers, and both duplicate-definition reporting and infer-graph return-cycle diagnostics use those shared sort keys instead of hand-rolled per-site source ordering, so later semantic-product publication can build on one deterministic merge surface.
- ✓ Document the planned per-definition validation-context split before implementation work. `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now defines the context/caching boundary between definition-local validation state and validator-global registries, leaving only the implementation work live.
- ✓ Document the planned structured diagnostic-sink contract before implementation work. `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now defines the structured diagnostic fields, first-error policy, and migration boundary for replacing `SemanticsValidator`'s shared mutable `error_` flow.
- ✓ Document the planned first semantic-product builder slice before implementation work. `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now defines the narrow first-stage builder scope: resolved call targets, binding/result types, effect/capability summaries, and struct/layout metadata, while leaving graph-backed local/query/`try(...)`/`on_error` metadata for the second builder slice.
- ✓ Document the planned second semantic-product builder slice before implementation work. `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now defines the second-stage builder scope: publishing graph-backed local `auto`, query, `try(...)`, and `on_error` metadata onto the semantic product so testing-only snapshot plumbing can later be retired.
- ✓ Document the planned lowerer entry-setup handoff before implementation work. `docs/PrimeStruct.md` now defines how `IrLowerer` entry setup should consume resolved call targets and helper-routing decisions from the semantic product instead of re-deriving them from AST state.
- ✓ Variadic arg-pack IR value-pack parity slice: concrete value-pack parity is now locked across IR/VM/native for the active arg-pack shapes. IR conversion/validation coverage plus VM/native compile-run coverage exercise direct calls, pure spread forwarding, and mixed explicit-prefix + spread forwarding for concrete value-pack `Result<i32, ParseError>` and status-only `Result<ParseError>` flows, and the current IR-backed suite also covers `map`, `FileError`, and `File<Write>` value-pack lowering while VM/native compile-run coverage locks those same forwarding shapes for `map`, `Buffer<T>`, `FileError`, and `File<Write>` so the remaining runtime-materialization queue can focus on borrowed/pointer and receiver-specific gaps instead of concrete value packs.
- ✓ Variadic arg-pack IR borrowed/pointer parity slice: borrowed and pointer arg-pack parity is now locked across IR/VM/native for the active indexed forwarding shapes. IR struct-type inference preserves indexed args-pack struct identity through `dereference(...)`, base-kind `try(...)` inference resolves indexed `dereference(at(values, ...))` `Reference<Result<T, E>>` / `Pointer<Result<T, E>>` packs symmetrically, and the current IR conversion/validation plus VM/native compile-run coverage locks explicit `location(...)` forwarding and indexed borrowed/pointer flows across `Result`, `File<Mode>`, canonical experimental-map receivers, and `Buffer<T>` pack shapes so those paths no longer regress into `struct parameter type mismatch` or asymmetric pointer-only handling.
- ✓ Variadic arg-pack IR container/resource receiver slice: indexed collection/resource receiver parity is now locked for the active stdlib-owned variadic helper shapes. Method-receiver type resolution recognizes indexed `Buffer<T>` args-pack receivers, count-access classification accepts borrowed/pointer `dereference(at(values, ...))` buffer targets, and array/vector access-target inference treats direct and indexed `Buffer<T>` pack elements as count-capable collection receivers, so VM/native lowering no longer falls back to the generic `count requires array, vector, map, or string target` path when variadic buffer helpers call `.empty()`, `.is_valid()`, or `.count()` on indexed pack elements. Wrapped `Reference<File<Mode>>` / `Pointer<File<Mode>>` canonical `at([values] ..., [index] ...)` receivers also lower through the file-handle result/emission path directly, and the current IR conversion/validation plus VM/native compile-run coverage locks those indexed buffer/file/map receiver flows so the remaining arg-pack queue can stay focused on broader borrowed/pointer parity instead of container/resource receiver routing.
- ✓ Variadic arg-pack IR reject/diagnostic slice: deterministic unsupported-kind and unsupported-forwarding diagnostics are now locked in IR lowering. IR lowering emits the explicit `variadic args<T> does not support string pointers or references` reject for `args<Pointer<string>>` / `args<Reference<string>>` declarations instead of the previous generic backend-level pointer/reference string diagnostic, rejects non-`location(...)` forwarding into `args<Reference<T>>` / `args<Pointer<T>>` with dedicated wrapped-pack diagnostics, and semantics/IR validation plus VM/native/C++ compile coverage assert those rejects directly so successful program exits cannot mask missing diagnostics.
- ✓ Share direct collection-dispatch setup failures through the structured diagnostic sink. `SemanticsValidatorExprCollectionDispatchSetup.cpp` now publishes its direct std-namespaced vector count/capacity unknown-target failures and direct shadowed `at(...)` receiver unknown-method failure through the shared structured-diagnostic helper instead of only setting `error_`.
- ✓ Share direct collection count/capacity fallback failures through the structured diagnostic sink. `SemanticsValidatorExprCollectionCountCapacity.cpp` now publishes its owned direct vector/map count-capacity unknown-target, wrong-target, and unknown-method fallback failures through the shared structured-diagnostic helper instead of only setting `error_`.
- ✓ Share direct pre-dispatch collection helper/access fallback failures through the structured diagnostic sink. `SemanticsValidatorExprPreDispatchDirectCalls.cpp` now publishes its owned borrowed experimental-map helper unknown-target failures, bare map unknown-target fallback failures, canonical map key-mismatch fallback failures, and experimental-map access unknown-target fallback failures through the shared structured-diagnostic helper instead of only setting `error_`.
- ✓ Share direct post-access precheck failures through the structured diagnostic sink. `SemanticsValidatorExprPostAccessPrechecks.cpp` now publishes its owned static-helper method-syntax reject, GPU-builtin shape/definition rejects, statement-only path-space builtin reject, and stdlib buffer wrapper compute-definition rejects through the shared structured-diagnostic helper instead of only setting `error_`.
- ✓ Share direct field-access shape/receiver failures through the structured diagnostic sink. `SemanticsValidatorExprFieldResolution.cpp` now publishes its owned top-level field-access receiver, template-argument, named-argument, block-argument, non-struct-receiver, and unknown-field failures through the shared structured-diagnostic helper instead of only setting `error_`.
- ✓ Share direct collection-access shape/receiver/key/index failures through the structured diagnostic sink so owned map/vector/string access validation publishes immediately instead of only assigning `error_`.
- ✓ Share direct collection-access target-resolution failures through the structured diagnostic sink so owned `unknown method` / `unknown call target` access rejects publish immediately instead of only assigning `error_`.
- ✓ Share direct collection count/capacity builtin compatibility failures through the structured diagnostic sink so owned count/capacity/map-count shape and target rejects publish immediately instead of only assigning `error_`.
- ✓ Tighten the direct expression sink slices so `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprCollectionDispatchSetup.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprLateFallbackBuiltins.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprLateUnknownTargetFallbacks.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprMethodCompatibilitySetup.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprStructConstructors.cpp`, and `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprResolvedCallArguments.cpp` funnel their owned direct failures through one shared local `fail...Diagnostic(...)` helper per file instead of repeating raw `error_` writes across those validators.
- ✓ Tighten the direct statement-side sink slices so `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorStatementReturns.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorStatementControlFlow.cpp`, and `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorStatementBodyArguments.cpp` funnel their owned direct failures through one shared local `fail...Diagnostic(...)` helper per file instead of repeating raw `error_` writes across those validators.
- ✓ Tighten the direct collection access/map/pre-dispatch sink slices so `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprCollectionAccessValidation.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprLateMapAccessBuiltins.cpp`, and `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprPreDispatchDirectCalls.cpp` funnel their owned direct failures through one shared local `fail...Diagnostic(...)` helper per file instead of repeating raw `error_` writes across those validators.
- ✓ Tighten the adjacent collection-expression sink slices so `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprCollectionPredicates.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprDirectCollectionFallbacks.cpp`, and `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprLateCallCompatibility.cpp` funnel their owned direct failures through one shared local `fail...Diagnostic(...)` helper per file instead of repeating raw `error_` writes across those validators.
- ✓ Tighten the direct control-flow/body-argument/map-SoA sink slices so `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprControlFlow.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprBodyArguments.cpp`, and `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprMapSoaBuiltins.cpp` each funnel their owned direct failures through one shared local `fail...Diagnostic(...)` helper instead of repeating raw `error_` writes across each file.
- ✓ Tighten the remaining nested-expression lambda/block and map-key mismatch sink slices so `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprLambda.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprBlock.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprCollectionAccessValidation.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprLateMapAccessBuiltins.cpp`, and `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprPreDispatchDirectCalls.cpp` funnel their owned direct failures through shared local `fail...Diagnostic(...)` helpers instead of open-coding lambda/block/value and map-key mismatch `error_` writes.
- ✓ Tighten the remaining build/helper validation sink slices so `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorBuildLifecycle.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorBuildStructFields.cpp`, and `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorBuildParameters.cpp` funnel their owned direct lifecycle, struct-field envelope, SoA field-envelope, parameter, and helper-definition failures through shared local `fail...Diagnostic(...)` helpers instead of repeating raw `error_` writes across those validators.
- ✓ Tighten the pass/build validator sink slices so `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorPasses.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorPassesEffects.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorPassesExecutions.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorPassesStructLayouts.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorBuildImports.cpp`, and `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorBuildReturnKinds.cpp` funnel their owned direct entry, effects/executions, struct-layout, import-alias, and return-kind failures through shared local fail helpers instead of repeating raw `error_` writes across those validators.
- ✓ Tighten the remaining vector/container/reference sink slices so `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprVectorHelpers.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorStatementVectorResolution.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorStatementContainerHelpers.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprReferenceEscapes.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprMethodResolution.cpp`, and `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprLateCallCompatibility.cpp` funnel their owned direct vector helper, vector statement, container ownership, reference escape, string-count mismatch, and late vector-capacity failures through shared local fail helpers instead of repeating raw `error_` writes across those validators.
- ✓ Tighten the remaining mutation/helper sink slices so `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprMutationBorrows.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorResultHelpers.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprResolvedCallArguments.cpp`, and `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprMethodTargetResolution.cpp` funnel their owned direct mutation/borrow, `on_error` transform parsing, unsafe reference escape, and FileError helper-target failures through shared local fail helpers instead of repeating raw `error_` writes across those validators.
- ✓ Tighten the remaining infrastructure structured diagnostic-sink slices so `src/semantics/SemanticsValidator.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorDiagnostics.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorBuildImports.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorBuildReturnKinds.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorBuildTransforms.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorPassesDefinitions.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorPassesExecutions.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorInferGraph.cpp`, `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorStatement.cpp`, and `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorStatementReturns.cpp` now reuse shared uncontextualized or collected-record diagnostic helpers for stage failures, duplicate-definition/finalized-record synchronization, build/import/return-kind/transform aggregation, definition/execution pass aggregation, infer-cycle publication, and the remaining binding/auto-return fallback rewrites instead of leaving those paths on scattered raw `error_` writes.
- ✓ Tighten the shared helper-owned structured diagnostic sink slices so `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorDiagnostics.cpp` plus the adjacent build/helper/access/literal/numeric/result/method validators reuse shared `failExprDiagnostic(...)` / `failDefinitionDiagnostic(...)` helpers instead of open-coding helper-local context capture and raw `error_` writes across those files.
- ✓ Tighten the remaining helper/runtime structured diagnostic sink slices so build transform batching, infer resolved/direct count-capacity helpers, pass-level effects/layout helpers, collection dispatch, resolved-call arguments, vector statement helpers, vector helper expression routing, and container helper ownership failures all route through shared core fail helpers instead of helper-local raw `error_` writes.
- ✓ Tighten the remaining helper/runtime structured diagnostic sink slices so inferred return/return-statement fallbacks, omitted-initializer and execution-pass helpers, method/post-access field wrappers, build-definition-map definition conflicts, and the remaining build-parameter/struct-field wrapper failures all route through shared core fail helpers instead of helper-local raw `error_` writes.
- ✓ Tighten the remaining pass-wrapper and transform-parser structured diagnostic sink slices so pass-level effects/layout parse failures plus uncontextualized transform/on_error parsing now route through shared core fail helpers instead of helper-local raw `error_` writes.
- ✓ Finish the structured diagnostic-sink migration for `SemanticsValidator` shared mutable `error_` producers. Direct nested-expression, deeper statement, validator-body, helper/runtime, and infer-graph fallback producers now publish through shared local or core fail helpers instead of assigning `error_` inline; the remaining `error_` writes are limited to the shared diagnostics core helper bodies, preserved-error scope restoration, and existing diagnostic string comparisons.
- ✓ Share direct late map-access builtin failures through the structured diagnostic sink. `SemanticsValidatorExprLateMapAccessBuiltins.cpp` now publishes owned `contains`, `tryAt`, and direct map-access unknown-target, wrong-target, and key-compatibility failures through the shared structured-diagnostic helper instead of only setting `error_`.
- ✓ Share direct method-resolution failures through the structured diagnostic sink. `SemanticsValidatorExprMethodResolution.cpp` now publishes owned method-receiver, vector helper arity/index, file-write arity, same-path helper, and direct unknown-method failures through the shared structured-diagnostic helper instead of only setting `error_`.
- ✓ Tighten the method-resolution structured diagnostic-sink slice so `src/semantics/SemanticsValidatorExprMethodResolution.cpp` funnels its owned direct receiver/arity/index/unknown-method failures through one shared `failMethodResolutionDiagnostic(...)` helper instead of repeating raw `error_` writes across the file.
- ✓ Share direct argument-type and spread-argument validation failures through the structured diagnostic sink. `SemanticsValidatorExprArgumentValidation.cpp` now publishes owned argument-type, collection-shape mismatch, struct mismatch, numeric-kind mismatch, canonical map-key mismatch, and spread-argument compatibility failures through shared argument-validation publication helpers instead of only setting `error_`.
- ✓ Share direct early pointer-helper call-shape failures through the structured diagnostic sink. `SemanticsValidatorExprCallResolution.cpp` now publishes owned `location(...)` / `dereference(...)` named-argument, template-argument, block-argument, arity, invalid-local-binding, and non-pointer-like receiver failures through a shared call-resolution publication helper instead of only setting `error_`.
- ✓ Implement the migration adapter for resolved direct-call, receiver-call, and helper-routing target facts. Lowerer entry/setup call resolution now snapshots semantic-product direct-call targets, receiver/method-call targets, and helper-routing choices into a temporary adapter and prefers those semantic-product facts before AST-side compatibility fallback.
- ✓ Implement the `IrLowerer` entry-setup cutover for semantic-product call target facts. Lowerer entry setup now consumes semantic-product direct-call targets, receiver/method-call targets, and helper-routing choices through the temporary adapter instead of re-deriving those target facts solely from AST state.
- ✓ Publish semantic-product struct field ordering, names, and resolved layout envelopes/types. The semantic product now carries ordered per-field metadata for struct-like definitions, including field names, source order, and the resolved explicit-or-inferred binding envelope/type text that lowerer layout planning will consume next instead of rebuilding `LayoutFieldBinding` vectors from raw AST statements.
- ✓ Consume semantic-product struct field ordering/name/envelope/type metadata in lowerer layout setup. `runLowerImportsStructsSetup(...)` now builds `structFieldInfoByName` from published semantic-product field metadata when available instead of reconstructing ordered `LayoutFieldBinding` vectors from raw AST binding statements.
- ✓ Add pipeline-facing tests that pin inspection-surface order and consistency across `ast-semantic`, semantic-product dump, and `ir`. Completed via the new public `primec/testing/CompilePipelineDumpHelpers.h` helper plus compile-pipeline coverage that captures all three surfaces from one source and proves the semantic-product dump is the lowering-facing boundary between syntax-shaped AST output and backend-facing IR.
- ✓ Move lowering-facing dump assertions onto semantic-product dump helpers. Completed: lowering-facing semantic-product dump assertions now use `primec/testing/CompilePipelineDumpHelpers.h`, while the remaining CLI dump tests are limited to alias/parity entrypoint behavior instead of asserting lowering-facing semantic content through shell dumps.
- ✓ Add end-to-end semantic-product boundary conformance for the C++ lowering path. Completed: backend coverage now drives compile pipeline → semantic product → `prepareIrModule(...)` → `cpp-ir` emission through the shared semantic-product-aware testing helpers in one boundary test.
- ✓ Add end-to-end semantic-product boundary conformance for the VM lowering path. Completed: backend coverage now drives compile pipeline → semantic product → `prepareIrModule(...)` → VM execution through the shared semantic-product-aware testing helpers in one boundary test and pins direct-call/method-call semantic facts on that path.
- ✓ Add end-to-end semantic-product boundary conformance for the native lowering path. Completed: backend coverage now drives compile pipeline → semantic product → `prepareIrModule(...)` → native emission through the shared semantic-product-aware testing helpers in one boundary test and pins direct-call/method-call semantic facts on that path.
- ✓ Add end-to-end compile-pipeline conformance cases that exercise the semantic-product boundary through C++/VM/native lowering paths. Completed: the public pipeline conformance helper now covers C++/VM/native lowering end to end via compile pipeline → semantic product → `prepareIrModule(...)` → backend emission/execution tests.
- ✓ Move lowering-facing compile-pipeline assertions onto semantic-product pipeline conformance helpers. Completed: lowering-facing compile-pipeline assertions now use semantic-product-aware compile-pipeline helpers instead of ad hoc test-local `runCompilePipeline(...)` + `prepareIrModule(...)` wiring.
- ✓ Move lowering-facing backend-facing assertions onto semantic-product-aware backend conformance helpers. Completed: backend registry conformance now uses the public `runCompilePipelineBackendConformanceForTesting(...)` helper for C++/VM/native backend emission/execution, while semantic-product direct-call/method-call fact checks route through that shared backend conformance surface instead of open-coded per-backend wiring.
- ✓ Move lowering-facing assertions onto semantic-product dump helpers or pipeline-facing conformance helpers. Completed: lowering-facing dump, compile-pipeline, and backend-facing assertions now all route through `primec/testing/CompilePipelineDumpHelpers.h` helper surfaces instead of direct CLI dumps or ad hoc per-test backend wiring.
- ✓ Add pipeline-facing tests that pin lowering consumption of semantic-product facts on the C++ backend. Completed: backend coverage now runs compile pipeline → semantic product → `prepareIrModule(...)` → `cpp-ir` emission through the public pipeline conformance helper and pins direct-call/method-call semantic facts on that path.
- ✓ Add pipeline-facing tests that pin lowering consumption of semantic-product facts on the VM backend. Completed: backend coverage now runs compile pipeline → semantic product → `prepareIrModule(...)` → VM execution through the public pipeline conformance helper and pins direct-call/method-call semantic facts on that path.
- ✓ Add pipeline-facing tests that pin lowering consumption of semantic-product facts on the native backend. Completed: backend coverage now runs compile pipeline → semantic product → `prepareIrModule(...)` → native emission through the public pipeline conformance helper and pins direct-call/method-call semantic facts on that path.
- ✓ Implement pipeline-facing tests that exercise the semantic-product inspection surface and its relation to existing AST/IR dumps once that stage exists. Completed: inspection-order coverage plus C++/VM/native backend-consumer conformance now pin the semantic-product inspection surface as the lowering-facing boundary between `ast-semantic` and backend-facing IR/backends.
- ✓ Delete redundant lowering-facing dump helper entrypoints. Completed: the unused generic `captureCompilePipelineDumpForTesting(...)` entrypoint is gone from `primec/testing/CompilePipelineDumpHelpers.h`, leaving only the boundary-dump, prepared-IR, and backend-conformance helper surfaces that still serve lowering-facing tests.
- ✓ Delete dump-facing compatibility helpers that only exposed lowering facts before the semantic-product dump existed. Completed: the old generic compile-pipeline dump capture helper is removed, and public dump-facing helper surface is now limited to the semantic-product-aware boundary dump path instead of a pre-semantic-product compatibility wrapper.
- ✓ Migrate the entry-arg IR unit-test family off direct raw-`Program` `IrLowerer::lower(...)` calls. Completed: `tests/unit/test_ir_pipeline_entry_args.cpp` now lowers through shared `parseValidateAndLower(...)` helper wiring in `tests/unit/test_ir_pipeline_helpers.h`, so that family uses semantic-product-aware lowering instead of the raw compatibility overload.
- ✓ Migrate the pointer, GPU, and struct-layout serialization IR unit-test families off direct raw-`Program` `IrLowerer::lower(...)` calls. Completed: `tests/unit/test_ir_pipeline_pointers.cpp`, `tests/unit/test_ir_pipeline_gpu.cpp`, and `tests/unit/test_ir_pipeline_serialization_struct_layouts.h` now lower through shared `parseValidateAndLower(...)` helper wiring in `tests/unit/test_ir_pipeline_helpers.h`, so those families use semantic-product-aware lowering instead of the raw compatibility overload.
- ✓ Migrate the pointer-numeric, variadic basics/results, method-call/argv, struct serialization, and control-flow serialization IR unit-test families off direct raw-`Program` `IrLowerer::lower(...)` calls. Completed: `tests/unit/test_ir_pipeline_pointers_numeric.h`, `tests/unit/test_ir_pipeline_conversions_variadic_basics.cpp`, `tests/unit/test_ir_pipeline_conversions_variadic_results.cpp`, `tests/unit/test_ir_pipeline_conversions_method_calls_and_argv.cpp`, `tests/unit/test_ir_pipeline_serialization_structs.h`, and `tests/unit/test_ir_pipeline_serialization_control_flow_core.h` now lower through shared `parseValidateAndLower(...)` helper wiring in `tests/unit/test_ir_pipeline_helpers.h`, so those families use semantic-product-aware lowering instead of the raw compatibility overload.
- ✓ Migrate the conversions-heavy remainder plus the remaining serialization-calls, serialization-control-flow-metadata, and validation IR unit-test families off direct raw-`Program` `IrLowerer::lower(...)` calls. Completed: the remaining conversions-heavy families in `tests/unit/test_ir_pipeline_conversions_core.h`, `tests/unit/test_ir_pipeline_conversions_numbers.cpp`, and the variadic collection/pointer/file-handle shards, plus `tests/unit/test_ir_pipeline_serialization_calls.h`, `tests/unit/test_ir_pipeline_serialization_control_flow_metadata.h`, and `tests/unit/test_ir_pipeline_validation_chunks/test_ir_pipeline_validation_01.h`, now lower through semantic-product-aware helper entrypoints instead of the raw compatibility overload.
- ✓ Keep one explicit semantic-product-vs-raw fallback parity case until raw-overload retirement is ready. Completed: `tests/unit/test_semantics_type_resolution_graph_snapshots.cpp` remains the one intentional parity case with both semantic-product-aware and raw `IrLowerer::lower(...)` entrypoints, and the source-lock coverage in `tests/unit/test_ir_pipeline_backends_graph_contexts.h` now pins that boundary explicitly.
- ✓ Narrow `primec/testing/SemanticsValidationHelpers.h` to syntax-owned and provenance-owned assertions only. Completed: graph-backed SCC, condensation-DAG, and type-resolution snapshot APIs moved into `primec/testing/SemanticsGraphHelpers.h`, leaving `primec/testing/SemanticsValidationHelpers.h` as a syntax-owned canonicalization/assertion surface instead of a mixed lowering-facing snapshot API.
- ✓ Delete redundant testing-only semantic snapshot capture plumbing. Completed: the type-resolution parity helper no longer captures a bespoke stringified diagnostic snapshot and instead carries raw `CompilePipelineDiagnosticInfo`, leaving only snapshot serialization and fixture/loader cleanup in the remaining transport bucket.
- ✓ Delete redundant testing-only semantic snapshot serialization/plumbing. Completed: the type-resolution parity helper no longer serializes prepared IR into a snapshot-only byte buffer, leaving only the fixture/loader seam in the remaining transport bucket.
- ✓ Delete redundant testing-only semantic snapshot fixture/loader plumbing. Completed: the dedicated `tests/unit/test_ir_pipeline_type_resolution_helpers.h` wrapper is gone and the remaining parity fixture lives directly in `tests/unit/test_ir_pipeline_type_resolution_parity.cpp`, so no separate snapshot-only fixture/loader layer remains.
- ✓ Migrate tests and public testing helpers from `primec/testing/SemanticsValidationHelpers.h` onto semantic-product inspection surfaces. Completed: lowering-facing dump, compile-pipeline, and backend-facing assertions now route through semantic-product-aware helper surfaces, graph/type-resolution testing snapshots live in `primec/testing/SemanticsGraphHelpers.h`, `primec/testing/SemanticsValidationHelpers.h` is reduced to syntax-owned canonicalization/assertion helpers, the raw-`Program` lowering compatibility overload and its fallback parity case are gone, prepared-IR helper transport is internalized, redundant public backend helper entrypoints are gone, and the testing-only semantic snapshot transport layer is fully deleted.
- ✓ Delete lowerer-side direct-call stdlib/helper alias fallback paths once semantic-product direct-call targets exist. Completed: the semantic-product-aware direct-call resolver no longer falls back to lowerer-local import alias recovery and now drops straight to non-alias scoped/root lookup when no published direct-call target is present, leaving receiver-call and collection-bridge alias fallback as the remaining cleanup seams.
- ✓ Delete lowerer-side receiver/method-call stdlib/helper alias fallback paths once semantic-product receiver-call targets exist. Completed: the semantic-product-aware method-call resolver no longer re-enters lowerer-local import alias recovery for receiver/type lookup after a semantic-product receiver-call miss, while the explicit raw helper overload keeps its alias behavior for dedicated non-semantic-product tests.
- ✓ Delete lowerer-side collection/builtin bridge alias fallback paths once semantic-product helper-routing facts exist. Completed: the semantic-product-aware call-path resolver already ignores lowerer-local import alias recovery for bridge routing and consumes published bridge path choices, published direct-call targets, or non-alias scoped/root lookup only, so the final collection/builtin bridge alias-fallback queue item was stale.
- ✓ Delete lowerer-side stdlib/helper alias fallback paths now that their semantic-product removal contract is documented, once equivalent canonical resolution is provided by the semantic product. Completed: the direct-call, receiver/method-call, and collection/builtin bridge fallback seams are all retired, and production lowering now consumes semantic-product call/helper routing without lowerer-local alias recovery.
- ✓ Split the top Group 8 borrowed-view queue so standalone borrowed-value materialization is tracked explicitly ahead of later invalidation rules. Completed: the live queue now names standalone `ref(...)` value materialization as the first prerequisite before the already-tracked richer standalone borrowed field-view and invalidation surfaces, and the stale `No live TODO items currently tracked.` line is gone.
- ✓ Split the standalone borrowed field-view helper TODO into carrier-introduction, helper-path-routing, and later preservation slices. Completed: the live queue now starts with introducing the reusable standalone borrowed field-view carrier on `/soa_vector/field_view/<field>`, then routing that shared helper path onto it, before the later binding/pass/return preservation slice.
- ✓ Split the standalone borrowed field-view carrier TODO again at the real reflected-storage seam. Completed: the live queue now names the reusable reflected per-field borrowed-view carrier over `SoaColumn<T>` storage as the next prerequisite before routing the shared synthetic `/soa_vector/field_view/<field>` helper path onto that carrier and preserving it across later binding/pass/return surfaces.
- ✓ Correct the standalone borrowed field-view carrier TODO to the real storage boundary. Completed: the live queue now names a reusable non-owning strided field-view carrier over whole-element `SoaColumn<T>` storage as the next prerequisite, since `SoaColumn<T>` still stores contiguous whole `T` elements and cannot honestly project one named field as plain `SoaColumn<Field>` storage.
- ✓ Split the standalone borrowed field-view carrier TODO again at the real field-slot addressing seam. Completed: the live queue now starts with introducing a reusable reflected field-slot pointer/offset helper over whole-element `SoaColumn<T>` storage before the later strided field-view carrier, shared `/soa_vector/field_view/<field>` helper routing, and binding/pass/return preservation slices.
- ✓ Split the standalone borrowed field-view carrier TODO again at the real reflection-offset seam. Completed: the live queue now starts with adding reflected field offset/stride helpers for reflect-enabled structs before the later field-slot pointer helper, strided field-view carrier, shared `/soa_vector/field_view/<field>` helper routing, and binding/pass/return preservation slices.
- ✓ Split the standalone borrowed field-view carrier TODO again at the validated layout-facts seam. Completed: the live queue now names reflected field byte offsets and reflected whole-element stride as separate prerequisites before the later field-slot pointer helper, strided field-view carrier, shared `/soa_vector/field_view/<field>` helper routing, and binding/pass/return preservation slices.
- ✓ Split the standalone borrowed field-view carrier TODO again at the reflected layout-facts publication seam. Completed: the live queue now names publishing validated reflected field byte offsets and whole-element stride as reusable layout facts before the later generated `SoaSchemaFieldOffset(...)` / `SoaSchemaElementStride()` wrappers, field-slot pointer helper, strided field-view carrier, shared `/soa_vector/field_view/<field>` helper routing, and binding/pass/return preservation slices.
- ✓ Add a reusable non-owning `SoaColumn<T>` borrowed-view helper as the standalone borrowed field-view carrier substrate. Completed: `soaColumnBorrowedView<T>(...)` now returns a non-owning `SoaColumn<T>` view over existing storage, with direct semantics and C++/native/VM coverage proving shared count/read/write behavior without taking ownership of the underlying data.
- ✓ Publish validated reflected field byte offsets and whole-element stride for reflect-enabled structs as reusable layout facts. Completed: the reflection metadata context now computes validated non-static field byte offsets plus whole-element stride bytes for struct layouts and reuses those facts during `generate(SoaSchema)` helper emission.
- ✓ Emit generated `SoaSchemaFieldOffset(...)` and `SoaSchemaElementStride()` helpers on top of the shared reflected layout facts. Completed: `generate(SoaSchema)` now emits deterministic indexed field-offset helpers plus whole-element stride helpers alongside the existing name/type/visibility/chunk/storage helper family, with direct semantics and C++/native/VM runtime coverage.
- ✓ Split the standalone borrowed field-view carrier TODO again at the byte-addressable pointer primitive seam. Completed: the live queue now names a raw pointer reinterpret primitive plus typed reinterpretation, then byte-addressable pointer offsetting, as the next prerequisites before the later reflected field-slot pointer helper, strided field-view carrier, shared `/soa_vector/field_view/<field>` helper routing, and binding/pass/return preservation slices.
- ✓ Expose a low-level pointer reinterpret primitive that can cast whole-element storage pointers to raw byte-addressable pointers without changing the address. Completed: `/std/intrinsics/memory/reinterpret<T>(...)` now provides an address-preserving pointer cast and the experimental buffer helpers expose `bufferReinterpret<T, U>(...)` wrappers for standard use.
- ✓ Move binding/result/effect/capability consumption to semantic-product-only ownership. Completed: callable effect/capability metadata, binding/storage classification, return/result metadata, and the remaining temporary-call classification seams in statement-binding/default-parameter lowering now all consume published semantic-product facts instead of rebuilding those decisions from AST transforms after the lowering boundary.

    - `VmDebugSession` stores argv text in owning storage, and any exposed or internal views point only into that owned storage for the lifetime of the session.

    - Ownership comments and member naming in `include/primec/Vm.h` match the actual lifetime contract after the change.
  - stop_rule: Stop once debug-session argv handling no longer depends on caller-owned string storage; broader VM CLI argument refactors belong in a separate leaf.
  - notes: Start in `include/primec/Vm.h`, `src/VmDebugSessionSetup.cpp`, `src/VmIoHelpers.cpp`, and the debug entrypoints in `src/primevm_main.cpp` and `src/VmDebugAdapter.cpp`.
  - finished_at: 2026-04-19
  - evidence: Replaced the debug-session argv `string_view` copy with deep-owned string storage plus stable internal view rebuilding in `VmDebugSession`, renamed the header members to match that ownership contract, and added focused session and adapter regressions that mutate caller-side argv buffers after launch while `PrintArgv` and `PrintArgvUnsafe` still emit the original owned text.

  - notes: Start with `src/ir_lowerer/IrLowererLowerReturnAndCalls.h`, `src/ir_lowerer/IrLowererLowerReturnInfo.h`, `src/ir_lowerer/IrLowererLowerInlineCalls.h`, and `src/ir_lowerer/IrLowererLowerEmitExpr.h`; keep the existing step-unit boundaries intact and avoid widening into operator or statement-lane rewrites.
  - finished_at: 2026-04-19
  - evidence: Added `IrLowererLowerReturnEmitStage.{h,cpp}` so the remaining return-info/inline-call/expression-emission lane now builds through one ordinary compiled orchestration unit, deleted the old `IrLowererLowerReturnAndCalls.h` wrapper, rewired `IrLowererLower.cpp` to consume the new stage state instead of the include-owner seam, extended the lowerer testing umbrella with `IrLowererLowerReturnEmitStage.h`, and refreshed source-lock coverage to pin the new stage source and helper-header contract.

- [x] TODO-4004: Fail closed on missing semantic-product facts
  - owner: ai
  - created_at: 2026-04-19
  - phase: Semantic Ownership Cutover
  - depends_on: none
  - scope: Make production lowering fail closed when required semantic-product facts are missing instead of consulting validator-owned or AST-rederived semantic metadata.
  - acceptance:
    - Focused lowerer and `prepareIrModule` coverage proves the covered direct-call, method-call, local-auto, query, `try(...)`, and `on_error` families fail when published semantic-product facts are missing.
    - Production lowerer helpers read published semantic-product data only for the covered families.
    - Remaining compatibility fallbacks are deleted or explicitly limited to non-production fixture paths.
  - stop_rule: Stop once semantic-product presence fully disables production semantic fallback behavior for the covered lowering families.
  - notes: Primary verification seams are `prepareIrModule(...)`, `IrLowerer::lower(...)`, and the backend-registry / graph-context conformance tests that already pin semantic-product entry contracts.
  - finished_at: 2026-04-19
  - evidence: Added semantic-id-only semantic-product fact lookup helpers in `src/ir_lowerer/IrLowererSemanticProductTargetAdapters.{h,cpp}`, rewired production lowering to use those stricter helpers for local-auto, query, `try(...)`, and `on_error` handling, stopped semantic-product-backed call resolution from reconstructing direct/method targets from scope when published routing is absent, added focused regressions that reject definition-path/resolved-path/operand-path compatibility fallback in production mode, added a `prepareIrModule(...)` regression for local-auto fallback rejection, and refreshed graph-context source-lock coverage so production lowerer code stays pinned to the stricter semantic-id path.

- [x] TODO-4005: Split semantic publication into scoped builders
  - owner: ai
  - created_at: 2026-04-19
  - phase: Semantic Product Publication
  - depends_on: none
  - scope: Reorganize semantic-product publication around definition/module-scoped builders so fact publication, lookup indexing, and transient-cache release happen in smaller ownership-bounded stages.
  - acceptance:
    - Publication code is split into dedicated builder units by fact family or module scope instead of one broad `buildSemanticProgram(...)` sweep.
    - Module artifact population and fact ordering remain deterministic while avoiding repeated whole-program traversals where equivalent scoped data already exists.
    - Release validation and semantic-product dump coverage pass unchanged except for intentional contract updates.
  - stop_rule: Stop once semantic-product publication is partitioned into smaller builders with clear ownership and no new broad whole-program collector sweeps are introduced.
  - notes: This starts after TODO-4002 lands and should focus on `src/semantics/SemanticsValidate.cpp`; keep routing/callable-summary publication and graph-backed inference publication as the first candidate split if the builder breakup does not fit one commit.
  - finished_at: 2026-04-19
  - evidence: Added `src/semantics/SemanticPublicationBuilders.{h,cpp}` so semantic-product shell setup, routing publication, metadata publication, scoped fact-family publication, and module finalization now build through named helpers instead of one `buildSemanticProgram(...)` sweep, rewired `SemanticsValidate.cpp` to hand the unified publication surface into that builder module, updated `CMakeLists.txt` to compile the new unit, and refreshed graph-context source-lock coverage so the builder module owns module indexing, routing lookup publication, and fact-family staging.
